<p>LSP/AST/<code>session_search</code> adds <strong>semantic-based</strong> code intelligence (moving definitions, finding references, syntax tree search/replacement) over vanilla text-based <code>Grep</code>/<code>Glob</code>.</p>
<h3>8.1.1 Code Intelligence: What LSP + AST Add</h3>
<p>Vanilla Claude Code only performs <strong>text matching</strong> searches with <code>Grep</code> (ripgrep) / <code>Glob</code> ([[00_cc_overview]] §3.1). OMC's <code>t</code> MCP server puts <strong>semantic-based</strong> tools on top of it:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>LSP tools</strong> (<code>src/tools/lsp-tools.ts</code>, <code>lsp/</code>): <code>lsp_goto_definition</code> (jump to definition), <code>lsp_find_references</code> (dereference transfer), <code>lsp_hover</code> (type/signature), <code>lsp_diagnostics</code>/<code>lsp_diagnostics_directory</code> (type/compilation error — <code>verifier</code> used as "fresh proof"), <code>lsp_rename</code>/<code>lsp_prepare_rename</code> (symbol-safe rename), <code>lsp_document_symbols</code>/<code>lsp_workspace_symbols</code>, <code>lsp_code_actions</code>. Directly register the language server <code>gopls</code> (Go)·<code>clangd</code> (C/C++).</li>
<li><strong>AST Tools</strong> (<code>ast-tools.ts</code>): Search syntax tree patterns with <code>ast_grep_search</code> (e.g. "all async functions without try/catch" from the architect prompt), structural replacement with <code>ast_grep_replace</code>.</li>
<li><strong><code>python_repl</code></strong>(<code>python-repl/</code>): Data analysis execution backend for the scientist agent.</li>
</ul>
<p>The <code>&#x3C;Tool_Usage></code> in the architect·debugger·verifier prompt instructs you to call these tools explicitly — they accurately handle type errors and symbol relationships that are difficult to catch with textual grep.</p>
<h3>8.2 <code>.omc/</code> disk layout</h3>
<p>Persistent paths defined by <code>&#x3C;worktree_paths></code> in <code>CLAUDE.md</code> (<code>src/tools/state-tools.ts</code> verifies paths within worktree boundaries):</p>
<pre><code>.omc/state/                      모드 상태(autopilot/ralph/ultrawork/... -state.json)
.omc/state/sessions/{sessionId}/ 세션별 격리 상태(skill-active-state.json 등)
.omc/notepad.md                  세션 간 작업 노트(priority/working/manual 영역)
.omc/project-memory.json         프로젝트 메모리(노트/디렉티브)
.omc/plans/                      planner 산출 계획
.omc/research/                   리서치 산출
.omc/logs/                       로그
.omc/ultragoal/ledger.jsonl      ultragoal 내구성 원장
.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}  팀 워커 격리 worktree
</code></pre>
<p>State writing ensures concurrency safety with <code>atomicWriteJsonSync</code> (atomic write) and <code>withFileLockSync</code> (file lock) (<code>src/tools/state-tools.ts</code>, <code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code>).</p>
<h3>8.3 Permanence across turns, sessions, and compression</h3>
<p>Three layers work together to maintain “memory”:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Between turns</strong>: Stop Hook (<code>persistent-mode.mjs</code>) reads <code>.omc/state/*-state.json</code> and continues the loop (§7.1).</li>
<li><strong>Intersession</strong>: <code>SessionStart</code> Hook (<code>session-start.mjs</code>) restores the active state, and <code>project-memory-session.mjs</code>/<code>wiki-session-start.mjs</code> reinjects project memory/wiki into a new session prompt.</li>
<li><strong>Between compression</strong>: <code>PreCompact</code> Hook checkpoints memory/wiki before compression to prevent information loss due to context cutting.</li>
</ol>
<p>The <code>&#x3C;remember></code> (7 days)/<code>&#x3C;remember priority></code> (permanent) tag and the <code>remember</code> skill build user knowledge in this layer.</p>

<h2>8. 상태·메모리 영속 (<code>t</code> MCP 서버 + <code>.omc/</code>)</h2>
<h2>8. 상태·메모리 영속 (<code>t</code> MCP 서버 + <code>.omc/</code>)</h2>
<h3>8.1 <code>t</code> MCP 서버 도구</h3>
<p><code>bridge/mcp-server.cjs</code>(소스 <code>src/tools/*.ts</code>)가 노출하는 도구군. 바닐라 Claude Code 도구([[00_cc_overview]] §3) 위에 얹히는 OMC 전용 표면:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>도구군</th>
<th>도구</th>
<th>소스</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>모드 상태</td>
<td><code>state_read/write/clear/list_active/get_status</code></td>
<td><code>state-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>노트패드</td>
<td><code>notepad_read/write_priority/write_working/write_manual/prune/stats</code></td>
<td><code>notepad-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>프로젝트 메모리</td>
<td><code>project_memory_read/write/add_note/add_directive</code></td>
<td><code>memory-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>공유 메모리</td>
<td><code>shared_memory_read/write/list/delete/cleanup</code></td>
<td><code>shared-memory-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>세션 이력</td>
<td><code>session_search</code>, <code>resume-session</code></td>
<td><code>session-history-tools.ts</code>, <code>resume-session.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>LSP</td>
<td><code>lsp_hover/goto_definition/find_references/diagnostics/rename/document_symbols/workspace_symbols/code_actions/...</code> + 언어서버(<code>gopls</code>,<code>clangd</code>)</td>
<td><code>lsp-tools.ts</code>, <code>lsp/</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>AST</td>
<td><code>ast_grep_search</code>, <code>ast_grep_replace</code></td>
<td><code>ast-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>실행</td>
<td><code>python_repl</code></td>
<td><code>python-repl/</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>wiki</td>
<td><code>wiki_add/read/query/list/delete/ingest/lint</code></td>
<td><code>wiki-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>스킬</td>
<td><code>list_omc_skills</code>, <code>load_omc_skills_global/local</code></td>
<td><code>skills-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>trace</td>
<td><code>trace_summary</code>, <code>trace_timeline</code></td>
<td><code>trace-tools.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>deepinit</td>
<td><code>deepinit_manifest</code></td>
<td><code>deepinit-manifest.ts</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>LSP/AST/<code>session_search</code>는 바닐라의 텍스트 기반 <code>Grep</code>/<code>Glob</code>보다 <strong>의미 기반</strong> 코드 인텔리전스를 추가한다(정의 이동, 참조 찾기, 구문트리 검색·치환).</p>
<h3>8.1.1 코드 인텔리전스: LSP + AST가 더하는 것</h3>
<p>바닐라 Claude Code는 <code>Grep</code>(ripgrep) / <code>Glob</code>로 <strong>텍스트 매칭</strong> 검색만 한다([[00_cc_overview]] §3.1). OMC의 <code>t</code> MCP 서버는 그 위에 <strong>의미 기반</strong> 도구를 얹는다:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>LSP 도구</strong>(<code>src/tools/lsp-tools.ts</code>, <code>lsp/</code>): <code>lsp_goto_definition</code>(정의로 점프), <code>lsp_find_references</code>(역참조 전수), <code>lsp_hover</code>(타입/시그니처), <code>lsp_diagnostics</code>/<code>lsp_diagnostics_directory</code>(타입·컴파일 오류 — <code>verifier</code>가 "신선한 증거"로 사용), <code>lsp_rename</code>/<code>lsp_prepare_rename</code>(심볼 안전 리네임), <code>lsp_document_symbols</code>/<code>lsp_workspace_symbols</code>, <code>lsp_code_actions</code>. 언어서버 <code>gopls</code>(Go)·<code>clangd</code>(C/C++)를 직접 등록.</li>
<li><strong>AST 도구</strong>(<code>ast-tools.ts</code>): <code>ast_grep_search</code>로 구문트리 패턴 검색(예: architect 프롬프트의 "try/catch 없는 모든 async 함수"), <code>ast_grep_replace</code>로 구조적 치환.</li>
<li><strong><code>python_repl</code></strong>(<code>python-repl/</code>): scientist 에이전트의 데이터 분석 실행 백엔드.</li>
</ul>
<p>architect·debugger·verifier 프롬프트의 <code>&#x3C;Tool_Usage></code>가 이 도구들을 명시적으로 호출하도록 지시한다 — 텍스트 grep으로는 잡기 어려운 타입 오류·심볼 관계를 정확히 다룬다.</p>
<h3>8.2 <code>.omc/</code> 디스크 레이아웃</h3>
<p><code>CLAUDE.md</code>의 <code>&#x3C;worktree_paths></code>가 정의하는 영속 경로(<code>src/tools/state-tools.ts</code>가 worktree 경계 안으로 경로를 검증):</p>
<pre><code>.omc/state/                      모드 상태(autopilot/ralph/ultrawork/... -state.json)
.omc/state/sessions/{sessionId}/ 세션별 격리 상태(skill-active-state.json 등)
.omc/notepad.md                  세션 간 작업 노트(priority/working/manual 영역)
.omc/project-memory.json         프로젝트 메모리(노트/디렉티브)
.omc/plans/                      planner 산출 계획
.omc/research/                   리서치 산출
.omc/logs/                       로그
.omc/ultragoal/ledger.jsonl      ultragoal 내구성 원장
.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}  팀 워커 격리 worktree
</code></pre>
<p>상태 쓰기는 <code>atomicWriteJsonSync</code>(원자적 쓰기)와 <code>withFileLockSync</code>(파일 락)로 동시성 안전을 확보한다(<code>src/tools/state-tools.ts</code>, <code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code>).</p>
<h3>8.3 턴·세션·압축을 가로지르는 영속</h3>
<p>세 계층이 협력해 "기억"을 유지한다:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>턴 간</strong>: Stop Hook(<code>persistent-mode.mjs</code>)이 <code>.omc/state/*-state.json</code>을 읽어 루프를 이어감(§7.1).</li>
<li><strong>세션 간</strong>: <code>SessionStart</code> Hook(<code>session-start.mjs</code>)이 active 상태를 복원하고, <code>project-memory-session.mjs</code>/<code>wiki-session-start.mjs</code>가 프로젝트 메모리·wiki를 새 세션 프롬프트에 재주입.</li>
<li><strong>압축 간</strong>: <code>PreCompact</code> Hook이 압축 전 메모리/wiki를 체크포인트해 컨텍스트 절삭으로 인한 정보 손실을 막음.</li>
</ol>
<p><code>&#x3C;remember></code>(7일)/<code>&#x3C;remember priority></code>(영구) 태그와 <code>remember</code> 스킬이 이 계층에 사용자 지식을 쌓는다.</p>
<hr>
1c:["$","$L30","07-overview-08",{"id":"07-overview-08","order":14,"tier":"developer","title":"Overview §8","description":"000_overview/00_omc_overview.md","lang":"en","badge":"DEV","children":["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$3f"}}]}]
40:Te7f,<h2>Overview</h2>
<p><strong>Overview §9</strong> — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>src/hooks/mode-registry/</code>가 실행 모드를 일급으로 등록한다. <code>state-tools.ts</code>의 정본 모드:</li>
<li>EXECUTION_MODES = autopilot, autoresearch, team, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, deep-interview, self-improve</li>
<li>
<ul>
<li>상태 전용 = ralplan, omc-teams, skill-active, ultragoal</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>getActiveModes</code>/<code>isModeActive</code>/<code>getAllModeStatuses</code>로 활성 모드를 질의하고, <code>clearModeState</code>로 정리한다. <code>cancel</code> 스킬(<code>/oh-my-claudecode:cancel</code>)이 활성 모드를 종료하며, 영속 루프의 reason 문자열은 항상 "완료·검증 후 <code>/cancel</code>로 깨끗이 종료"를 안내해 무한 루프를 끊는 출구를 보장한다. `scripts/test-mutual</li>
</ul>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h2>9. Mutual exclusion with mod registry</h2>
<h2>9. Mutual exclusion with mod registry</h2>
<p><code>src/hooks/mode-registry/</code> registers the execution mode as first-class. The canonical mode for <code>state-tools.ts</code>:</p>
<pre><code>EXECUTION_MODES = autopilot, autoresearch, team, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, deep-interview, self-improve
+ 상태 전용 = ralplan, omc-teams, skill-active, ultragoal
</code></pre>
<p>Query active modes with <code>getActiveModes</code>/<code>isModeActive</code>/<code>getAllModeStatuses</code> and clear with <code>clearModeState</code>. The <code>cancel</code> skill (<code>/oh-my-claudecode:cancel</code>) terminates the active mode, and the reason string of the persistent loop always indicates "After completion/verification, exit cleanly with <code>/cancel</code>", ensuring an exit that breaks the infinite loop. <code>scripts/test-mutual-exclusion.ts</code> tests for mode mutual exclusion (e.g. ralph includes ultrawork, thus avoiding simultaneous active conflicts).</p>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>000_overview/00_omc_overview.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>

<h2>9. 모드 레지스트리와 상호 배제</h2>
<h2>9. 모드 레지스트리와 상호 배제</h2>
<p><code>src/hooks/mode-registry/</code>가 실행 모드를 일급으로 등록한다. <code>state-tools.ts</code>의 정본 모드:</p>
<pre><code>EXECUTION_MODES = autopilot, autoresearch, team, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, deep-interview, self-improve
+ 상태 전용 = ralplan, omc-teams, skill-active, ultragoal
</code></pre>
<p><code>getActiveModes</code>/<code>isModeActive</code>/<code>getAllModeStatuses</code>로 활성 모드를 질의하고, <code>clearModeState</code>로 정리한다. <code>cancel</code> 스킬(<code>/oh-my-claudecode:cancel</code>)이 활성 모드를 종료하며, 영속 루프의 reason 문자열은 항상 "완료·검증 후 <code>/cancel</code>로 깨끗이 종료"를 안내해 무한 루프를 끊는 출구를 보장한다. <code>scripts/test-mutual-exclusion.ts</code>가 모드 상호 배제를 테스트한다(예: ralph가 ultrawork를 포함하므로 동시 활성 충돌 방지).</p>

<h2>Source code snippets</h2>
<h3><code>170_oh-my-claudecode/README.md</code></h3>
<h2>Quick Start</h2>
<p><strong>Step 1: Install</strong></p>
<p>Marketplace/plugin install (recommended for most Claude Code users).
These are Claude Code slash commands — enter them <strong>one at a time</strong> (pasting both lines at once will fail):</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode
</code></pre>
<p>Then:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">/plugin install oh-my-claudecode
</code></pre>
<p>If you prefer the npm CLI/runtime path instead of the marketplace flow:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm i -g oh-my-claude-sisyphus@latest
</code></pre>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Known npm warning:</strong> npm may print <code>deprecated prebuild-install@7.1.3</code> during the CLI install.
This currently comes from the upstream <code>better-sqlite3</code> native-addon dependency
(<code>better-sqlite3 -> prebuild-install</code>); <code>prebuild-install@7.1.3</code> is still the latest
published version, so there is no safe repo-side dependency bump or override to remove
the warning yet. The warning is tracked in <a href="https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode/issues/2913">#2913</a>
and does not by itself mean the OMC CLI install failed.</p>
</blockquote>
<p><strong>Step 2: Setup</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">

## Main analysis

## 10. What OMC adds compared to vanilla Claude Code (Summary)

## 10. What OMC adds compared to vanilla Claude Code (Summary)

| tier | Vanilla Claude Code ([[00_cc_overview]]) | OMC added |
| --- | --- | --- |
| agent | Universal single agent + Task subagent | 19 role-specific agents (specifying model tier and read-only boundaries) |
| Delegation | User Manual Instructions | `&#x3C;delegation_rules>`·Automatic delegation rules with model routing |
| Permanence | If `end_turn`, turn ends | **Autonomous Loop Beyond Turns** with Stop Hook `decision:block` (ralph/autopilot/ultragoal) |
| status | In-session conversation + memory.md | `.omc/` disk status·notepad·project memory·wiki·ledger, session·compression restoration |
| code intel | Grep/Glob (text) | LSP(definition/reference/diagnosis/rename) + AST grep + python_repl |
| multi worker | Serial Task | N worker team based on tmux+worktree, messaging·merge·heartbeat |
| external model | None | Codex·Gemini CLI agreement via ask/ccg |
| Keyword UX | slash command | UserPromptSubmit Hook Magic Keyword Automatic Trigger |

OMC does not reimplement the core agent loop. **Orchestration layer** is added by making full use of the host's Hook, Task, MCP, and plugin extension points.

### 10.1 End-to-end tracking example: “Make X on autopilot”

If we string the source flow in one line:

1. User input includes "autopilot"/"build me" → `keyword-detector.mjs` in `UserPromptSubmit` triggers `autopilot` skill (`[MAGIC KEYWORD]`).
2. `skills/autopilot/SKILL.md` active → Write `.omc/state/autopilot-state.json{active:true, prompt}` with `state_write`. If there is `ralplan-*`/`deep-interview-*` output, Phase is skipped.
3. **Phase 0/1**: Spec/plan created by analyst(opus)+architect(opus), planner writes `.omc/plans/*.md`.
4. **Phase 2**: Parallel spawning of executor(s) as Tasks (read-only agents are sealed with `disallowedTools`). Code Intel with LSP/AST. `PostToolUse` Hook updates project-memory.
5. **Phase 4**: Verifier collects fresh evidence with `lsp_diagnostics`/test, up to 5 QA cycles (same error 3 times → abort).
6. When the turn is about to end, the `Stop` Hook (`persistent-mode.mjs`) reads `.omc/state/autopilot-state.json`, and if it is incomplete, **turn re-entry** with `{decision:"block", reason:"continue ..."}`.
7. When all phases are completed and verification is passed, exit the mode with `/oh-my-claudecode:cancel` guided by reason, `state_clear`. If compression occurs during the process, `PreCompact` Hook checks the memory/wiki, and if it is a new session, `SessionStart` restores the state.

§3 (Agent), §4 (Skill), §7 (Hook), and §8 (State) are all mobilized in this one flow — all components of OMC converge on one goal: “autonomous execution beyond turn/session/compression to completion.”

---

## Korean corpus (reference)

> Verbatim KO corpus `000_overview/00_omc_overview.md` for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

## 10. 바닐라 Claude Code 대비 OMC가 더하는 것 (요약)

## 10. 바닐라 Claude Code 대비 OMC가 더하는 것 (요약)

| 계층 | 바닐라 Claude Code ([[00_cc_overview]]) | OMC가 추가 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 에이전트 | 범용 단일 에이전트 + Task 서브에이전트 | 19개 역할 특화 에이전트(모델 티어·읽기전용 경계 명시) |
| 위임 | 사용자가 수동 지시 | `&#x3C;delegation_rules>`·모델 라우팅으로 자동 위임 규약 |
| 영속성 | `end_turn`이면 턴 종료 | Stop Hook `decision:block`으로 **턴을 넘는 자율 루프** (ralph/autopilot/ultragoal) |
| 상태 | 세션 내 대화 + memory.md | `.omc/` 디스크 상태·노트패드·프로젝트메모리·wiki·ledger, 세션·압축 복원 |
| 코드 인텔 | Grep/Glob(텍스트) | LSP(정의/참조/진단/rename) + AST grep + python_repl |
| 멀티 워커 | 직렬 Task | tmux+worktree 기반 N 워커 team, 메시징·머지·하트비트 |
| 외부 모델 | 없음 | ask/ccg로 Codex·Gemini CLI 합의 |
| 키워드 UX | 슬래시 명령 | UserPromptSubmit Hook 매직 키워드 자동 트리거 |

OMC는 코어 에이전트 루프를 재구현하지 않는다. 호스트의 Hook·Task·MCP·플러그인 확장점을 최대한 활용해 **오케스트레이션 레이어**를 얹은 것이다.

### 10.1 end-to-end 추적 예: "autopilot으로 X를 만들어줘"

소스 흐름을 한 줄로 꿰면:

1. 사용자 입력에 "autopilot"/"build me" 포함 → `UserPromptSubmit`의 `keyword-detector.mjs`가 `autopilot` 스킬 트리거(`[MAGIC KEYWORD]`).
2. `skills/autopilot/SKILL.md` 활성 → `state_write`로 `.omc/state/autopilot-state.json{active:true, prompt}` 기록. `ralplan-*`/`deep-interview-*` 산출물이 있으면 Phase 건너뜀.
3. **Phase 0/1**: analyst(opus)+architect(opus)로 스펙·계획, planner가 `.omc/plans/*.md` 작성.
4. **Phase 2**: executor(들)를 Task로 병렬 스폰(읽기전용 에이전트는 `disallowedTools`로 봉인). LSP/AST로 코드 인텔. `PostToolUse` Hook이 project-memory 갱신.
5. **Phase 4**: verifier가 `lsp_diagnostics`/테스트로 신선한 증거 수집, 최대 5회 QA 사이클(동일 에러 3회→중단).
6. 턴이 끝나려 하면 `Stop` Hook(`persistent-mode.mjs`)이 `.omc/state/autopilot-state.json`을 읽고 미완이면 `{decision:"block", reason:"continue ..."}`로 **턴 재진입**.
7. 모든 Phase 완료·검증 통과 시 reason이 안내한 `/oh-my-claudecode:cancel`로 모드 종료, `state_clear`. 도중 압축이 일어나면 `PreCompact` Hook이 메모리/wiki 체크포인트, 새 세션이면 `SessionStart`가 상태 복원.

이 한 흐름에 §3(에이전트)·§4(스킬)·§7(Hook)·§8(상태)이 모두 동원된다 — OMC의 모든 구성요소는 "턴/세션/압축을 넘어 완료까지 자율 실행"이라는 한 목표로 수렴한다.

---
</code></pre>
1e:["$","$L30","07-overview-10",{"id":"07-overview-10","order":16,"tier":"developer","title":"Overview §10","description":"000_overview/00_omc_overview.md","lang":"en","badge":"DEV","children":["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$41"}}]}]
42:T28c6,<h2>Overview</h2>
<p><strong>Overview §11</strong> — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>도구/검색/MCP 레이어</strong>: cli-jaw는 도구 스키마를 지연 로딩(<code>ToolSearch</code>로 필요 시 펫치)하고 grep/browser/MCP를 직접 노출한다. OMC는 반대로 <code>t</code> 단일 MCP 서버에 LSP·AST·python·wiki·state를 응집해 한 번에 등록하고, 명령은 SKILL.md를 지연 로드하는 디스패처로 만들어 프롬프트 비용을 줄인다. cli-jaw의 deferred-tool 패턴과 OMC의</li>
<li><strong>턴 생존 실행</strong>: OMC의 자율성은 전부 <strong>Stop Hook 차단</strong>(디스크 상태 → <code>decision:block</code> → 재프롬프트)으로 구현되어, 모델/하니스 변경 없이 "턴을 넘는 실행"을 얻는다. cli-jaw의 백그라운드 실행/ScheduleWakeup/poll 기반 대기와 철학이 같지만(둘 다 "절대 죽지 않고 폴링"), OMC는 이를 <strong>외부 cron 없이 호스트 라이프사이클 Hook만으로</strong> 달성한다는 점</li>
<li><strong>상태 영속</strong>: cli-jaw가 인스턴스/대시보드 메모리를 두는 것처럼, OMC는 <code>.omc/</code>에 모드 상태·노트패드·프로젝트메모리·wiki·ledger를 원자적 쓰기+파일 락으로 영속하고 SessionStart/PreCompact Hook으로 세션·압축을 가로질러 복원한다. "턴 간(Stop) / 세션 간(SessionStart) / 압축 간(PreCompact)" 3계층 복원은 cli-jaw의 메모리 영속 설계에 직</li>
<li><strong>검증 게이트 분리</strong>: OMC는 "작성 패스 ≠ 검토 패스, 같은 컨텍스트 자기승인 금지"를 <code>CLAUDE.md</code>로 강제하고 <code>verifier</code>/<code>critic</code>/<code>code-reviewer</code>를 별도 에이전트로 둔다. cli-jaw의 verify/code-review 스킬과 결이 같으나, OMC는 이를 <strong>오케스트레이터 규약 + 읽기전용 에이전트 권한 박탈</strong>(<code>disallowedTools</code>)로 이중 보장한다.</li>
<li><strong>출구 보장</strong>: OMC의 모든 영속 reason 문자열은 "완료·검증 후 <code>/cancel</code>"을 안내하고 하드 iteration 상한(<code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>)을 둔다. 자율 루프를 설계할 때 무한 루프 방지를 위한 명시적 종료 안내 + 카운터 상한은 cli-jaw의 /loop·poll 설계에도 필수적인 안전장치다.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Quick Start</h2>
<p><strong>Step 1: Install</strong></p>
<p>Marketplace/plugin install (recommended for most Claude Code users).
These are Claude Code slash commands — enter them <strong>one at a time</strong> (pasting both lines at once will fail):</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode
</code></pre>
<p>Then:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">/plugin install oh-my-claudecode
</code></pre>
<p>If you prefer the npm CLI/runtime path instead of the marketplace flow:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm i -g oh-my-claude-sisyphus@latest
</code></pre>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Known npm warning:</strong> npm may print <code>deprecated prebuild-install@7.1.3</code> during the CLI install.
This currently comes from the upstream <code>better-sqlite3</code> native-addon dependency
(<code>better-sqlite3 -> prebuild-install</code>); <code>prebuild-install@7.1.3</code> is still the latest
published version, so there is no safe repo-side dependency bump or override to remove
the warning yet. The warning is tracked in <a href="https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode/issues/2913">#2913</a>

<p><strong>Step 2: Setup</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">

## Main analysis

## 11. Implications of cli-jaw comparison

## 11. Implications of cli-jaw comparison

- **Tools/Search/MCP layer**: cli-jaw lazy-loads the tool schema (fetching when needed with `ToolSearch`) and exposes grep/browser/MCP directly. OMC, on the other hand, aggregates LSP, AST, python, wiki, and state on a single MCP server and registers them all at once, and makes SKILL.md a lazy-loading dispatcher for commands to reduce prompt costs. cli-jaw's deferred-tool pattern and OMC's "thin command → lazy load SKILL.md" pattern are different solutions to the same problem (non-prompt). Semantic-based code search (LSP/AST) is a reference model that complements the weaknesses of cli-jaw's grep-centered search.
- **Turn-surviving execution**: The autonomy of the OMC is fully implemented with **Stop Hook blocking** (disk state → `decision:block` → reprompt), thus obtaining “turn-surviving execution” without model/harness changes. The philosophy is the same as cli-jaw's background execution/ScheduleWakeup/poll-based waiting (both "never die and poll"), but the difference is that OMC achieves this **with only a host lifecycle Hook and no external cron**. While cli-jaw is strong in waiting for external events, OMC is strong in internal loops until its own work is completed.
- **State Persistence**: Just as cli-jaw places instance/dashboard memory, OMC persists mode state, notepad, project memory, wiki, and ledger in `.omc/` with atomic writes + file locks and restores them across sessions and compression with SessionStart/PreCompact Hook. “Stop / SessionStart / PreCompact” three-layer restoration is a decomposition that can be directly borrowed from cli-jaw's memory persistence design.
- **Team orchestration vs. employee dispatch**: The OMC team **physically isolates** workers with a tmux panel + git worktree and coordinates them with messaging, heartbeat, and merge coordinators. While cli-jaw's employee dispatch is a logical delegation, OMC structurally blocks file conflicts with worktree isolation and specifies the merge stage — a pattern to refer to when conflict safety is needed in parallel code operations. Additionally, OMC can launch workers not only through Claude but also through codex/gemini CLI, making cli-jaw a blueprint for configuring a heterogeneous model worker pool.
- **Verification Gate Separation**: OMC enforces “creation pass ≠ review pass, no same context self-acknowledgment” to `CLAUDE.md` and places `verifier`/`critic`/`code-reviewer` as separate agents. It has the same effect as cli-jaw's verify/code-review skills, but OMC double-guarantees it with **orchestrator protocol + read-only agent permission deprivation** (`disallowedTools`).
- **Exit Guarantee**: All persistent reason strings in OMC guide “`/cancel` after completion/verification” and place a hard iteration upper limit (`OMC_SECURITY=strict`). When designing an autonomous loop, explicit termination guidance + counter upper limit to prevent infinite loops is also an essential safety device in cli-jaw's /loop·poll design.

---

## Korean corpus (reference)

> Verbatim KO corpus `000_overview/00_omc_overview.md` for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

## 11. cli-jaw 비교 시사점

## 11. cli-jaw 비교 시사점

- **도구/검색/MCP 레이어**: cli-jaw는 도구 스키마를 지연 로딩(`ToolSearch`로 필요 시 펫치)하고 grep/browser/MCP를 직접 노출한다. OMC는 반대로 `t` 단일 MCP 서버에 LSP·AST·python·wiki·state를 응집해 한 번에 등록하고, 명령은 SKILL.md를 지연 로드하는 디스패처로 만들어 프롬프트 비용을 줄인다. cli-jaw의 deferred-tool 패턴과 OMC의 "thin command → SKILL.md 지연 로드" 패턴은 같은 문제(프롬프트 비대화)에 대한 다른 해법이다. 의미 기반 코드 검색(LSP/AST)은 cli-jaw의 grep 중심 검색이 약한 지점을 보완하는 참고 모델이다.
- **턴 생존 실행**: OMC의 자율성은 전부 **Stop Hook 차단**(디스크 상태 → `decision:block` → 재프롬프트)으로 구현되어, 모델/하니스 변경 없이 "턴을 넘는 실행"을 얻는다. cli-jaw의 백그라운드 실행/ScheduleWakeup/poll 기반 대기와 철학이 같지만(둘 다 "절대 죽지 않고 폴링"), OMC는 이를 **외부 cron 없이 호스트 라이프사이클 Hook만으로** 달성한다는 점이 다르다. cli-jaw가 외부 이벤트 대기에 강하다면, OMC는 자기 작업 완료까지의 내부 루프에 강하다.
- **상태 영속**: cli-jaw가 인스턴스/대시보드 메모리를 두는 것처럼, OMC는 `.omc/`에 모드 상태·노트패드·프로젝트메모리·wiki·ledger를 원자적 쓰기+파일 락으로 영속하고 SessionStart/PreCompact Hook으로 세션·압축을 가로질러 복원한다. "턴 간(Stop) / 세션 간(SessionStart) / 압축 간(PreCompact)" 3계층 복원은 cli-jaw의 메모리 영속 설계에 직접 차용 가능한 분해다.
- **팀 오케스트레이션 vs 직원 디스패치**: OMC team은 tmux 패널 + git worktree로 워커를 **물리적으로 격리**하고 메시징·하트비트·머지 코디네이터로 조율한다. cli-jaw의 직원(employee) 디스패치가 논리적 위임이라면, OMC는 worktree 격리로 파일 충돌을 구조적으로 차단하고 머지 단계를 명시한다 — 병렬 코드 작업에서 충돌 안전성이 필요할 때 참고할 패턴. 또한 OMC는 워커를 Claude뿐 아니라 codex/gemini CLI로도 띄울 수 있어, cli-jaw가 이종 모델 워커 풀을 구성할 때의 청사진이 된다.
- **검증 게이트 분리**: OMC는 "작성 패스 ≠ 검토 패스, 같은 컨텍스트 자기승인 금지"를 `CLAUDE.md`로 강제하고 `verifier`/`critic`/`code-reviewer`를 별도 에이전트로 둔다. cli-jaw의 verify/code-review 스킬과 결이 같으나, OMC는 이를 **오케스트레이터 규약 + 읽기전용 에이전트 권한 박탈**(`disallowedTools`)로 이중 보장한다.
- **출구 보장**: OMC의 모든 영속 reason 문자열은 "완료·검증 후 `/cancel`"을 안내하고 하드 iteration 상한(`OMC_SECURITY=strict`)을 둔다. 자율 루프를 설계할 때 무한 루프 방지를 위한 명시적 종료 안내 + 카운터 상한은 cli-jaw의 /loop·poll 설계에도 필수적인 안전장치다.

---
</code></pre>

<p>이 문서는 <code>Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode</code>(OMC, npm명 <code>oh-my-claude-sisyphus</code> v4.14.4)가 <strong>터미널 UI / 입력 표면</strong> 차원에서 무엇을 가지고 무엇을 갖지 않는지 소스 기반으로 분석한다. 결론을 먼저 말하면: <strong>OMC는 자체 TUI 렌더러·입력 루프·대화 스트리밍 표시를 갖지 않는다.</strong> REPL·키 입력·붙여넣기·도구 결과 렌더링·alt screen·더블 버퍼링</p>

</blockquote>

<hr>
<ul>
<li>[[01_cc_tui]]가 분석한 Claude Code TUI는 커스텀 Ink 포크(더블 버퍼, alt screen, <code>useInput</code>/붙여넣기/이미지 드롭) + Yoga flexbox + React Reconciler + AppState 스토어 + <code>ToolRenderer</code> 인터페이스 + 권한 모드 UI로 이루어진 완전한 풀스크린 React 앱이다. OMC는 이 중 <strong>어느 것도 재구현하지 않는다.</strong> 코드로 검증된 부재:</li>
<li>| TUI 능력 | Claude Code (호스트) | OMC | 검증 |</li>
<li>| 터미널 렌더 런타임(Ink/Ratatui) | 커스텀 Ink 포크 | 없음 | <code>grep -ril "from 'ink'\|ratatui\|blessed" src</code> → 0건 |</li>
<li>| 레이아웃 엔진(Yoga/flexbox) | Yoga | 없음 (수동 문자열 폭 계산) | <code>src/hud/render.ts</code>의 <code>stringWidth</code>/<code>getCharWidth</code> 기반 truncate/wrap |</li>
<li>| 입력 루프(REPL, 키, 붙여넣기, 이미지) | <code>useInput</code> + <code>Z24</code> 핸들러 | 없음 | OMC 입력 표면은 훅 stdin JSON뿐(§3) |</li>
<li>| 대화/스트리밍 메시지 영역 | React 상태 갱신 + 쓰로틀 | 없음 (전사본을 <strong>사후</strong> 파싱만) | <code>src/hud/transcript.ts</code>가 <code>transcript_path</code> JSONL을 읽기 전용 파싱 |</li>
<li>| 도구별 렌더러(<code>ToolRenderer</code>) | 5-메서드 인터페이스 | 없음 (마지막 도구명만 배지) | <code>src/hud/elements/last-tool.ts</code> |</li>
</ul>

<li>
<ol start="4">
<li>대화/스트리밍 표시 — 사후 전사본 파싱(읽기 전용 미러)</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>4.1 데이터 흐름 (호스트 1차 → OMC 2차)</li>
</ul>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) TUI/input surface</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis Snapshot</strong>: <code>/Users/jun/Developer/codex/170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> (plugin + statusline/HUD sources).</p>
</blockquote>
<blockquote>
<p>This document provides a source-based analysis of what <code>Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode</code> (OMC, npm name <code>oh-my-claude-sisyphus</code> v4.14.4) has and does not have in the <strong>Terminal UI / Input Surface</strong> dimension. To conclude: <strong>OMC does not have its own TUI renderer, input loop, or dialog streaming representation.</strong> The REPL, keystrokes, pasting, tool results rendering, alt screen, and double buffering are all handled by host Claude Code's custom Ink/Yoga stack ([[01_cc_tui]]). What OMC adds to the input/output surface is exactly three-pronged — (1) <strong>OMC HUD</strong> (<code>src/hud/</code>, ~30 modules, pure ANSI string renderer) on top of the Claude Code <code>statusLine</code> hook, (2) <strong>magic keyword input interception + slash command dispatcher</strong> in the <code>UserPromptSubmit</code> hook, and (3) in the Stop/SessionEnd hook. Out-of-band output surfaces such as <strong>Notifications (Telegram/Discord/Slack) + OpenClaw gateway signals</strong>. In other words, the TUI dimension of OMC is a “host delegation + single statusline HUD augmentation” model.</p>
<p>Base path: <code>src/hud/**</code>, <code>scripts/lib/hud-wrapper-template.txt</code>, <code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code>, <code>commands/hud.md</code>, <code>scripts/keyword-detector.mjs</code>, <code>scripts/skill-injector.mjs</code>, <code>commands/*.md</code>, <code>skills/configure-notifications/SKILL.md</code>, <code>src/openclaw/**</code>, <code>docs/CJK-IME-KNOWN-ISSUES.md</code>, <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>, <code>package.json</code>, <code>src/cli/hud-watch.ts</code>.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr>
<h2>1. Intentional absence — TUI surface owned by the host</h2>
<p>Claude Code TUI, analyzed by [[01_cc_tui]], is a complete full-screen React app consisting of a custom Ink fork (double buffer, alt screen, <code>useInput</code>/paste/image drop) + Yoga flexbox + React Reconciler + AppState store + <code>ToolRenderer</code> interface + permission mode UI. OMC is not reimplementing <strong>any of these</strong>. Absence verified in code:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>TUI Abilities</th>
<th>Claude Code (Host)</th>
<th>OMC</th>
<th>verification</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Terminal Render Runtime (Ink/Ratatui)</td>
<td>Custom Ink Fork</td>
<td>None</td>
<td><code>grep -ril "from 'ink'|ratatui|blessed" src</code> → 0 results</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layout engine (Yoga/flexbox)</td>
<td>Yoga</td>
<td>None (manual string width calculation)</td>
<td>truncate/wrap based on <code>stringWidth</code>/<code>getCharWidth</code> in <code>src/hud/render.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>input loop (REPL, key, paste, image)</td>
<td><code>useInput</code> + <code>Z24</code> handler</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>OMC input surface only hooks stdin JSON (§3)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Chat/Streaming Message Area</td>
<td>React state update + throttle</td>
<td>None (only <strong>post</strong> parsing of transcript)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/transcript.ts</code> parsing <code>transcript_path</code> JSONL read-only</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tool-specific renderer (<code>ToolRenderer</code>)</td>
<td>5-method interface</td>
<td>None (only the last tool name is badged)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/elements/last-tool.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Permission approval UI</td>
<td>local-jsx component</td>
<td>None (only <strong>display</strong> standby permission as a badge)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/elements/permission.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>alt screen / double buffer</td>
<td><code>\x1b[?1049h</code> etc.</td>
<td>none (statusLine is one line stdout)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/index.ts:564</code> <code>console.log(output)</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Theme system (dark/light/auto)</td>
<td><code>ThemeProvider</code></td>
<td>None (fixed ANSI 8/16 colors)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/colors.ts</code> hardcoding SGR</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The strongest evidence of its absence is <code>docs/CJK-IME-KNOWN-ISSUES.md</code>. This document addresses Korean/Japanese/Chinese IME input issues (characters not visible during composition, cursor misposition), and lists the root cause as <strong>"Because Claude Code CLI renders the terminal UI with React Ink (raw mode does not provide <code>compositionstart</code>/<code>compositionupdate</code> callbacks)"</strong>. If the OMC owned the input pipeline, they would have written it off as a bug in their own code — instead, they documented it as a limitation of the host (Ink TextInput) and only presented a workaround. <strong>For OMC, the input surface is the host's responsibility, not its own.</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph Host["Claude Code 코어 TUI (호스트, 01_cc_tui)"]
        Ink["커스텀 Ink 포크&#x3C;br/>(alt screen·더블버퍼·useInput·붙여넣기·이미지)"]
        Yoga["Yoga flexbox"]
        Msg["대화/도구 결과 렌더 (ToolRenderer)"]
        SL["statusLine 훅 인터페이스&#x3C;br/>(stdin JSON → 한 줄 stdout)"]
        Ink --> Yoga --> Msg
        Ink --> SL
    end
    subgraph OMC["OMC가 더하는 입력/출력 표면 (전부 호스트 확장점 위)"]
        HUD["OMC HUD (src/hud/)&#x3C;br/>순수 ANSI 문자열 렌더러"]
        KW["매직 키워드 가로채기&#x3C;br/>(UserPromptSubmit 훅)"]
        Cmd["슬래시 명령 디스패처&#x3C;br/>(commands/*.md → SKILL.md)"]
        Notif["알림 + OpenClaw 게이트웨이&#x3C;br/>(Stop/SessionEnd 대역외 출력)"]
    end
    SL -->|stdin JSON| HUD
    HUD -->|한 줄(또는 멀티라인) ANSI| SL
    Ink -.프롬프트.-> KW
    Ink -.슬래시.-> Cmd
    Ink -.턴 종료.-> Notif
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>2. OMC HUD — unique self-render surface (<code>src/hud/</code>)</h2>
<p>The only path through which the OMC actually draws pixels (characters) on the screen is <strong>statusline HUD</strong>. Claude Code's <code>settings.json</code> <code>statusLine</code> hook calls an external command at every turn/cycle, passes the session status to <strong>stdin JSON</strong>, and draws a <strong>stdout line (or multiple lines)</strong> of the command in the status line right above the input window. OMC inserts its HUD script using the command of this hook.</p>
<h3>2.1 Installation/startup chain (settings.json statusLine → wrapper → dist/hud)</h3>
<p>Important structural fact: <strong>HUD is not in the OMC's <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>.</strong> <code>hooks.json</code> only has <code>UserPromptSubmit</code>/<code>Stop</code>/<code>SessionEnd</code>/<code>SubagentStop</code> (no <code>statusLine</code>·<code>Notification</code> items). HUD wiring is a separate installation step where the <code>hud</code> skill writes directly to the <code>statusLine</code> field in the user's <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code> (<code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code> Step 5):</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{ "statusLine": { "type": "command",
    "command": "node ${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hud/omc-hud.mjs" } }
</code></pre>
<p><code>omc-hud.mjs</code> is not written by hand, but is a copy of the original template <code>scripts/lib/hud-wrapper-template.txt</code> (drift test <code>src/__tests__/hud-wrapper-template-sync.test.ts</code> forces synchronization). This wrapper is just a <strong>version resolver</strong>, and it finds the actual renderer (<code>dist/hud/index.js</code>) with 5 levels of priority and dynamically <code>import()</code>s it (<code>hud-wrapper-template.txt:92~245</code>):</p>
<ol>
<li><code>dist/hud/index.js</code> in the <code>OMC_PLUGIN_ROOT</code> environment variable (priority, <code>omc --plugin-dir</code>)</li>
<li>Plugin cache <code>{configDir}/plugins/cache/omc/170_oh-my-claudecode/{version}/dist/hud/index.js</code> — Sort by <strong>semver descending</strong> to select latest build, skip unbuilt versions</li>
<li>Marketplace clone <code>{configDir}/plugins/marketplaces/omc/dist/hud/index.js</code></li>
<li>npm package fallback (<code>oh-my-claude-sisyphus</code>/<code>oh-my-claudecode</code>)</li>
<li>If all fails, <code>[OMC HUD] Plugin not installed. Run: /oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup</code> instruction output</li>
</ol>
<p>This multi-level interpretation ensures that the statusLine command produces a single line in any installation type (local development/plugin cache/marketplace/npm). Commands (<code>commands/hud.md</code>) lazily load <code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code> as a "compatible entry point where you do not want to load the full skill description at the prompt every session" — [[00_omc_overview]] The "thin command → lazy load SKILL.md" pattern from §4.1 also applies to the HUD.</p>
<h3>2.2 Render pipeline (stdin JSON → element composition → ANSI one line)</h3>
<p><code>src/hud/index.ts:main()</code> is the entry. Claude Code In direct contrast to TUI's declarative render with React reconciler → Yoga → double buffer, **OMC HUD is an imperative string builder that runs once and dies:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>steps</th>
<th>Action</th>
<th>Evidence</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>stdin listen</td>
<td>JSON parsing by Claude Code (<code>cwd</code>, ​​<code>transcript_path</code>, <code>model</code>, <code>context_window</code>, rate limits)</td>
<td><code>src/hud/stdin.ts:readStdin</code>, <code>index.ts:259</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>context% stabilization</td>
<td>Suppresses shaking by less than ±3% compared to the previous stdin cache</td>
<td><code>stdin.ts:18 TRANSIENT_CONTEXT_PERCENT_TOLERANCE</code>, <code>stabilizeContextPercent</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Width automatic detection</td>
<td><code>maxWidth</code> determined by <code>process.stderr.columns</code>/<code>stdout.columns</code>/<code>COLUMNS</code>, truncate→wrap promoted</td>
<td><code>index.ts:288~298</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Transcript parsing</td>
<td>Read <code>transcript_path</code> JSONL and extract <strong>running agent·todo·thinking·waiting permission·last tool·token</strong></td>
<td><code>src/hud/transcript.ts:parseTranscript</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Read OMC Status</td>
<td>load ralph/ultrawork/prd/autopilot mode state from <code>.omc/state/</code></td>
<td><code>src/hud/omc-state.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Usage API</td>
<td>5h/week rate limit·subscription level (enterprise detection) asynchronous inquiry</td>
<td><code>src/hud/usage-api.ts:getUsage</code>, <code>getSubscriptionInfo</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Render Element</td>
<td>Stringify each element into an independent function → <code>Map&#x3C;name,string></code></td>
<td><code>src/hud/render.ts:render</code> + <code>src/hud/elements/*.ts</code> (28)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layout</td>
<td>Order/group composition with <code>DEFAULT_ELEMENT_ORDER</code>(line1=git/cwd/profile, main=OMC body), <code>|</code> separator</td>
<td><code>types.ts:646 DEFAULT_ELEMENT_ORDER</code>, <code>render.ts:54 PLAIN_SEPARATOR</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>width custom</td>
<td>ANSI preserved truncate(<code>truncateLineToMaxWidth</code>) / separator border wrap / <code>maxOutputLines</code> limit</td>
<td><code>render.ts:87,140,214</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>safety output</td>
<td>If safeMode, remove ANSI·ASCII-only(<code>sanitizeOutput</code>), otherwise blank → NBSP sort</td>
<td><code>index.ts:557~568</code>, <code>src/hud/sanitize.ts</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant CC as Claude Code (호스트)
    participant W as omc-hud.mjs (wrapper)
    participant H as dist/hud/index.js
    participant T as transcript.ts
    participant S as .omc/state + usage-api
    participant TTY as 터미널 statusline

    CC->>W: statusLine 명령 실행 + stdin JSON
    W->>W: 5단계 import 해석(env/캐시/마켓/npm)
    W->>H: dynamic import() → main()
    H->>H: stdin 파싱 + ctx% 안정화 + maxWidth 감지
    H->>T: parseTranscript(transcript_path)
    T-->>H: agents/todo/thinking/permission/토큰
    H->>S: ralph/ultrawork/prd/autopilot 상태 + rate limits
    S-->>H: 모드/한도/구독등급
    H->>H: 요소 25종 렌더 → Map → 순서·폭 합성
    H->>H: safeMode면 ANSI strip(ASCII)
    H-->>TTY: console.log(한 줄 또는 멀티라인)
    Note over H,TTY: 더블버퍼/alt screen 없음 — 호스트가 stdout 통째 교체
</code></pre>
<p>There is no Yoga in <code>render.ts</code>, so terminal width handling is done <strong>by hand</strong> — SGR sequences are skipped in width calculations with <code>ANSI_REGEX</code>, and surrogate pairs (emojis) and CJK double-width characters are measured in codepoints with <code>getCharWidth</code> (<code>render.ts:113~119</code>). [[01_cc_tui]]'s Yoga <code>textMeasureFunc</code> does the same thing (CJK 2-wide) at the engine level, and OMC reproduces it manually with a single statusline.</p>
<h3>2.3 statusLine stdin contract — only input for OMC HUD</h3>
<p>The only input that the HUD receives is <strong>a chunk of stdin JSON that the host passes to the statusLine command</strong>. This schema is the OMC HUD's interface contract and connection point with the host (<code>src/hud/types.ts:47 StatuslineStdin</code>):</p>
<pre><code class="language-ts">interface StatuslineStdin {
  transcript_path?: string;   // 대화 JSONL 경로 — 사후 파싱 소스(§4)
  cwd?: string;               // worktree 루트 해석 기준(resolveToWorktreeRoot)
  model?: { ... };            // 모델 표시명/id (getModelName/getModelId)
  context_window?: { context_window_size?: number; ... }; // ctx% 계산
  rate_limits?: { ... };      // 5h/주간 한도 — getUsage()와 병합
}
</code></pre>
<p>OMC HUD does not receive screen/event information other than this JSON (it does not know key input, cursor, and scroll positions). So, in order for the HUD to know the "conversation status", there is no choice but to use the detour of <strong>directly rereading</strong> the JSONL pointed to by <code>transcript_path</code> (§4). <code>context_window</code> is percentageized with <code>getContextPercent</code>, but statusLine is re-executed multiple times per second and the value fluctuates with <code>stabilizeContextPercent</code> (±3% tolerance, <code>stdin.ts:18</code>). rate_limits takes the fresher 5h/7d values ​​from stdin, and <code>getUsage()</code> takes the Sonnet/Opus weekly, monthly, and stale meta and merges them into <code>mergeStdinRateLimits</code> (<code>index.ts:366~371</code>). The usage API is cached with a TTL of <code>usageApiPollIntervalMs</code> (default 90 seconds, <code>types.ts</code>) so it does not hit the network with every poll.</p>
<h3>2.4 Element catalog and 3-group layout</h3>
<p>HUD has 25 element renderers (<code>src/hud/elements/*.ts</code>, excluding index) as independent functions and arranges them into 3 groups of <code>DEFAULT_ELEMENT_ORDER</code> (<code>types.ts:646</code>):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>group</th>
<th>Basic elements (order)</th>
<th>Meaning</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>line1</code></td>
<td>hostname, cwd, gitRepo, gitBranch, gitStatus, apiKeySource, profile</td>
<td>git/environment context line (default top)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>main</code></td>
<td>omcLabel, model, enterpriseCost, rateLimits, customBuckets, permission, thinking, promptTime, session, tokens, <strong>ralph, autopilot, prd, skills, lastSkill, contextBar, agents, background</strong>, callCounts, lastTool, sessionSummary</td>
<td>OMC body status line</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>detail</code></td>
<td>missionBoard, agents, contextWarning, payloadWarning, todos</td>
<td>Multiline Detail (Collapse Target)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Presets (<code>minimal</code>/<code>focused</code> (default)/<code>full</code>) turn elements on/off, and <code>elementOrder</code>/<code>layout</code> control order and group movement. In the default configuration, the three types of git, cwd, hostname, and apiKeySource are off for backward compatibility, and ralph/autopilot/prd/agents (multiline, maximum 5 lines)/thinking/todos are on (<code>types.ts DEFAULT_HUD_CONFIG</code>). <code>permissionStatus</code> is off by default because there are many heuristic false positives — permissions are handled by the host as a real UI, and the HUD is only displayed, so it was turned off conservatively. <code>mission-board</code> is an opt-in overall execution progress tracking element that reads the <code>.omc/team/</code> mailbox to collect multi-worker status (<code>mission-board.ts:429</code>), but this is also a string synthesized in the statusline detail group, not the alt screen full screen board.</p>
<h3>2.5 safeMode and single-line constraints — Consequences of lack of double buffering</h3>
<p>The OMC HUD has no double buffer or alt screen. statusLine is a model in which the host re-executes the command every cycle and completely replaces stdout, so the OMC has no screen area to draw the “next frame”. This limitation gives rise to two defenses:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>safeMode (default on)</strong>: Strip ANSI and output as ASCII-only to prevent terminal render corruption (especially Windows·tmux) during simultaneous updates (<code>index.ts:557</code>, issue #346). The emoji badge (<code>🔧 🤖 ⚡</code>) also falls back to <code>T: A: S:</code> ASCII in Windows/WSL with <code>callCountsFormat: "auto"</code> (<code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code>).</li>
<li><strong>maxOutputLines (default 4)</strong>: Caps the number of lines and collapses them into <code>... (+N lines)</code> (<code>render.ts:214 limitOutputLines</code>) to prevent the multiline agent display from pushing up and narrowing the input window (issue #222).</li>
</ul>
<p>The defense in <code>sanitize.ts</code> is not only intentionally leaving out SGR (color/bold), but <strong>picking out and removing</strong> dangerous control sequences: non-SGR CSI (cursor movement <code>\x1b[A</code>, screen clear <code>\x1b[2J</code>, cursor visibility <code>\x1b[?25l</code>), OSC, stripping simple escapes to regular expressions (<code>stripAnsi</code>, <code>sanitize.ts:46~50</code>), replace Unicode block characters (<code>█</code>, etc.) with ASCII equivalents (<code>replaceUnicodeBlocks</code>). The key is that <strong>OMC HUD must never move the cursor or clear the screen</strong> — that is the domain of the host double buffer, and if the statusline command passes such a sequence, the host render will be broken. safeMode is a safety net that forcibly blocks this boundary violation at the output stage.</p>
<p>While the HUD is an output surface, it also has one rare side effect of writing to state: if <code>contextLimitWarning.autoCompact</code> is on and the token context exceeds the threshold, it writes a trigger file to <code>.omc/state/compact-requested.json</code> (<code>index.ts:519-544</code>). Allowing companion hooks to read this file and inject <code>/compact</code> suggestions — the HUD's only path to signaling the host compression flow beyond the screen. The enterprise/subscription level is detected with <code>getSubscriptionInfo</code> and branches to display <code>enterpriseCost</code> instead of the token-window limit, but since Anthropic sometimes includes a $0 enterprise field even in non-enterprise paid plans, it is replaced only after checking for the existence of <code>enterpriseSpentUsd</code> (<code>render.ts:310~340</code>) — Conservative branching within a single line.</p>
<h3>2.6 watch mode — statusline as separate tmux panel (poll loop)</h3>
<p><code>omc hud --watch</code> polls and updates the HUD outside of the statusLine context (in a separate tmux panel/terminal) (<code>src/cli/hud-watch.ts</code>). Since stdin is TTY and there is no JSON, statusLine execution reads <code>hud-stdin-cache.json</code> left on the disk and reproduces the last state (<code>stdin.ts:writeStdinCache</code>/<code>readStdinCache</code>, session scope path). The loop calls <code>hudMain(true, skipInit)</code> at <code>intervalMs</code> intervals and never dies until the end signal — [[00_omc_overview]]'s "never die, poll" philosophy also appears on the HUD surface (skip cleanup with <code>skipInit=true</code> after the first time).</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. Input Surface — intercept but no render</h2>
<p>OMC does not directly receive keystrokes, cursor, or paste (§1). The only points where the OMC intervenes in "input" are the <strong>hook</strong>, which intercepts text before the prompt goes to the model, and the <strong>slash command dispatcher</strong>, both of which post-process <strong>text payload</strong> generated by the host's input loop, but the screen input UI is the host's.</p>
<h3>3.1 Magic keyword interception (<code>UserPromptSubmit</code> hook)</h3>
<p><code>UserPromptSubmit</code> in <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> sequentially executes <code>keyword-detector.mjs</code> → <code>skill-injector.mjs</code> ([[00_omc_overview]] §4.4). When the text entered by the user into the input window (host Ink) is submitted, the OMC detects magic keywords (<code>ralph</code>/<code>autopilot</code>/<code>ultrawork</code>/<code>ccg</code>/<code>deslop</code>/<code>ultrathink</code>, etc.) as regular expressions in the string and injects <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: ...]</code> system-reminder or inserts the learning skill into the context. This is <strong>input UX augmentation</strong> but <strong>not rendering</strong> — the OMC does not draw the input window or display an autocomplete popup. Keyword results simply appear as system-reminder text in the host dialog area. Detections are evaluated in order of priority ([[00_omc_overview]] §4.4): <code>cancelomc/stopomc</code> → <code>ralph</code> → <code>autopilot</code> → <code>ultrawork/ulw</code> → <code>ccg</code> → <code>ralplan</code> → <code>deep interview</code> → <code>ai-slop-cleaner</code>(<code>deslop</code>) → <code>tdd</code> → <code>code review</code> → <code>security review</code> → <code>ultrathink</code> → <code>deepsearch</code> → <code>analyze</code>. (Note: <code>team</code> does not automatically trigger keywords, but only accepts an explicit <code>/team</code> — <code>keyword-detector.mjs</code> comment.) From an input surface perspective, this is <strong>middleware that transforms a host-generated text payload just before passing it to the model</strong>, not an input widget. <code>skill-injector.mjs</code> follows and inserts the learning skill into the context, but suppresses input bloat with a limit of 5/3000 characters per session and file-based dedup.</p>
<h3>3.2 Slash Commands = Thin Dispatcher (above host slash UI)</h3>
<p>The input, autocompletion, and list UI of the slash command itself are drawn by the host ([[01_cc_tui]]'s slash command system). The OMC only registers <code>commands/*.md</code> (28) as plug-in commands, and each file is almost entirely a <strong>thin dispatcher</strong> that "reads the same name <code>skills/&#x3C;x>/SKILL.md</code> and follows <code>$ARGUMENTS</code>" (the same skeleton as <code>commands/hud.md</code> and <code>commands/configure-notifications.md</code>). From a TUI perspective, the OMC slash command is an input-routing layer that does not draw a new screen component, but only adds the entry point name to the host's slash-exposed slot.</p>
<p>Observed OMC slash commands (entry points exposed on the input surface):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>command</th>
<th>TUI effect</th>
<th>Dispatch Destination</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>/oh-my-claudecode:hud [setup|minimal|focused|full|status]</code></td>
<td>statusline HUD preset conversion/installation</td>
<td><code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications</code></td>
<td>AskUserQuestion Interactive Settings (Host UI)</td>
<td><code>skills/configure-notifications/SKILL.md</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor</code></td>
<td>Diagnostic text output (including whether HUD is installed)</td>
<td><code>skills/omc-doctor/SKILL.md</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/ccg</code>, <code>/trace</code>, <code>/verify</code>, <code>/sciomc</code>, <code>/self-improve</code> …</td>
<td>No new screens, only skill actions</td>
<td>Each <code>SKILL.md</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>3.3 CLI surface — <code>omc</code> binary (off-host)</h3>
<p>The <code>bin</code> of <code>package.json</code> is <code>oh-my-claudecode</code>/<code>omc</code>/<code>omc-cli</code> → <code>bridge/cli.cjs</code> (93,572 lines, [[00_omc_overview]] §1.2), which is a separate CLI <strong>outside</strong> the Claude Code session. Provides <code>omc ask &#x3C;claude|codex|gemini></code>, <code>omc team</code>, <code>omc hud --watch</code>, <code>omc setup</code>, etc. The rendering of this surface is not Ink, but <strong>plain streaming stdout</strong> based on <code>chalk</code> (ANSI colors) + <code>commander</code> (argument parsing) (deps: <code>chalk, commander</code>; no ink/blessed). If <code>omc hud</code> is called with TTY without stdin, the installation diagnosis is output (<code>index.ts:218 showDiagnostic</code> → <code>[OMC] HUD v… | preset: …</code> + <code>HUD script: installed/MISSING</code>, <code>statusLine: configured/NOT configured</code>). In other words, OMC's CLI is a line-oriented CLI, not a full-screen TUI. The external advisor (Codex/Gemini CLI) output of <code>omc ask</code> also receives the stdout of the CLI and sends it back to the line, but does not create its own chat view — the consistency of not holding the alt screen on any surface of the OMC extends to the CLI.</p>
<p>This has significant implications when cli-jaw handles multiple CLIs: the OMC sticks to the non-invasive principle of "let each external CLI draw its own TUI, and the OMC consumes/relays its stdout as text only." Since it does not create its own full screen, there is no structural screen conflict (alt screen competition) in heterogeneous CLI combinations.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. Conversation/streaming display — post-transcript parsing (read-only mirror)</h2>
<p>OMC <strong>does not render</strong> token streaming. Streaming display is the host's responsibility. What the OMC does is read-only mirroring by post-parsing the <strong>transcript JSONL</strong> that the host writes to disk and <em>summarizing</em> it into a HUD badge (<code>src/hud/transcript.ts</code>). Parsing extracts the "conversation state" and its badge representation:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>extract signal</th>
<th>Detection method</th>
<th>HUD representation</th>
<th>Evidence</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>thinking</td>
<td><code>thinking</code>/<code>redacted_thinking</code> block found → <code>active:true</code> (in <code>THINKING_RECENCY_MS</code>)</td>
<td><code>thinking</code> badge (text/dots)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:503</code>, <code>elements/thinking.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Running agent</td>
<td><code>tool_use</code> track <code>subagent_type</code>·<code>model</code>·<code>description</code> with name=<code>Task</code>/<code>Agent</code>, evict when completed</td>
<td><code>agents:… </code> (code+tier color+elapsed time, multi-line tree)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:513~</code>, <code>elements/agents.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>todo progress</td>
<td>Latest <code>todos</code> array in <code>tool_use</code> name=<code>TodoWrite</code></td>
<td><code>todos:2/5</code> (complete color)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:545</code>, <code>elements/todos.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<td>Standby permission</td>

<td><code>permission</code> badge (display only, not permission UI)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:577~588</code>, <code>elements/permission.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Token/Context</td>
<td>stdin <code>context_window</code> + transcript token sum</td>
<td><code>ctx:[████░░]67%</code>, token badge</td>
<td><code>stdin.ts:getContextPercent</code>, <code>elements/context.ts</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Transcript parsing employs several defenses to suit an environment where the statusline is re-executed multiple times per second: (1) <strong>Result Cache</strong> — Reduces re-parsing costs by cloning the same baseResult (<code>transcript.ts:122 cloneTranscriptData</code>), (2) <strong>bounded agent map</strong> — If concurrent tracking agents exceed <code>maxAgentMapSize</code>, evicts the oldest <em>complete</em> agent, resulting in memory flooding. prevention(<code>transcript.ts:528~544</code>), (3) <strong>thinking freshness</strong> — thinking blocks only see what is inside <code>THINKING_RECENCY_MS</code> <code>active:true</code>(<code>transcript.ts:304~306</code>), (4) <strong>persistent session start time</strong> — large transcripts are tail parsed, so the first entry may not be the actual session start, so the actual start time is stored once in the HUD state Stabilize session health (<code>session</code>) calculation (<code>index.ts:337~361</code>, issue #528). Permission tracking is a matching process that inserts the <code>tool_use.id</code> of the permission request tool into the map and removes it when the corresponding <code>tool_result</code> (<code>tool_use_id</code>) arrives, leaving only the “rights currently waiting” (<code>transcript.ts:577~588</code>). All of this is a device for observing the host conversation <strong>without side effects</strong>.</p>
<p>Key difference: [[01_cc_tui]]'s message area is the <strong>primary source</strong> from which React state is <em>updated</em> in real time as streaming deltas. The same information in the OMC HUD is a <strong>second derivative</strong> of polling parsing of the disk transcript, and is merely a condensed summary on one line above the input window, without redrawing the text of the conversation. So the OMC HUD is a <strong>HUD/instrument panel</strong> that shows “what agent is doing now and for how many seconds” in one line, not a conversation view.</p>
<h3>4.1 Data flow (Host 1st → OMC 2nd)</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>steps</th>
<th>1st (host)</th>
<th>2nd (OMC HUD)</th>
<th>Evidence</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>token streaming</td>
<td>Message area real-time update with React state delta</td>
<td>Do not mirror (no re-render of conversation body)</td>
<td>—</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Show inference</td>
<td>thinking block render</td>
<td><code>thinking</code> badge (active freshness)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:503</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Subagent</td>
<td>Run Task tool</td>
<td><code>agents:</code> code+tier+elapse (multiline)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:513</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>todo</td>
<td>TodoWrite Render</td>
<td><code>todos:n/m</code> progress color</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:545</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Context</td>
<td>Internal Token Accounting</td>
<td><code>ctx:%</code> + payload estimation warning</td>
<td><code>stdin.ts</code>, <code>payload-estimate.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Permissions</td>
<td>local-jsx approval dialog</td>
<td><code>permission</code> badge (display only)</td>
<td><code>transcript.ts:577</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><code>payload-estimate.ts</code> only issues a warning by <em>estimating</em> the payload pressure using a local transcript artifact, since the host does not provide the exact serialized API request body to the statusline (<code>index.ts:514~517</code> comments). This is a clear example of the secondary nature (derivative) of the OMC HUD's information — only the host knows the exact primary value, and the OMC approximates it with traces left on the disk.</p>
<h3>4.2 Agent code/tier visualization (OMC native vocabulary)</h3>
<p>The OMC-specific visual vocabulary added by the HUD is <strong>Agent single-character code + model tier case/color</strong> (<code>elements/agents.ts</code>). The tier is encoded into the letter case with uppercase = Opus, lowercase = Sonnet/Haiku (<code>getAgentCode</code>), and the tier color from <code>colors.ts</code> is applied (Opus=magenta, Sonnet=yellow, Haiku=green). Example: <code>architect=A</code>(opus), <code>executor=x</code>(sonnet), <code>explore=e</code>, <code>critic=C</code>. Adjust the density by selecting <code>count/codes/codes-duration/detailed/descriptions/tasks/multiline</code> as <code>count/codes/codes-duration/detailed/descriptions/tasks/multiline</code>, and multiline displays the elapsed time and description (≤45 characters) for each agent in each line using tree characters (<code>├─</code>,<code>└─</code>). This is a dedicated surface that projects OMC multi-agent orchestration ([[00_omc_overview]] §3) in one line, which is not present in the host TUI.</p>
<h3>4.3 Threshold color coding and mode badges</h3>
<p>The threshold function in <code>colors.ts</code> signals the state as a color: context <code>≥85% red / ≥70% yellow / else green</code> (<code>getContextColor</code>), ralph <code>max*0.9 red / max*0.7 yellow</code> (<code>getRalphColor</code>). The mod badge is read directly from <code>.omc/state/</code> and is <code>[OMC#4.14.4] … ralph:3/10 US-002 ultrawork ctx:67% agents:2 bg:3/5 todos:2/5</code> The real-time progress of OMC persistent mode ([[00_omc_overview]] §7) is projected onto the host statusline. If there is an update, add the update notification in one line with <code>[OMC] -> 4.x omc update</code> (<code>render.ts:296~306</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. Out-of-band output surface — Notification + OpenClaw</h2>
<p><strong>Output to other channels</strong> that are not on the terminal screen are also part of the input/output surface. OMC has two things:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Notifications (Telegram/Discord/Slack)</strong>: <code>skills/configure-notifications/SKILL.md</code> receives bot token/webhook with AskUserQuestion (host interactive UI) and stores it in <code>~/.claude/.omc-config.json</code>. A mobile push is sent to the user when the session ends, waits for input, or completes the background. It is not a screen render, but a notification surface that reaches users who have left their session.</li>
<li><strong>OpenClaw Gateway Signal</strong>: <code>src/openclaw/</code> "wakes up" the gateway with a non-blocking fire-and-forget on hook events (session-start, etc.) (<code>index.ts</code> <code>wakeGateway</code>/<code>wakeCommandGateway</code>, <code>buildOpenClawSignal</code>). It sends a signal to the HTTP gateway or command gateway, loads only the whitelist context (<code>sessionId</code>/<code>projectPath</code>/<code>tmuxSession</code>) to prevent data leakage, and suppresses congestion with burst dedupe (<code>dedupe.ts</code>). This is a <strong>gateway output surface</strong> that allows external orchestrators/dashboards to receive OMC session state.</li>
</ul>
<p>These two surfaces are unrelated to any screen components in [[01_cc_tui]] and are additional input/output paths created by the OMC outside of the terminal drawn by the host.</p>
<h3>5.1 OpenClaw signal structure (gateway output contract)</h3>
<p><code>src/openclaw/</code> converts hook events into <strong>signals</strong> that can be consumed by external gateways. The public API in <code>index.ts</code> consists of <code>wakeGateway</code>/<code>wakeCommandGateway</code> (HTTP gateway vs command gateway two backends), <code>buildOpenClawSignal</code>, <code>interpolateInstruction</code>, <code>resolveGateway</code>, and <code>shouldCollapseOpenClawBurst</code>. The signal type is classified as <code>OpenClawSignalKind</code>/<code>OpenClawSignalPhase</code>/<code>OpenClawSignalPriority</code> (<code>types.ts</code>), and when sending, the context is narrowed to a whitelist and only <code>sessionId</code>/<code>projectPath</code>/<code>tmuxSession</code> is loaded (<code>index.ts:buildWhitelistedContext</code>) — to prevent arbitrary conversation content from leaking to the gateway. Minimizing data. If the same event occurs in a short period of time, collapse the burst with <code>dedupe.ts:shouldCollapseOpenClawBurst</code> to prevent external surface bursting. The tmux context is augmented with <code>getCurrentTmuxSession</code>/<code>parseTmuxTail</code>. The significance of this surface is that the OMC emits its state not just to the terminal screen, but also as structured signals that are read by the machine — while the statusline HUD is an output surface for humans, OpenClaw is an output surface for external orchestrators.</p>
<hr>

<hr>
<h2>7. Tight coupling/reuse perspective</h2>
<h3>Reuse seam</h3>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> The <code>src/hud/</code> renderer is a <strong>pure functional pipeline</strong> that takes only a chunk of statusline stdin JSON as input and outputs a single ANSI line, so it can be ported to any statusLine-supporting host (Claude Code type).</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Transcript JSONL parser (<code>transcript.ts</code>) is a general-purpose pattern for read-only mirroring of the host conversation state — reusable as an "observation surface" for other metaharnesses.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Agent code/tier visualization (case=tier, color=tier, multi-line tree) is a good vocabulary for condensing multi-agent progression into one line.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> safeMode/ASCII fallback·NBSP alignment·maxOutputLines cap is a product of the statusLine single line constraint, which becomes unnecessary when moving to a double-buffered full-screen TUI (the design is tied to the host surface).</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The purity of the HUD is partial as it relies on external side effects such as <code>.omc/state/</code>·<code>transcript_path</code>·usage-api.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Tightly coupled risk</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>risk</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>statusLine contract dependent</td>
<td>The HUD is tied entirely to the host's <code>statusLine</code> hook (stdin JSON schema, single-line stdout). If the host changes this interface, the entire HUD will break.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Depending on transcript format</td>
<td>Conversation state mirroring depends on host JSONL block type (<code>thinking</code>/<code>tool_use</code> name=<code>Task</code>/<code>Skill</code>/<code>TodoWrite</code>) — vulnerable to host format changes.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>No IME input control</td>
<td>Because the input surface is a host one, the OMC cannot fix the CJK combination bug and only provides documentation and workarounds (<code>docs/CJK-IME-KNOWN-ISSUES.md</code>).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Version interpretation complexity</td>
<td>The wrapper's five-step import interpretation (env/cache/market/npm/fallback) creates different failure modes for each installation type.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>single row density compression</td>
<td>By cramming all OMC status (mode, agent, rate limit, token, git) into one statusline, the truncate/wrap logic becomes large (<code>render.ts</code>).</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<hr>
<h2>8. Comparison with Claude Code TUI</h2>
<p>Following the format of the final comparison table in [[01_cc_tui]], the same dimensions are compared from an OMC perspective:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>perspective</th>
<th>Claude Code TUI ([[01_cc_tui]])</th>
<th>OMC</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Language/Stack</td>
<td>JavaScript, custom Ink fork + React</td>
<td>TypeScript, no framework (pure ANSI strings)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>render model</td>
<td>declarative (React reconciler)</td>
<td>Imperative (string builder that runs once and dies)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layout</td>
<td>Yoga flexbox engine</td>
<td>Manual <code>stringWidth</code>/<code>getCharWidth</code> truncate·wrap</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>screen area</td>
<td>alt screen + double buffer full screen</td>
<td>statusline One line (maximum <code>maxOutputLines</code> lines)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>input loop</td>
<td><code>useInput</code>/paste/image/focus</td>
<td>None (host delegated) + keyword text post-processing</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Chat/Streaming</td>
<td>Real-time status update (1st time)</td>
<td>Transcript JSONL post-parsing (secondary mirror)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tools/Permissions UI</td>
<td><code>ToolRenderer</code> + local-jsx accept</td>
<td>Last tool name/standby permission badge (display only)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>theme</td>
<td>dark/light/auto <code>ThemeProvider</code></td>
<td>Fixed 8/16 colors + safeMode ASCII fallback</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>status bar</td>
<td><code>zZz()</code> asynchronous statusLineText</td>
<td>OMC HUD (mode, agent, rate limit, token)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>expansion unit</td>
<td>local-jsx slash command</td>
<td>statusLine hook + UserPromptSubmit hook + thin slash dispatcher</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>bond direction</td>
<td>Self-contained full screen app</td>
<td>Dependent on host statusLine/transcript contract</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Bottom line: While the Claude Code TUI is a <strong>complete full-screen React app</strong>, the OMC's TUI dimension is <strong>a single-line dashboard + input middleware</strong> that lives on one of the app's statusLine slots**. Compared to Codex TUI (Ratatui native, [[01_cc_tui]] comparison table), OMC is at the extreme end of having no render runtime at all.</p>
<hr>
<h2>9. Core files/areas list</h2>
<ul>
<li><code>src/hud/index.ts</code> — HUD entry (<code>main</code>), stdin → element composition → ANSI output, safeMode·autocompact trigger</li>
<li><code>src/hud/render.ts</code> — Element composition · ANSI preservation truncate(<code>truncateLineToMaxWidth</code>) · Separator wrap · <code>limitOutputLines</code></li>
<li><code>src/hud/stdin.ts</code> — stdin cache for statusLine stdin parsing·ctx% stabilization·watch</li>
<li><code>src/hud/transcript.ts</code> — Conversation JSONL post-parsing (agents/todo/thinking/permission/token), bounded agent map</li>
<li><code>src/hud/elements/*.ts</code> (25) — Element-specific renderer (agents·ralph·context·limits·thinking·permission·todos…)</li>
<li><code>src/hud/colors.ts</code> — ANSI SGR + threshold color (<code>getContextColor</code>/<code>getRalphColor</code>) + model tier color</li>
<li><code>src/hud/types.ts:47,646</code> — <code>StatuslineStdin</code> contract, <code>DEFAULT_ELEMENT_ORDER</code> group 3, <code>DEFAULT_HUD_CONFIG</code></li>
<li><code>src/hud/mission-board.ts</code> — Overall execution progress board (team mailbox aggregate, detail group)</li>
<li><code>scripts/lib/hud-wrapper-template.txt</code> — statusLine wrapper original (step 5 import interpretation)</li>
<li><code>skills/hud/SKILL.md</code> / <code>commands/hud.md</code> — HUD installation/preset + thin dispatcher</li>
<li><code>src/cli/hud-watch.ts</code> — <code>omc hud --watch</code> polling loop (never dies)</li>
<li><code>scripts/keyword-detector.mjs</code> / <code>scripts/skill-injector.mjs</code> — Intercept UserPromptSubmit input</li>
<li><code>src/openclaw/{index,signal,dispatcher,config,dedupe}.ts</code> — Gateway signal output surface.</li>
<li><code>skills/configure-notifications/SKILL.md</code> — Telegram/Discord/Slack notification settings</li>
<li><code>docs/CJK-IME-KNOWN-ISSUES.md</code> — Input surface host (Ink) ownership evidence.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>10. Implications of cli-jaw comparison</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>TUI Owned vs. Delegated</strong>: OMC did not have its own TUI at all and chose a "dashboard" model that augmented the host statusline with a single line. If cli-jaw is a meta-harness that dispatches multiple CLIs, OMC HUD is a blueprint that <em>each host only exposes statusLine, and a unified status line can be put on top of it</em> — When cli-jaw wants to put a consistent status surface on top of heterogeneous CLI (Claude/Codex/Gemini), "one-line stdout contract like statusLine + post-transcript parsing" is directly borrowed as a low-invasive pattern that does not touch host internals. It is possible.</li>
<li><strong>Input Interception vs Render</strong>: OMC does not have an input <em>UI</em>, but intercepts the input <em>text</em> with the <code>UserPromptSubmit</code> hook and does keyword routing. When routing cli-jaw's multi-channel input (messages per channel), the separation of "leaving input rendering to the host and only post-processing the submitted text" is a boundary that also applies to cli-jaw's PABCD dispatch (separation of rendering and routing responsibilities).</li>
<li><strong>Post-mirroring = Observation Surface</strong>: The key insight of OMC HUD is "Do not render the conversation state first-round, but post-parse the transcript for a second-order summary." When cli-jaw tracks the status of multiple CLIs through file memory and multiple channels, the method of read-only mirroring the transcripts/logs they leave instead of invasively hooking each CLI is the same strategy to reduce coupling.</li>
<li><strong>Value of Out-of-Band Output</strong>: OMC's notifications (Telegram/Discord/Slack) and OpenClaw gateway signals are output surfaces that "reach the user/external orchestrator after leaving the terminal". Exactly the same as cli-jaw's multi-channel + never-dying poll philosophy, OpenClaw's whitelist + dedupe signal design, which "fire-and-forgets the session state off the screen", serves as a reference model for safety devices (data minimization and congestion suppression) for cli-jaw channel notifications.</li>
</ul>
