<p>스킬 시스템 차원에서 특히 중요한 것은 <strong>메타 스킬 3종(<code>skill</code>/<code>skillify</code>/<code>learner</code>)</strong> 으로, 이들이 트랙 B(학습 스킬)의 생성·관리 인터페이스다(§3.4, §4).</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. 트랙 B — 학습 스킬: OMC 자체 디스커버리/로딩 엔진</h2>
<p>번들 스킬과 달리, <strong>학습 스킬(learned skill)은 OMC가 처음부터 끝까지 직접 구현</strong>한 시스템이다. <code>src/hooks/learner/</code>의 finder → parser → loader 파이프라인이 핵심이다.</p>
<h3>3.1 디스커버리 — 3-소스 재귀 탐색 (<code>finder.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>findSkillFiles(projectRoot)</code>는 우선순위 순으로 디렉토리를 재귀 스캔한다(<code>src/hooks/learner/finder.ts:60~</code>, 깊이 상한 <code>MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH=10</code>):</p>
<pre><code>1. project: {projectRoot}/.omc/skills/        ← scope: "project" (우선)
2. project: {projectRoot}/.agents/skills/     ← scope: "project" (읽기전용 호환 소스)
3. user:    ~/.omc/skills/                     ← scope: "user" (신규 선호 위치)
4. user:    {CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR|~/.claude}/skills/omc-learned/  ← scope: "user" (레거시)
</code></pre>
<p>(소스: <code>constants.ts</code>의 <code>PROJECT_SKILLS_SUBDIR</code>/<code>PROJECT_AGENT_SKILLS_SUBDIR</code>/<code>GLOBAL_SKILLS_DIR</code>/<code>USER_SKILLS_DIR</code>)</p>
<p>보안 장치가 인상적이다 — <code>finder.ts</code>는 <strong>심볼릭 링크 탈출을 차단</strong>한다(<code>isWithinBoundary()</code> + <code>safeRealpathSync()</code>, <code>finder.ts:50~</code>). realpath가 소스 디렉토리 경계 밖이면 후보에서 제외하고 dedup은 realpath 기준(<code>seenRealPaths</code>)으로 한다. 이는 [[04_cc_skill]]의 inode 중복 제거와 유사한 동기지만, OMC는 <strong>path traversal 보안</strong>을 1급 관심사로 둔다(<code>skills-tools.ts</code>의 <code>validateProjectRoot</code>도 <code>..</code> 거부 + cwd/HOME 경계 검증).</p>
<h3>3.2 파싱 — 자체 YAML 파서 + 필수 필드 검증 (<code>parser.ts</code>)</h3>
<p>OMC는 외부 YAML 라이브러리를 쓰지 않고 <code>parseYamlMetadata()</code>로 직접 파싱한다(<code>parser.ts:74~</code>). 검증 규칙:</p>
<ul>
<li>프론트매터(<code>^---\n...\n---</code>)가 없으면 <code>valid: false</code>("Missing YAML frontmatter").</li>
<li><code>id</code>가 없으면 <code>name</code>에서 파생(<code>소문자 + 공백→'-' + 영숫자만</code>).</li>
<li><code>source</code> 누락 시 <code>'manual'</code> 기본값.</li>
<li><strong>필수 필드</strong>: <code>name</code>, <code>description</code>, <code>triggers</code>(비어 있으면 안 됨). 하나라도 없으면 <code>valid: false</code> → loader가 스킵.</li>
</ul>
<h3>3.3 로딩 — project>user 우선순위 dedup (<code>loader.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>loadAllSkills(projectRoot)</code>는 후보를 SHA-256 콘텐츠 해시로 식별하고, <strong>같은 <code>id</code>면 project가 user를 오버라이드</strong>한다(<code>loader.ts:25~</code>):</p>
<pre><code class="language-ts">// loader.ts (발췌)
const skill = {
  ...,
  contentHash: createContentHash(content),   // SHA-256 앞 16자
  priority: candidate.scope === 'project' ? 1 : 0,
};
const existing = seenIds.get(skillId);
if (!existing || skill.priority > existing.priority) seenIds.set(skillId, skill);
// 반환: priority 내림차순 (project 먼저)
</code></pre>
<p>이는 [[04_cc_skill]]의 "깊은 디렉터리 우선" / <code>loadedFrom</code> 우선순위와 같은 종류의 문제(중복 스킬 해소)를 OMC식 2-tier scope 우선순위(<code>project=1 > user=0</code>)로 푼 것이다.</p>
<h3>3.4 학습 스킬 프론트매터 계약 (<code>types.ts</code> — 번들 스킬과 완전히 다른 스키마)</h3>
<p><code>SkillMetadata</code> 인터페이스(소스: <code>src/hooks/learner/types.ts</code>)는 트랙 A의 SKILL.md 계약과 <strong>별개</strong>다:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>필드</th>
<th>타입</th>
<th align="center">필수</th>
<th>의미</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>id</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center">(파생)</td>
<td>고유 식별자. 없으면 <code>name</code>에서 생성</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>name</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center">✅</td>
<td>사람이 읽는 이름</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>description</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center">✅</td>
<td>스킬 설명</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>triggers</code></td>
<td>string[]</td>
<td align="center">✅</td>
<td><strong>주입 트리거 키워드</strong> (매칭의 핵심)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>source</code></td>
<td><code>'extracted'|'promoted'|'manual'</code></td>
<td align="center">(기본 manual)</td>
<td>출처</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>createdAt</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>생성 시각</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>sessionId</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>추출 원천 세션</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>quality</code></td>
<td>number(0-100)</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>품질 점수. <code>MIN_QUALITY_SCORE=50</code> 이상만 자동 주입</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>usageCount</code></td>
<td>number</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>성공 적용 횟수(점수 부스트)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>tags</code></td>
<td>string[]</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>분류 태그(보조 매칭)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>matching</code></td>
<td><code>'exact'|'fuzzy'</code></td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>트리거 매칭 전략</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>model</code> / <code>agent</code></td>
<td>string</td>
<td align="center"></td>
<td>실행 모델/에이전트 힌트</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>흥미롭게도 학습 스킬은 번들 스킬에 없는 <code>model</code>/<code>agent</code> 힌트를 <strong>여기서는 지원</strong>한다 — 단, 이는 호스트 Skill 도구가 인식하는 CC의 <code>model</code> 필드와 무관한 OMC 자체 메타데이터다.</p>
<h3>3.5 매칭 스코어링 (<code>loader.ts:findMatchingSkills</code>)</h3>
<p>학습 스킬은 paths glob이 아니라 <strong>키워드 스코어링</strong>으로 활성화된다(<code>loader.ts:84~</code>):</p>
<pre><code>trigger 포함 매칭: +10  (필수 매칭 신호)
tag 포함 매칭:     +5
+ (매칭 있을 때만) quality/20 부스트, min(usageCount,10) 부스트
→ score>0 필터 → 내림차순 정렬 → limit개
</code></pre>
<p>이는 CC의 결정론적 glob 매칭([[04_cc_skill]]의 <code>ignore().add(paths)</code>)과 대비되는 <strong>확률적/순위 기반</strong> 활성화다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. 학습 스킬 주입 경로 (<code>skill-injector.mjs</code> Hook)</h2>
<p>학습 스킬이 실제로 컨텍스트에 들어가는 경로는 <code>UserPromptSubmit</code> Hook의 두 번째 스크립트 <code>skill-injector.mjs</code>다(<code>hooks/hooks.json</code> 검증: <code>keyword-detector.mjs</code> timeout 5s → <code>skill-injector.mjs</code> timeout 3s 순차 실행).</p>
<h3>4.1 디스크립터만 주입 (본문은 디스크에)</h3>
<p>핵심 설계는 <strong>스킬 본문을 주입하지 않고 디스크립터(메타데이터 + 경로 + 요약)만 주입</strong>하는 것이다(<code>skill-injector.mjs:formatSkillDescriptor</code>):</p>
<pre><code>### &#x3C;name> (&#x3C;scope>)
&#x3C;skill-metadata>{"path","triggers","score","scope"}&#x3C;/skill-metadata>
Summary: &#x3C;첫 유효 라인, 240자>
Load instructions: if this skill is needed, read &#x3C;path> and follow the full instructions there.
</code></pre>
<p>전체는 <code>&#x3C;mnemosyne></code> 태그로 감싸고 컨텍스트 예산을 둔다:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>MAX_LEARNED_SKILL_DESCRIPTOR_CHARS = 1000</code> (디스크립터 1개)</li>
<li><code>MAX_LEARNED_SKILLS_CONTEXT_CHARS = 3000</code> (전체 주입)</li>
<li><code>MAX_SKILLS_PER_SESSION = 5</code> (세션당)</li>
</ul>
<p>예산 초과 시 "[Additional learned skills omitted ...]"로 절삭한다. 이것이 §1.2의 얇은 디스패처와 동일한 철학 — <strong>"디스크립터는 프롬프트에, 본문은 디스크에"</strong> — 의 학습 스킬판이다.</p>
<h3>4.2 파일 기반 세션 dedup (issue #2577)</h3>
<p><code>UserPromptSubmit</code> Hook은 <strong>매 턴 새 Node 프로세스</strong>로 뜬다. 따라서 인메모리 Map은 항상 비어 같은 스킬이 매 턴 재주입되는 버그가 있었다. OMC는 이를 <strong>파일 기반 세션 상태</strong>로 해결한다(<code>skill-injector.mjs:readFallbackState/writeFallbackState</code>):</p>
<pre><code>{cwd}/.omc/state/skill-sessions-fallback.json
  → { sessions: { [sessionId]: { injectedPaths:[...], timestamp } } }
  → TTL 1시간(FALLBACK_SESSION_TTL_MS), 만료 세션 prune
  → 이미 주입된 path는 alreadyInjected로 스킵
</code></pre>
<h3>4.3 브리지/폴백 이중 경로</h3>
<p>가능하면 컴파일된 번들 <code>dist/hooks/skill-bridge.cjs</code>(재귀 디스커버리 + 영속 세션 캐시)를 쓰고, 없으면(<code>first run before build</code>) 인라인 폴백(<code>findMatchingSkillsFallback</code>, <strong>비재귀</strong> + JSON 파일 dedup)을 쓴다. 폴백 경로조차 <code>realpathSync</code> dedup과 프롬프트 인젝션 방어를 갖춘다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. 스킬 우선순위 경로 — 세 가지 활성화 채널</h2>
<p>OMC 번들 스킬을 활성화하는 경로는 셋이며 우선순위/성격이 다르다.</p>
<h3>5.1 키워드 자동 트리거 (<code>keyword-detector.mjs</code>)</h3>
<p><code>UserPromptSubmit</code> Hook의 첫 스크립트가 매직 키워드를 탐지해 <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: NAME]</code> 메시지를 주입한다. <strong>우선순위 배열은 하드코딩</strong>되어 있다(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:1030</code>):</p>
<pre><code class="language-js">const priorityOrder = ['cancel','ralph','ultragoal','autopilot','ultrawork',
  'ccg','ralplan','deep-interview','ai-slop-cleaner','tdd','code-review',
  'security-review','ultrathink','deepsearch','analyze'];
</code></pre>
<p><code>cancel</code>이 최상위(루프 탈출 보장), 그다음 영속 모드들이 온다. <strong><code>team</code>은 자동 트리거에서 제거</strong>되어 명시적 <code>/team</code>으로만 기동된다(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:1028</code> 주석). 주입 메시지는 본문이 아니라 "읽으라"는 라우팅 지시다(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:959~</code>):</p>
<pre><code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: RALPH]
Preferred invocation: /oh-my-claudecode:ralph &#x3C;args>
Read fallback: open skills/ralph/SKILL.md and follow its SKILL.md instructions.
IMPORTANT: Start the ralph workflow immediately. ...
</code></pre>
<p>오발 방지가 정교하다 — <code>hasActivationIntentNearKeyword</code>(use/run/start/enable... 동사 근접), 따옴표 안 키워드 무시, 시스템 에코 블록(<code>[MAGIC KEYWORD:...]</code> 재붙여넣기) 제거를 거친 뒤에야 트리거한다(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:580~660</code>).</p>
<h3>5.2 명시적 호출 vs 모델 자동 호출</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>채널</th>
<th>진입점</th>
<th>우선순위 성격</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>사용자 명시</strong></td>
<td><code>/oh-my-claudecode:&#x3C;skill></code> (commands/*.md 디스패처)</td>
<td>가장 강함. 키워드 게이팅 우회</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>키워드 자동</strong></td>
<td><code>keyword-detector.mjs</code> → <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD]</code></td>
<td>priorityOrder 배열 순 + 활성화 의도 검증</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>모델 자동(Skill 도구)</strong></td>
<td>호스트 CC가 <code>description</code> 기반 노출 ([[04_cc_skill]])</td>
<td>OMC 미개입(호스트 위임)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>학습 스킬 주입</strong></td>
<td><code>skill-injector.mjs</code> → <code>&#x3C;mnemosyne></code> 디스크립터</td>
<td>score>0 순, 세션당 5개</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>5.3 파이프라인 체인 (<code>pipeline</code>/<code>next-skill</code>/<code>handoff</code>)</h3>
<p>OMC 고유의 다단계 우선순위 경로는 frontmatter <code>pipeline</code> 배열로 표현된다. <code>deep-dive</code>가 표본:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml"># skills/deep-dive/SKILL.md
pipeline: [deep-dive, plan, autopilot]
next-skill: plan
next-skill-args: --consensus --direct
handoff: .omc/specs/deep-dive-{slug}.md
</code></pre>
<p>한 스킬이 산출물(<code>handoff</code> 경로)을 남기고 <code>next-skill</code>로 제어를 넘긴다. autopilot은 이 산출물(<code>.omc/specs/...</code>/<code>.omc/plans/ralplan-*</code>)이 있으면 앞 Phase를 건너뛴다([[00_omc_overview]] §4.3). 이는 CC에 없는 <strong>스킬 간 상태 인계 우선순위</strong> 메커니즘이다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. 스킬–MCP 결합</h2>
<p>OMC에서 스킬과 MCP의 결합은 양방향이다.</p>
<h3>6.1 단일 <code>t</code> MCP 서버</h3>
<p><code>.mcp.json</code>은 단 하나의 stdio 서버만 등록한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{ "mcpServers": { "t": { "command": "node",
  "args": ["${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bridge/mcp-server.cjs"] } } }
</code></pre>
<p><code>team</code> MCP는 런타임 동적 기동([[00_omc_overview]] §1.2). [[04_cc_skill]]의 <code>loadedFrom: "mcp"</code> 커맨드(MCP 서버가 제공하는 slash command)와 달리, OMC의 <code>t</code>는 <strong>커맨드가 아니라 도구(tool)</strong> 를 노출한다 — MCP가 스킬을 <em>제공</em>하는 게 아니라, 스킬이 MCP 도구를 <em>소비</em>한다.</p>
<h3>6.2 스킬을 다루는 MCP 도구 3종 (<code>skills-tools.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>t</code> MCP 서버는 <strong>학습 스킬을 질의하는 도구 자체</strong>를 노출한다(소스: <code>src/tools/skills-tools.ts</code>):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>도구</th>
<th>동작</th>
<th>보안</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>list_omc_skills</code></td>
<td>project + user 전체 학습 스킬 메타 나열(project 우선)</td>
<td><code>validateProjectRoot</code>(<code>..</code> 거부, cwd/HOME 경계)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>load_omc_skills_local</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/skills/</code> (project scope)만</td>
<td>동일</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>load_omc_skills_global</code></td>
<td><code>~/.omc/skills/</code> + <code>~/.claude/skills/omc-learned/</code> (user scope)</td>
<td>(인자 없음)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>이 도구들은 §3의 <code>loadAllSkills()</code>를 그대로 호출한다 — <strong>즉 트랙 B의 디스커버리 엔진이 Hook(주입)과 MCP(질의) 양쪽에 공유된다</strong>. 출력은 본문이 아니라 메타(<code>id</code>/<code>name</code>/<code>description</code>/<code>triggers</code>/<code>tags</code>/<code>scope</code>/<code>path</code>)로, 모델이 필요 시 path를 읽도록 유도한다(§4.1과 동일 철학).</p>
<h3>6.3 스킬이 소비하는 다른 <code>t</code> MCP 도구</h3>
<p>번들 스킬 본문은 <code>t</code> MCP의 비-스킬 도구를 호출한다([[00_omc_overview]] §8.1): <code>wiki</code>/<code>remember</code> 스킬 → <code>wiki_*</code>/<code>project_memory_*</code>/<code>notepad_*</code>, <code>ralph</code>/<code>autopilot</code> → <code>state_read/write</code>, <code>trace</code> → <code>trace_summary</code>, architect/verifier 계열 → <code>lsp_*</code>/<code>ast_grep_*</code>. 스킬은 행동 규약(프롬프트)이고, MCP 도구는 그 규약이 부리는 영속/코드인텔 표면이다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant U as 사용자
    participant CC as Claude Code 코어
    participant UPS as UserPromptSubmit Hook
    participant KW as keyword-detector.mjs
    participant SI as skill-injector.mjs
    participant Loader as learner/loader.ts
    participant T as t MCP 서버
    participant FS as .omc/skills + SKILL.md

    U->>CC: "ralph로 X 끝까지 해줘"
    CC->>UPS: Hook 발화
    UPS->>KW: (timeout 5s)
    KW->>KW: 활성화 의도 검증 + priorityOrder
    KW-->>CC: [MAGIC KEYWORD: RALPH] "read skills/ralph/SKILL.md"
    UPS->>SI: (timeout 3s)
    SI->>Loader: matchSkillsForInjection(prompt)
    Loader->>FS: 3-소스 재귀 탐색 + trigger 스코어
    Loader-->>SI: 매칭 학습 스킬(≤5)
    SI->>FS: skill-sessions-fallback.json (dedup)
    SI-->>CC: &#x3C;mnemosyne> 디스크립터(≤3000자)
    CC->>FS: skills/ralph/SKILL.md 읽기
    CC->>T: state_write(ralph active) / lsp_diagnostics ...
    T-->>CC: 상태/진단 결과
    Note over CC,FS: 본문은 디스크에, 디스크립터/메타만 프롬프트에
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>7. 바닐라 Claude Code 대비 OMC 스킬 시스템 (요약)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>차원</th>
<th>바닐라 Claude Code ([[04_cc_skill]])</th>
<th>OMC</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>로더</strong></td>
<td>단일 memoized 로더 체인, <code>loadedFrom</code> 4종 통합</td>
<td>트랙 A=호스트 위임, 트랙 B=자체 <code>learner/</code> 로더(별개)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>번들 frontmatter</strong></td>
<td><code>allowed-tools</code>/<code>paths</code>/<code>context</code>/<code>model</code>/<code>effort</code>/<code>hooks</code> 전부 지원</td>
<td>그 중 <strong>0개 사용</strong>; 대신 <code>level</code>/<code>pipeline</code>/<code>handoff</code> 자체 추가</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>조건부 활성화</strong></td>
<td><code>paths</code> glob + .gitignore 매칭(결정론)</td>
<td>키워드 스코어링 + 활성화 의도 검증(순위 기반)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>동적 발견</strong></td>
<td>파일 조작 시 <code>.claude/skills/</code> 자동 탐색</td>
<td>학습 스킬 3-소스 재귀 + 심볼릭링크 탈출 차단</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>컨텍스트 절약</strong></td>
<td>(없음, 본문 주입)</td>
<td>얇은 디스패처 + <code>&#x3C;mnemosyne></code> 디스크립터(본문은 디스크)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>MCP 결합</strong></td>
<td>MCP가 slash command 제공(<code>loadedFrom:mcp</code>)</td>
<td><code>t</code> MCP가 스킬 <em>질의 도구</em> 제공 + 스킬이 도구 소비</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>스킬 생성</strong></td>
<td>사용자 수작업</td>
<td><code>skillify</code>/<code>learner</code>로 세션→스킬 추출, <code>skill</code> 메타스킬로 CRUD</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>권한</strong></td>
<td>스킬 <code>allowed-tools</code> 소스별 검증</td>
<td>스킬 아닌 <strong>에이전트 <code>disallowedTools</code></strong> + PreToolUse Hook</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>텔레메트리</strong></td>
<td>5개 <code>tengu_*</code> 이벤트</td>
<td>(스킬 전용 텔레메트리 없음; 호스트 위임)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>요컨대 OMC는 <strong>스킬 로딩 메커니즘 자체는 호스트에 위임</strong>하고(트랙 A), 그 위에 (a) 컨텍스트 절약형 지연 로딩(디스패처/디스크립터), (b) 자체 학습-스킬 디스커버리/주입 엔진(트랙 B), (c) 오케스트레이션 메타데이터(level/pipeline/handoff/키워드 우선순위)를 얹는다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>8. cli-jaw 비교 시사점</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>지연 로딩 패턴의 수렴</strong>: cli-jaw는 도구 스키마를 <code>ToolSearch</code>로 필요 시 펫치하고, OMC는 (a) <code>commands/*.md</code> 얇은 디스패처가 SKILL.md를 호출 시점에 읽고 (b) <code>skill-injector</code>가 본문 대신 <code>&#x3C;mnemosyne></code> 디스크립터(3000자 예산)만 주입한다. 셋 다 "프롬프트 비대화"라는 동일 문제의 변주다. cli-jaw 멀티-CLI 디스패치에서 각 CLI의 스킬/명령 카탈로그를 풀 본문 대신 <strong>디스크립터+경로</strong>로 광고하고 선택 시점에 본문을 로드하는 설계는 OMC에서 직접 차용 가능하다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>이중 트랙(정적 vs 학습) 분리</strong>: OMC는 "플러그인이 배포하는 고정 스킬(트랙 A)"과 "런타임에 추출·축적되는 학습 스킬(트랙 B)"을 완전히 다른 스키마/로더/주입 경로로 분리한다. cli-jaw가 PABCD 워크플로 산출물이나 채널별 반복 패턴을 재사용 스킬로 승격할 때, OMC의 <code>id/triggers/quality/usageCount</code> 스키마 + <code>project>user</code> 우선순위 dedup + trigger 스코어링은 file-memory 기반 학습 레이어의 청사진이다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>디스커버리의 보안 경계</strong>: OMC finder는 심볼릭링크 탈출 차단(<code>isWithinBoundary</code>)·realpath dedup·path traversal 거부(<code>validateProjectRoot</code>, cwd/HOME 경계)·역할경계 태그(<code>&#x3C;system></code> 등) 스트립을 1급 관심사로 둔다. 멀티-CLI/멀티-채널로 외부에서 들어온 스킬/명령을 로드하는 cli-jaw는 이 "디스크립터 sanitize + 경로 경계 검증" 모델을 그대로 적용해 프롬프트 인젝션·경로 탈출을 막을 수 있다.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>호스트 위임의 명료한 경계 설정</strong>: OMC는 스킬 <em>로딩 메커니즘</em>은 호스트(CC)에 전부 위임하고 자신은 <em>오케스트레이션 시맨틱</em>(level/pipeline/handoff/키워드 우선순위 배열)만 얹는다 — 코어를 재구현하지 않는다. cli-jaw가 메타-하니스로서 각 하위 CLI의 네이티브 스킬 로더를 존중하면서 그 위에 디스패치 우선순위(OMC의 하드코딩 <code>priorityOrder</code>에 해당)와 채널 간 인계(<code>handoff</code>/<code>next-skill</code>에 해당)만 표준화하는 분업 모델로 직접 매핑된다.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>snapshot_to_current 델타</h2>
<table>
<thead>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>변경 기록</h2>
<ul>
<li>2026-05-29: OMC v4.14.4 스킬 시스템 차원 작성. 이중 트랙(번들=호스트 위임 / 학습=자체 구현) 구조, plugin.json 선언적 등록, commands/*.md 얇은 디스패처, 번들 SKILL.md frontmatter 전수조사(level/pipeline/handoff는 OMC 전용, allowed-tools/paths/context/model은 부재=의도적 호스트 위임), 학습 스킬 디스커버리(finder.ts 3-소스 재귀 + 심볼릭링크 탈출 차단)·파싱(parser.ts 필수필드)·로딩(loader.ts project>user dedup)·매칭(trigger 스코어링)·주입(skill-injector.mjs  디스크립터 + 파일기반 세션 dedup), 스킬–MCP 결합(t MCP의 list/load_local/load_global 도구 + loadAllSkills 공유), 우선순위 3채널(키워드 priorityOrder/명시 호출/모델 자동)과 pipeline/next-skill/handoff 체인, CC 대비 요약표, cli-jaw 비교 시사점 4항목 포함.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Source code snippets</h2>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    User["/team 3:executor '...'"] --> Lead["TEAM LEAD (orchestrator, claude 핀고정)"]

</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">&#x3C;Success_Criteria>  모든 발견은 file:line 인용, 근본원인 식별(증상 아님),
                    "consider refactoring" 같은 일반 조언 금지
&#x3C;Constraints>       READ-ONLY(Write/Edit 차단), 안 읽은 코드 판단 금지,
                    핸드오프 대상 명시(analyst/planner/critic/qa-tester)
&#x3C;Investigation_Protocol>
   1) 컨텍스트 먼저(MANDATORY): Glob 구조맵 + Grep/Read, 병렬 실행
   2) 디버깅: 에러 전문 읽기 → git log/blame → 동작/고장 델타 비교
   3) 가설을 더 파기 전에 문서화
   4) 가설 ↔ 실제 코드 교차검증, 모든 주장에 file:line
   7) 3-failure circuit breaker: 3회 이상 수정 실패 시
      변형 시도 대신 아키텍처를 의심
&#x3C;Tool_Usage>       lsp_diagnostics / lsp_diagnostics_directory / ast_grep_search,
                   git blame/log. &#x3C;External_Consultation>: 2차 의견이 필요하면
                   Task(subagent_type="oh-my-claudecode:critic") 또는 /team CLI 워커;
                   위임 불가 시 조용히 skip, 절대 블로킹 금지
</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">**Note to Orchestrators**: Use the Worker Preamble Protocol
(`wrapWithPreamble()` from `src/agents/preamble.ts`) to ensure this agent
executes tasks directly without spawning sub-agents.
</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">- NEVER spawn sub-agents or use the Task tool
- NEVER run tmux pane/session orchestration commands
- NEVER run team spawning/orchestration skills ($team/$ultrawork/$autopilot/$ralph)
- worker = executor only, never leader/orchestrator
</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">tmux 세션 이름: omc-team-{teamName}-{workerName}
</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">worktree 경로:   {repoRoot}/.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}
브랜치(named):   omc-team/{teamName}/{workerName}
</code></pre>
<h3>README / docs</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">team-plan → team-prd → team-exec → team-verify → team-fix (loop)
</code></pre>
<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) subagent / collaboration</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis Snapshot</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — subagent/collaboration dispatch.</p>
</blockquote>
<blockquote>
<p>This document analyzes at the source level how <code>oh-my-claudecode</code> (OMC) superimposes 19 role-specific agents and <strong>tmux + git-worktree isolated multi-worker team</strong> orchestration on top of <strong>Claude Code's native Task subagent</strong> ([[05_cc_subagent]]). OMC does not create its own agent runtime — it uses Claude Code's single <code>Agent</code>(Task) tool, the <code>TeamCreate</code>/<code>SendMessage</code>/<code>TaskCreate</code> native team tools, and an MCP server registration as the host ([[00_omc_overview]] §1, §6), on top of which (1) 19 subagent definitions in <code>agents/*.md</code>, (2) Reinforces worker isolation/merge/heartbeat runtime of <code>team</code> MCP server (<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>) and <code>src/team/</code> (~60 modules), (3) <code>team-plan→team-prd→team-exec→team-verify→team-fix</code> 5-stage pipeline + step-by-step agent routing.</p>
<p>Base path: <code>agents/*.md</code> (19), <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code>, <code>skills/omc-teams/SKILL.md</code>, <code>src/team/{stage-router,role-router,git-worktree,tmux-session,phase-controller,followup-planner,unified-team,...}.ts</code>, <code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>, <code>src/agents/preamble.ts</code> (referenced from the agent prompt).</p>
</blockquote>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    User["/team 3:executor '...'"] --> Lead["TEAM LEAD (orchestrator, claude 핀고정)"]
    Lead --> Plan["team-plan&#x3C;br/>explore(haiku)+planner(opus)"]
    Plan --> Prd["team-prd&#x3C;br/>analyst(opus) [+critic]"]
    Prd --> Exec["team-exec&#x3C;br/>executor(sonnet) [+debugger/designer/writer/test-engineer]"]
    Exec --> Verify["team-verify&#x3C;br/>verifier(sonnet) [+security-reviewer/code-reviewer]"]
    Verify -->|pass| Done["완료 → shutdown → TeamDelete"]
    Verify -->|fail| Fix["team-fix&#x3C;br/>executor/debugger (bounded)"]
    Fix --> Exec
    Lead -.->|Task spawn| W1["worker-1&#x3C;br/>worktree+branch"]
    Lead -.->|Task spawn| W2["worker-2&#x3C;br/>worktree+branch"]
    Lead -.->|tmux start| WC["codex/gemini CLI 워커&#x3C;br/>(one-shot, tmux pane)"]
    W1 -.->|SendMessage / outbox| Lead
    WC -.->|outbox-reader| Lead
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. Two pillars of OMC collaboration architecture</h2>
<p>OMC's collaboration rests on <strong>two orthogonal mechanisms</strong>. Both use Claude Code native functions as hosts.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>axis</th>
<th>mechanism</th>
<th>host based</th>
<th>quarantine</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>delegation</strong></td>
<td><code>agents/*.md</code> Spawns 19 subagents as <code>Task</code></td>
<td>Claude Code Agent(Task) Tool ([[05_cc_subagent]] §1)</td>
<td>context isolation (zero-context briefing)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Coordination(team)</strong></td>
<td><code>team</code> skill + N worker coordination with <code>team</code> MCP server</td>
<td>Claude Code native teams (<code>TeamCreate</code>/<code>SendMessage</code>/<code>TaskCreate</code>)</td>
<td>File isolation (git worktree + tmux session)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Delegation is a one-time call (Claude Code subagent's "fresh agent" model — <code>subagent_type</code> specification, zero-context, [[05_cc_subagent]] §3.1), and team is a long-term coordination (Claude Code teammate model — <code>name</code>+<code>team_name</code>, mailbox messaging, [[05_cc_subagent]] §7). OMC adds <strong>role/model/permission specialization</strong> to the former and <strong>physical isolation, merge, survivability, and 5-stage pipeline</strong> to the latter.</p>
<hr>
<h2>2. Catalog of 19 subagents (<code>agents/*.md</code>)</h2>
<p>Each agent is defined by a YAML frontmatter (<code>name</code>, <code>description</code>, <code>model</code>, <code>level</code>, optional <code>disallowedTools</code>) in <code>agents/&#x3C;name>.md</code> and a <code>&#x3C;Agent_Prompt></code> body. It corresponds to the user-defined agent (<code>.claude/agents/*.md</code>, end of [[05_cc_subagent]] §2) of Claude Code's subagent definition schema ([[05_cc_subagent]] §10), and is automatically discovered by the host and exposed as <code>subagent_type</code> (<code>oh-my-claudecode:&#x3C;name></code>) of the <code>Agent</code> tool.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>agent</th>
<th>model</th>
<th align="center">level</th>
<th align="center">writing</th>
<th>role (source: <code>agents/&#x3C;name>.md</code> <code>&#x3C;Role></code>)</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>

<td>"Where is X/File containing Y/Z↔W connection" Codebase search</td>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<blockquote>
<p>The model tier is the actual value of the <code>agents/*.md</code> frontmatter <code>model</code> field. Claude Code The built-in agent's model override mechanism ([[05_cc_subagent]] §1, <code>model: "sonnet"|"opus"|"haiku"</code>) is used as is.</p>
</blockquote>
<h3>2.1 Specifying prompt skeleton and boundaries</h3>
<p>All agent prompts follow the same framework: <code>&#x3C;Role></code> (responsibilities + specifying boundaries with other agents as <strong>"what you don't do"</strong>) → <code>&#x3C;Why_This_Matters></code> → <code>&#x3C;Success_Criteria></code> → (if necessary) <code>&#x3C;Constraints></code> / <code>&#x3C;Investigation_Protocol></code> / <code>&#x3C;Tool_Usage></code>. For example, the <code>&#x3C;Role></code> in <code>agents/planner.md</code> states "It is not my responsibility to implement (executor), gap analysis (analyst), plan review (critic), or code analysis (architect)" — Claude Code In the zero-context briefing model of subagents ([[05_cc_subagent]] §3.1), each agent is prompted for boundaries so that they only process their own domain. Put it in.</p>
<h3>2.2 Double seal with read-only permissions</h3>
<p><code>architect</code>/<code>analyst</code>/<code>critic</code>/<code>explore</code>/<code>code-reviewer</code>/<code>security-reviewer</code>/<code>document-specialist</code>/<code>scientist</code> (8) are **deprived of writing tools at the host level with frontmatter <code>disallowedTools: Write, Edit</code>. This uses the <code>disallowedTools</code> ([[05_cc_subagent]] §10, "All tools except Edit, Write" calculation function <code>V1_</code> §3.2) of the Claude Code agent definition schema. The prompt's <code>&#x3C;Constraints></code> text (provision) and the frontmatter's <code>disallowedTools</code> (permissions) are applied simultaneously, forming a <strong>double seal of the protocol and permissions</strong> — a pre-blocking method that is different from Claude Code's handoff safety classifier ([[05_cc_subagent]] §9).</p>
<h3>2.3 Dissecting the agent prompt — <code>architect</code> specimen</h3>
<p><code>agents/architect.md</code>(model=opus, READ-ONLY) clearly shows that the OMC agent prompt is not just a simple role statement but also contains an <strong>execution protocol</strong>. Claude Code Subagents start with zero-context ([[05_cc_subagent]] §3.1), so the prompt should be briefed “as if you were the first smart colleague to enter the room,” and OMC even embeds the procedure here:</p>
<pre><code>&#x3C;Success_Criteria>  모든 발견은 file:line 인용, 근본원인 식별(증상 아님),
                    "consider refactoring" 같은 일반 조언 금지
&#x3C;Constraints>       READ-ONLY(Write/Edit 차단), 안 읽은 코드 판단 금지,
                    핸드오프 대상 명시(analyst/planner/critic/qa-tester)
&#x3C;Investigation_Protocol>
   1) 컨텍스트 먼저(MANDATORY): Glob 구조맵 + Grep/Read, 병렬 실행
   2) 디버깅: 에러 전문 읽기 → git log/blame → 동작/고장 델타 비교
   3) 가설을 더 파기 전에 문서화
   4) 가설 ↔ 실제 코드 교차검증, 모든 주장에 file:line
   7) 3-failure circuit breaker: 3회 이상 수정 실패 시
      변형 시도 대신 아키텍처를 의심
&#x3C;Tool_Usage>       lsp_diagnostics / lsp_diagnostics_directory / ast_grep_search,
                   git blame/log. &#x3C;External_Consultation>: 2차 의견이 필요하면
                   Task(subagent_type="oh-my-claudecode:critic") 또는 /team CLI 워커;
                   위임 불가 시 조용히 skip, 절대 블로킹 금지
</code></pre>
<p>The <code>&#x3C;External_Consultation></code> block is key — when the READ-ONLY agent wants to take a deeper look, it can <strong>spawn a <code>critic</code> again with the host's <code>Task</code> tool</strong> or launch a CLI worker with <code>/team</code>, but if delegation is not possible it will quietly skip it (equivalent to Claude Code's fork "Don't race" principle, [[05_cc_subagent]] §3.2-same philosophy — external results (waiting and not blocking). <code>&#x3C;Execution_Policy></code> specifies that "runtime efforts are inherited from the parent Claude Code session", so the agent frontmatter does not pin efforts and follows the host model configuration.</p>
<h3>2.4 Worker Preamble Protocol — Prevent recursive spawning</h3>
<p><code>agents/executor.md</code> and <code>agents/git-master.md</code> specify the following immediately after <code>&#x3C;Role></code> (source line 14):</p>
<pre><code>**Note to Orchestrators**: Use the Worker Preamble Protocol
(`wrapWithPreamble()` from `src/agents/preamble.ts`) to ensure this agent
executes tasks directly without spawning sub-agents.
</code></pre>
<p>This is a contract in which the OMC applies the "flat roster" constraints of the Claude Code teammate model (teammates cannot spawn other teammates, [[05_cc_subagent]] §7.1) to team workers as well. The actual team worker preamble is defined in the "Agent Preamble" block in <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code>, and its <code>== RULES ==</code> enforces:</p>
<pre><code>- NEVER spawn sub-agents or use the Task tool
- NEVER run tmux pane/session orchestration commands
- NEVER run team spawning/orchestration skills ($team/$ultrawork/$autopilot/$ralph)
- worker = executor only, never leader/orchestrator
</code></pre>
<p>In other words, the worker only runs the <code>TaskList</code>/<code>TaskUpdate</code>/<code>SendMessage</code> loop, executes the tool directly, and recursive delegation is blocked.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. team MCP server and tmux + git-worktree isolated worker</h2>
<h3>3.1 <code>team</code> MCP server (<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>)</h3>
<p><code>.mcp.json</code> directly registers only <code>t</code> (state/memory/LSP/AST MCP server), and <code>team</code> MCP server is started dynamically at runtime ([[00_omc_overview]] §1.2). <code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code> (source <code>src/team/</code>) exposes four tools:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>MCP Tools</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_start</code></td>
<td>Starting an external CLI worker (codex/gemini) session in the tmux panel</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_status</code></td>
<td>View worker status/heartbeat/task progress snapshot</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_wait</code></td>
<td>Wait for worker to complete (or timeout)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_cleanup</code></td>
<td>Organize session·worktree</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>This suite of tools manages the life cycle of <strong>external CLI workers</strong> (codex/gemini) rather than Claude's native teammate. Claude workers are spawned directly with <code>Task(team_name, name)</code> on the host, and CLI workers are launched in the tmux panel using this MCP tool.</p>
<h3>3.2 Unified view of two types of workers (<code>src/team/unified-team.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>src/team/unified-team.ts</code> merges the two backends into a single view:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>claude-native</strong>: Claude subagent registered as members of <code>~/.claude/teams/&#x3C;team>/config.json</code>. The host automatically manages mailbox messaging and internal task tracking ([[05_cc_subagent]] §7.2 inProcessRunner).</li>
<li><strong>mcp(tmux)</strong>: External CLI worker started by <code>src/team/tmux-session.ts</code>. Because it is <strong>one-shot</strong>, it does not participate in <code>TaskList</code>/<code>SendMessage</code>, and the lead directly manages the life cycle by creating a prompt file → spawning tmux → reading the output file (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "CLI Workers").</li>
</ul>
<h3>3.3 tmux session naming (<code>src/team/tmux-session.ts</code>)</h3>
<p>According to the comment at the top of <code>src/team/tmux-session.ts</code> and <code>TMUX_SESSION_PREFIX = 'omc-team'</code> (line 22):</p>
<pre><code>tmux 세션 이름: omc-team-{teamName}-{workerName}
</code></pre>
<p><code>detectTeamMultiplexerContext()</code> determines the tmux/cmux/none context using the <code>TMUX</code>/<code>CMUX_SURFACE_ID</code> environment variable (line 26~) and falls back to operate on cmux (Claude's multiplexer). <code>sanitizeName()</code> normalizes session/branch names to prevent injection. The panel layout is arranged as <code>main-vertical</code> by <code>applyMainVerticalLayout()</code> (line 43~).</p>
<h3>3.4 git-worktree isolation (<code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code>)</h3>
<p>To prevent file conflicts for each worker, <code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code> runs each worker in an isolated worktree. The canonical naming defined by <code>getWorktreePath()</code> (line 87) and the top comment:</p>
<pre><code>worktree 경로:   {repoRoot}/.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}
브랜치(named):   omc-team/{teamName}/{workerName}
</code></pre>
<blockquote>
<p>⚠️ The "Git Worktree Integration" section of <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> lists the path as <code>.omc/worktrees/{team}/{worker}</code>, but the <strong>source (<code>git-worktree.ts</code> lines 7-13, 87-89) is the original</strong> and is <code>.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}</code>. The documentation appears to lag behind the source.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>The worktree mode is <code>TeamWorktreeMode = 'disabled' | 'detached' | 'named'</code>(line 24) There are three:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>mode</th>
<th>Action</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>disabled</code></td>
<td>no worktree isolation (shared lead cwd)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>detached</code></td>
<td>detached HEAD worktree (branch not created)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>named</code></td>
<td><code>omc-team/{team}/{worker}</code> branch worktree</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>Conservative cleanup policy</strong> (source comment lines 8-13): "compatible clean worktrees are reused, dirty team worktrees are preserved, and cleanup never force-removes dirty worker changes." In other words, worktrees with remaining changes are not forcibly deleted but are preserved for post-examination. As safety devices, <code>validateWorktreeRemovalTarget</code> (<code>../lib/worktree-cleanup-safety.js</code>), <code>validateResolvedPath</code> (directory escape blocking), <code>withFileLockSync</code> (file lock), and <code>atomicWriteJson</code> (atomic meta write) are used.</p>
<p>This is the same philosophy as the Claude Code native worktree isolation (<code>isolation: "worktree"</code>, <code>agent-{uuid[:8]}</code> naming, [[05_cc_subagent]] §5), but extended to the team worker level — native isolates a single subagent, and OMC team isolates N workers into their own worktree+branch and specifies the merge step.</p>
<h3>3.5 Merge Coordination</h3>
<p>When a worker completes, <code>src/team/merge-coordinator.ts</code> / <code>merge-orchestrator.ts</code> merges the branch into the main branch (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> API Reference):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>function</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>checkMergeConflicts(workerBranch, baseBranch, repoRoot)</code></td>
<td>Non-destructive collision pre-test</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>mergeWorkerBranch(workerBranch, baseBranch, repoRoot)</code></td>
<td>Merge with <code>--no-ff</code> (disambiguate history)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>mergeAllWorkerBranches(teamName, repoRoot, baseBranch?)</code></td>
<td>Batch merge of all completed workers</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>cleanupTeamWorktrees(teamName, repoRoot)</code></td>
<td>Remove all worktrees and branches when a team terminates</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The worktree is only cleaned up when a team terminates, not when individual workers terminate (for post-mortem purposes, <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> Important Notes).</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. team-plan→team-prd→team-exec→team-verify→team-fix pipeline</h2>
<h3>4.1 5-stage pipeline</h3>
<p>"Staged Pipeline" in <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> + canonical pipeline defined by <code>&#x3C;team_pipeline></code> in <code>CLAUDE.md</code>:</p>
<pre><code>team-plan → team-prd → team-exec → team-verify → team-fix (loop)
</code></pre>
<p>Stage routing is handled by <code>src/team/stage-router.ts</code> (per-role assignment resolver), <code>phase-controller.ts</code> (task state distribution → current phase inference), and <code>followup-planner.ts</code> (bypass ralplan follow-up request gate). <code>TeamPhase</code> in <code>phase-controller.ts</code> (lines 3-9) is <code>initializing | planning | executing | fixing | completed | With failed</code>, the runtime phase is inferred separately from the 5th level skill name.</p>
<p>Entry/exit criteria for each stage (Source: <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Stage Entry/Exit Criteria"):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>steps</th>
<th>entry</th>
<th>Breakaway</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>team-plan</td>
<td>Start team call parsing and orchestration</td>
<td>Complete executable task graph</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-prd</td>
<td>Ambiguous scope and absence of acceptance standards</td>
<td>Specifying acceptance criteria and boundaries</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-exec</td>
<td>TeamCreate/TaskCreate/Assignment/Spawn Completed</td>
<td>Execution task reaches terminal</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-verify</td>
<td>end execution pass</td>
<td>(pass) pass gate / (fail) create fix task</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-fix</td>
<td>Verification detects defects, regressions, and incompleteness</td>
<td>Modification completed → Return to team-exec</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>4.2 Step-by-step agent routing (Core)</h3>
<p>The essence of the OMC team is that <strong>different professional agents</strong> are deployed at each stage. Your <code>N:agent-type</code> parameter will override <strong>only the team-exec stage worker type</strong>, and for the remaining stages the lead will select the stage-appropriate expert (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Stage Agent Routing"):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>steps</th>
<th>Required Agent</th>
<th>selection agent</th>
<th>Selection criteria</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>team-plan</td>
<td><code>explore</code>(haiku), <code>planner</code>(opus)</td>
<td><code>analyst</code>, <code>architect</code>(opus)</td>
<td>Unclear requirements→analyst, complex boundaries→architect</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-prd</td>
<td><code>analyst</code>(opus)</td>
<td><code>critic</code>(opus)</td>
<td>Scope challenge →critic</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-exec</td>
<td><code>executor</code>(sonnet)</td>
<td><code>executor</code>(opus), <code>debugger</code>, <code>designer</code>, <code>writer</code>, <code>test-engineer</code></td>
<td>Subtask type matching</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-verify</td>
<td><code>verifier</code>(sonnet)</td>
<td><code>test-engineer</code>, <code>security-reviewer</code>, <code>code-reviewer</code>(opus)</td>
<td>Authentication/password→security-reviewer, >20file→code-reviewer</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team-fix</td>
<td><code>executor</code>(sonnet)</td>
<td><code>debugger</code>, <code>executor</code>(opus)</td>
<td>Type/Build Error→debugger</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>Four routing rules</strong> (Source: SKILL.md "Routing rules"):</p>
<ol>
<li>Step agent is <strong>selected by lead</strong>, not user (user <code>N:agent-type</code> only overrides team-exec).</li>
<li>An expert agent complements the executor — analysis/review as architect/critic, UI as designer. <strong>tmux CLI worker is one-shot, so does not participate in team communication.</strong></li>
<li>When downgrading cost mode, opus→sonnet→haiku is lowered (but <strong>team-verify is at least sonnet</strong>).</li>
<li>For security-sensitive/large-scale (>20 files) changes, force <strong>security-reviewer + code-reviewer(opus) on team-verify</strong>.</li>
</ol>
<h3>4.3 Intent-based routing (<code>src/team/role-router.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>src/team/role-router.ts</code> analyzes the task text using regular expressions to infer the <code>LaneIntent</code> (lines 9-19: <code>implementation | verification | review | debug | design | docs | build-fix | cleanup | unknown</code>) and maps it to the most appropriate worker role. For example, the <code>build-fix</code> intent is caught with a pattern (line 33~) such as <code>\bfix(?:ing)?\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:build|ci|lint|compile|tsc|type.?check)</code> and routed to <code>debugger</code>. <code>RoleRouterResult</code> returns <code>role</code> + <code>confidence</code> (high/medium/low) + <code>reason</code>, leaving routing reasons.</p>
<h3>Step 4.4 Handoff — Context Loss Defense</h3>
<p>During phase transitions, “decisions, rejected alternatives, and risks identified” are only recorded in the lead conversation, and are lost when the lead context is compressed or the worker is restarted. To prevent this, <strong>each completion stage must write a pre-transition handoff document in <code>.omc/handoffs/&#x3C;stage>.md</code></strong> (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Stage Handoff Convention"). The format is 10 to 20 lines of <code>Decided/Rejected/Risks/Files/Remaining</code>. Handoffs are accumulated (verify plans → prd → exec to view the entire history) and are not deleted by <code>TeamDelete</code> (<code>.omc/handoffs/</code> is preserved), so they are the basis for session resume. This is a design in which the OMC compensates for information loss due to Claude Code teammate's history compaction ([[05_cc_subagent]] §7.2) through disk handoff.</p>
<h3>4.5 team + ralph composition — persistent wrapping</h3>
<p><code>/team ralph "..."</code> wraps the entire five-stage pipeline in a ralph persistent loop (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Team + Ralph Composition"):</p>
<ol>
<li>Start ralph outer loop (iteration 1) → 2. Execute team pipeline → 3. When team-verify passes, ralph performs <strong>architect verification</strong> (STANDARD tier minimum) → 4. When architect approves, both sides complete·<code>/cancel</code> → 5. When verify fails or architect rejects, team-fix → team-exec↔team-verify re-loop → 6. When fix loop exceeds <code>max_fix_loops</code>, ralph iteration Incremental/full retry → 7. If ralph exceeds <code>max_iterations</code>, terminal <code>failed</code>.</li>
</ol>
<p>States are cross-referenced with <code>state_write(mode="team", linked_ralph=true)</code> ↔ <code>state_write(mode="ralph", linked_team=true)</code>. team-fix is ​​bound to <code>max_attempts</code> so there is no infinite loop (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Verify/Fix Loop and Stop Conditions").</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. Team worker communication/survivability/governance (<code>src/team/</code>)</h2>
<p><code>src/team/</code> (~60 modules) augments additional runtime on top of the Claude Code native mailbox ([[05_cc_subagent]] §7.2):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Category</th>
<th>module</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Messaging</td>
<td><code>inbox-outbox.ts</code>, <code>message-router.ts</code>, <code>leader-inbox.ts</code>, <code>conflict-mailbox.ts</code>, <code>outbox-reader.ts</code></td>
<td>Cursor-based outbox reads (<code>readNewOutboxMessages</code>/<code>readAllTeamOutboxMessages</code>), lead inbox routing</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>survivability</td>
<td><code>heartbeat.ts</code>, <code>worker-health.ts</code>, <code>worker-restart.ts</code>, <code>idle-nudge.ts</code></td>
<td>Heartbeat freshness, dead worker detection, restart, idle nudge</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Distribution</td>
<td><code>dispatch-queue.ts</code>, <code>scaling.ts</code>, <code>allocation-policy.ts</code></td>
<td>Task queue, dynamic scale (scale_up/down), allocation policy</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Governance</td>
<td><code>governance.ts</code>, <code>audit-log.ts</code>, <code>sentinel-gate.ts</code>, <code>permissions.ts</code></td>
<td>Audit log, early completion blocking gate, worker permissions</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>status/running</td>
<td><code>runtime.ts</code>, <code>runtime-v2.ts</code>, <code>monitor.ts</code>, <code>team-status.ts</code>, <code>unified-team.ts</code></td>
<td>runtime loop, event driven v2, monitor, integrated state</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The lead automatically receives <code>SendMessage</code> in the Monitor loop (arrives as a new conversation turn, no polling required) and polls for progress with <code>TaskList</code>. Since CLI workers cannot enter the mailbox, <code>getTeamStatus()</code> provides pull-based monitoring by combining <strong>worker registration + heartbeat + task progress + outbox new messages</strong> into one snapshot (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Monitor Enhancement").</p>
<p><strong>outbox message → action mapping</strong>: <code>task_complete</code> (task complete/unblocked), <code>task_failed</code> (increment failed sidecar, retry/reassignment decision), <code>idle</code> (assign or shut down task), <code>error</code> (check <code>consecutiveErrors</code> threshold), <code>shutdown_ack</code> (remove from team), <code>heartbeat</code> (delay monitor).</p>
<h3>5.0a Dynamic Scaling</h3>
<p>When <code>OMC_TEAM_SCALING_ENABLED=1</code>, <code>src/team/scaling.ts</code> supports in-session scaling (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Dynamic Scaling"): <code>scale_up</code> (add workers within max_workers limit), <code>scale_down</code> (graceful drain of idle workers — remove them after completion of current task). A file-based scale lock prevents simultaneous scaling, and a monotonic worker index counter ensures unique worker names between scale events. Scale, restart, and spawn all read the <code>resolved_routing</code> snapshot from §6.1, so role-specific CLIs/models are stable throughout the life of the team.</p>
<h3>5.0b tmux/cmux multiplexer context</h3>
<p><code>detectTeamMultiplexerContext()</code> (line 26) in <code>src/team/tmux-session.ts</code> sets the context to the <code>TMUX</code> or <code>CMUX_SURFACE_ID</code> environment variable in <code>tmux | cmux | It is determined as none</code>. On cmux (Claude's multiplexer), the <code>cmux</code> binary is called with <code>cmuxExecAsync()</code> (line 74) to manage the panel, and surface targets that do not start with <code>%</code> are recognized as cmux targets (line 70). In Windows MSYS2/Git Bash, <code>isUnixLikeOnWindows()</code> (line 38) detects that the tmux panel is running in bash. Thanks to this abstraction, CLI workers pop up as <code>omc-team-{team}-{worker}</code> sessions equally in both tmux/cmux.</p>
<h3>5.1 Runtime V2 (Event Driven)</h3>
<p>When <code>OMC_RUNTIME_V2=1</code>, <code>runtime-v2.ts</code> operates as an event driven instead of a done.json polling watchdog (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Runtime V2"): Task completion is detected by CLI API life cycle transition (claim-task/transition-task-status), event is calculated using snapshot delta, Append to <code>.omc/state/team/{teamName}/events.jsonl</code>, worker status is <code>.omc/state/team/{teamName}/workers/{name}/status.json</code>. Sentinel gate (early completion blocking), circuit breaker (dead worker), and failure sidecar are preserved as is.</p>
<hr>
<h3>5.2 Inject AGENTS.md overlay into worktree</h3>
<p><code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code> injects the team-worker overlay into the root <code>AGENTS.md</code> with <code>installWorktreeRootAgents()</code> (line 251) when creating a worker worktree, and restores the original with <code>restoreWorktreeRootAgents()</code> (line 291) when cleaning up. The backup structure (<code>WorktreeRootAgentsBackup</code>, lines 73-79) preserves <code>hadOriginal</code>/<code>originalContent</code>/<code>installedContent</code>/<code>installedAt</code> to ensure non-destructive restoration. This is where the <code>AGENTS.md</code> marker overlay (<code>&#x3C;!-- OMX:TEAM:WORKER:START --></code>) from [[00_omc_overview]] §2.1 comes to life as per-worker guidance inside the isolated worktree — the worker is re-injected "I am a team worker, no recursive spawning" via <code>AGENTS.md</code> in its own worktree.</p>
<h3>5.3 team shutdown protocol (BLOCKING)</h3>
<p>Team shutdowns are strictly ordered (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Shutdown Protocol"). Claude Code teammate's shutdown priority (shutdown priority over unread messages, [[05_cc_subagent]] §7.2) is used as is, but OMC adds additional safeguards:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Completion Verification</strong>: Check whether the actual task is completed/failed before excluding internal tasks (<code>metadata._internal:true</code>) with <code>TaskList</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Send <code>shutdown_request</code> to each worker</strong> (format <code>request_id</code> = <code>shutdown-{timestamp}@{worker}</code>).</li>
<li><strong>BLOCKING</strong>: Up to 30 seconds per worker. Wait until <code>shutdown_response(approve:true)</code> is received or times out. Workers are automatically removed from config.json members.</li>
<li><strong><code>TeamDelete</code></strong>: <strong>Called only after all workers have been confirmed/timeout</strong>. Fail if no active members (excluding leads) remain.</li>
<li><strong>Orphan scan</strong>: Termination of processes that survive after <code>scripts/cleanup-orphans.mjs --team-name &#x3C;team></code> config disappears with SIGTERM → 5 seconds → SIGKILL.</li>
</ol>
<p>If the worker makes up a random <code>request_id</code>, the shutdown will silently fail (SKILL.md Gotcha #8), so the received ID must be returned as is.</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. External model delegation — ask / ccg / CLI worker</h2>
<p>OMC has three paths to pulling into the Claude et al model (all local CLI calls, not direct API keys — [[00_omc_overview]] §5):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>path</th>
<th>Source</th>
<th>Action</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>omc ask &#x3C;claude|codex|gemini> "..."</code></td>
<td><code>skills/ask/SKILL.md</code></td>
<td>Single advisor routing. Perpetuates the result as a ask artifact. Prohibit raw CLI assembly</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/ccg</code></td>
<td><code>skills/ccg/SKILL.md</code> (level 5)</td>
<td><code>/ask codex</code>+<code>/ask gemini</code> parallel firing → Claude synthesized. Fast multi-perspective without tmux worker</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>/team N:codex|gemini</code></td>
<td><code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code>, <code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code></td>
<td>Launch the actual CLI process as a worker in the tmux panel</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Among these, <code>ask</code>/<code>ccg</code> has a similar purpose to Claude Code's <strong>advisor pattern</strong> ([[05_cc_subagent]] §6) — it receives an external perspective before doing the actual work. However, the difference is that while Claude Code advisor asks the stronger Claude model (Opus) on the <strong>server side</strong>, OMC ask/ccg calls <strong>heterogeneous vendor CLI</strong> (Codex/Gemini) locally to synthesize multiple perspectives.</p>
<h3>6.1 per-role provider/model routing</h3>
<p><code>buildResolvedRoutingSnapshot</code> in <code>src/team/stage-router.ts</code> resolves the authentic role-specific provider/model <strong>once</strong> at team creation and freezes it in <code>TeamConfig.resolved_routing</code> (stickiness — invariant for the lifetime of the team). There are 15 canonical roles (<code>orchestrator</code>/<code>planner</code>/<code>analyst</code>/<code>architect</code>/<code>executor</code>/<code>debugger</code>/<code>critic</code>/<code>code-reviewer</code>/<code>security-reviewer</code>/<code>test-engineer</code>/<code>designer</code>/<code>writer</code>/<code>code-simplifier</code>/<code>explore</code>/<code>document-specialist</code>) and (<code>stage-router.ts</code>) <code>ROLE_TO_AGENT</code> mapping lines 30<del>46), the default tier is <code>ROLE_DEFAULT_TIER</code> (line 49</del>: orchestrator/planner/analyst/architect/critic/code-reviewer=HIGH, the rest MEDIUM).</p>
<p>Specify the provider for each role with <code>team.roleRouting</code> in <code>.claude/omc.jsonc</code> (e.g. <code>critic</code> → codex, <code>code-reviewer</code> → gemini). <strong><code>orchestrator</code> is pinned by claude</strong> and only <code>model</code> is adjustable (other keys are rejected by the verifier). Aliases are normalized with <code>normalizeDelegationRole()</code> (<code>reviewer</code> → <code>code-reviewer</code>, <code>harsh-critic</code> → <code>critic</code>, <code>build-fixer</code> → <code>debugger</code>). Priority: <code>OMC_TEAM_ROLE_OVERRIDES</code>(env) > project omc.jsonc > user config > built-in default. In the absence of the CLI, <code>buildLaunchArgs()</code> throws and falls to a <strong>sound fallback</strong> (deterministic claude assignment) — a silent fallback is considered a test failure.</p>
<hr>
<h2>7. Delegating subagent vs team worker — contrasting the two models</h2>
<p>OMC's two collaboration axes (§1) correspond exactly to Claude Code's two subagent model ([[05_cc_subagent]] §7.1 teammate vs generic subagent):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Characteristics</th>
<th>delegate subagent (<code>Task(subagent_type)</code>)</th>
<th>team worker (<code>Task(team_name, name)</code> or tmux CLI)</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Context</strong></td>
<td>zero-context, briefing with prompt</td>
<td>zero-context + worktree AGENTS.md overlay</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Lifespan</strong></td>
<td>Finished after a single run</td>
<td>Long-term coordination (mailbox polling) or one-shot (CLI)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Address</strong></td>
<td>Not possible</td>
<td><code>SendMessage({to:name})</code> (Claude worker only; CLI outbox)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Quarantine</strong></td>
<td>context only</td>
<td>+ git worktree + tmux session (block file conflicts)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Re-delegation</strong></td>
<td>Possible (architect→critic, etc.)</td>
<td><strong>Prohibited</strong> (Worker Preamble <code>== RULES ==</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Merge</strong></td>
<td>Not applicable</td>
<td><code>merge-coordinator</code> <code>--no-ff</code> merge</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>provider</strong></td>
<td>claude fixed</td>
<td>claude/codex/gemini(<code>roleRouting</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Choice Subject</strong></td>
<td>caller(<code>&#x3C;delegation_rules></code>)</td>
<td>lead (step-by-step routing), user only overrides exec</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Key difference: Delegation is a quick one-time expert call (like an architect calling a critic, allows recursion), while team workers are isolated long-term collaborators (no recursion, blocks conflicts with worktree, merges with merge). OMC reuses the same 19 agent definitions for both models — <code>Task(subagent_type="oh-my-claudecode:executor")</code> for delegates, and <code>Task(team_name=..., name="worker-1", subagent_type="oh-my-claudecode:executor")</code> for team workers.</p>
<hr>
<h2>8. End-to-end tracking — <code>/team 3:executor "fix all TS errors"</code></h2>
<p>Putting the source flow in one line (team is not subject to automatic triggering of keywords, but only <strong>explicitly triggers <code>/team</code></strong>, [[00_omc_overview]] §4.4):</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>team-plan</strong>: lead <code>TeamCreate("fix-ts-errors")</code> → becomes <code>team-lead@fix-ts-errors</code>. Decompose the code map with <code>explore</code> (haiku) and the task graph with <code>planner</code> (opus). <code>state_write(mode="team", current_phase="team-plan")</code>. Created handoffs <code>.omc/handoffs/team-plan.md</code>.</li>
<li><strong>team-prd</strong>: If the scope is ambiguous, the <code>analyst</code> (opus) extracts the acceptance criteria (it is clear here, so you can skip it).</li>
<li><strong>team-exec</strong>: <code>TaskCreate</code> Each worker is isolated to <code>.omc/team/fix-ts-errors/worktrees/worker-1</code> (branch <code>omc-team/fix-ts-errors/worker-1</code>) with <code>createWorkerWorktree</code>. <code>Task(team_name, name="worker-1", subagent_type="oh-my-claudecode:executor")</code> ×3 <strong>parallel</strong> spawn. Workers follow the preamble rules in a <code>TaskList</code> → <code>TaskUpdate(in_progress)</code> → directly implemented → <code>TaskUpdate(completed)</code> → <code>SendMessage(team-lead)</code> loop.</li>
<li><strong>Monitor</strong>: lead automatically receives <code>SendMessage</code> (new turn) + polls <code>TaskList</code>/<code>getTeamStatus()</code>. 5 minutes no response → status check, 10 minutes → reassignment (Task Watchdog Policy).</li>
<li><strong>team-verify</strong>: <code>verifier</code> (sonnet) collects fresh evidence with <code>lsp_diagnostics</code>/test. For security/large-scale cases, force <code>security-reviewer</code>+<code>code-reviewer</code>(opus). If it passes, it is 6, if it fails, <strong>team-fix</strong> (type error with <code>debugger</code>, bounded by <code>max_fix_loops</code>) → team-exec returns.</li>
<li><strong>Shutdown</strong>: Merge worker branch <code>--no-ff</code> with <code>merge-coordinator</code> → <code>shutdown_request</code> on each worker → wait for response/timeout → <code>TeamDelete</code> → scan for orphans → <code>state_clear(mode="team")</code>. The worktree is collectively cleaned up (preserved if dirty) upon termination of the team for post-mortem purposes.</li>
</ol>
<p>If it was <code>/team ralph</code>, this entire thing would be wrapped in a ralph persistent loop and retried 6 → 1 until architect verification passes (§4.5).</p>
<hr>
<h2>9. What OMC adds on top of Claude Code Native (Summary)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>perspective</th>
<th>Claude Code Native ([[05_cc_subagent]])</th>
<th>OMC added</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Subagent</td>
<td>General purpose + Built-in Explore/Plan/fork etc. + <code>.claude/agents/*</code></td>
<td>19 role-specific agents (model tier, read-only boundary, and <code>&#x3C;Role></code> non-responsibility specified)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Permissions</td>
<td><code>disallowedTools</code>, handoff classifier</td>
<td>8 read-only agent <code>disallowedTools</code> + prompt protocol double seal</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>teammate</td>
<td><code>name</code>+<code>team_name</code>, inProcessRunner poll mailbox</td>
<td><code>team</code> MCP + <code>src/team/</code> 60 modules (heartbeat/merge/governance/scale)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>worktree isolation</td>
<td><code>isolation:"worktree"</code>, <code>agent-{uuid}</code></td>
<td>NWorker <code>.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}</code> + <code>omc-team/{team}/{worker}</code> branch + merge coordination</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>external model</td>
<td>advisor (server side Opus)</td>
<td>ask/ccg/team CLI worker (Codex/Gemini, tmux panel)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Coordination Structure</td>
<td>flat roster teammate</td>
<td>5-stage pipeline + step-by-step agent routing + disk handoff</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Permanence</td>
<td>single shot/async/teammate</td>
<td>Permanent wrapping from team+ralph synthesis to architect verification</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>OMC does not reimplement Claude Code's Agent/teams/worktree mechanism, but adds a <strong>role specialization, physical isolation, and 5-stage pipeline orchestration layer</strong>.</p>
<hr>
<h2>10. team vs legacy swarm — why native teams</h2>
<p>OMC team replaces the SQLite-based legacy <code>swarm</code> (alias removed from <code>/swarm</code>, #1131). Both are multi-workers, but have different coordination mechanisms (<code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code> "Comparison: Team vs Legacy Swarm"):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>perspective</th>
<th>team (native)</th>
<th>swarm (legacy SQLite)</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Save</td>
<td><code>~/.claude/teams/</code>·<code>tasks/</code> JSON</td>
<td><code>.omc/state/swarm.db</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Dependency</td>
<td>None (host built-in)</td>
<td>Requires <code>better-sqlite3</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>task claim</td>
<td><code>TaskUpdate(owner)</code> lead pre-assignment</td>
<td>SQLite IMMEDIATE transaction (atomic)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>communication</td>
<td><code>SendMessage</code>(DM/broadcast/shutdown)</td>
<td>none(fire-and-forget)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>dependency graph</td>
<td><code>blocks</code>/<code>blockedBy</code> builtin</td>
<td>Not supported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>heartbeat</td>
<td>Host automatic idle notification</td>
<td>Manual table + polling</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Shutdown</td>
<td>graceful request/response</td>
<td>Signal End</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Key trade-off: Native teams have no external dependencies and a messaging/dependency graph, but <strong>no atomic claims</strong>, so leads must pre-allocate to block the race (SKILL.md Gotcha #2). The swarm was a SQLite transaction, so the claims were safe, but there was no communication or dependency. OMC chose to achieve communication, dependability, and survivability on the host infrastructure, and claim safety is supplemented by worktree isolation (blocking file conflicts themselves) + pre-allocation.</p>
<hr>
<h2>cli-jaw comparison implications</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>worktree isolation 5-phase pipeline vs PABCD + employee dispatch</strong>: cli-jaw's employee dispatch performs <strong>logical delegation</strong> on PABCD 5-phase (<code>dev-pabcd</code> skill). OMC team has the same 5 steps (team-plan→prd→exec→verify→fix), but each worker is <strong>physically isolated</strong> into the <code>.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}</code> + <code>omc-team/{team}/{worker}</code> branch and leaves an explicit merge·<code>--no-ff</code> history with the <code>merge-coordinator</code>. A pattern to be adopted by cli-jaw when file conflict safety is required in parallel code work — By inserting worktree isolation into the B/C/D phase of PABCD, simultaneous editing conflicts between employees can be structurally blocked.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Agent routing by stage vs. single employee role</strong>: Even if OMC has the same 5 stages, the lead selects a different professional agent (plan=planner/architect, verify=verifier/security-reviewer) for each stage, and user <code>N:agent-type</code> only overrides the exec stage. Unlike cli-jaw's employee dispatch that goes through all phases with one role, OMC guarantees the verify gate (read-only + no self-acknowledgement) as a separate agent with <strong>role switching for each phase</strong>. The design of separating verification gates into different models and different permissions from the writing path is a blueprint for integrating cli-jaw's verify/code-review skills into phase routing.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Heterogeneous model worker pool</strong>: OMC team launches workers in the tmux panel using Claude as well as codex/gemini CLI (<code>omc-team-{team}-{worker}</code> session), and pins providers by role with <code>team.roleRouting</code> (critic=codex, reviewer=gemini, etc.). When cli-jaw expands the employee pool beyond a single model, OMC's "resolved_routing snapshot once · team lifetime invariant + sound claude fallback in absence of CLI" pattern is a reference model for heterogeneous worker stability. It is similar to cli-jaw's web-ai/ccg skills (ChatGPT/Gemini/Grok), but OMC elevates it to <strong>long-term coordination worker</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Disk handoff vs. context transfer</strong>: When transitioning to a stage, OMC leaves the <code>Decided/Rejected/Risks</code> handoff in <code>.omc/handoffs/&#x3C;stage>.md</code> to preserve the decision history even during lead compression and worker restart, and does not erase it with <code>TeamDelete</code>, so it is used as the basis for resume. If cli-jaw's inter-employee context transfer is in-memory/message-based, OMC's disk handoff is a decomposition that perpetuates "why this decision" at the PABCD phase boundary, and can be borrowed directly from cli-jaw's phase transition design.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>005_subagents-collab/05_omc_subagent.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) 서브에이전트 / 협업</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — subagent/collaboration dispatch.</p>
</blockquote>
<blockquote>
<p>이 문서는 <code>oh-my-claudecode</code>(OMC)가 <strong>Claude Code의 네이티브 Task 서브에이전트</strong>([[05_cc_subagent]]) 위에 어떻게 19개 역할 특화 에이전트와 <strong>tmux + git-worktree 격리 멀티 워커 team</strong> 오케스트레이션을 얹는지 소스 레벨로 분석한다. OMC는 자체 에이전트 런타임을 만들지 않는다 — Claude Code의 단일 <code>Agent</code>(Task) 도구, <code>TeamCreate</code>/<code>SendMessage</code>/<code>TaskCreate</code> 네이티브 team 도구, MCP 서버 등록을 호스트로 사용하고([[00_omc_overview]] §1, §6), 그 위에 (1) <code>agents/*.md</code>의 19개 서브에이전트 정의, (2) <code>team</code> MCP 서버(<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>)와 <code>src/team/</code>(~60개 모듈)의 워커 격리·머지·하트비트 런타임, (3) <code>team-plan→team-prd→team-exec→team-verify→team-fix</code> 5단계 파이프라인 + 단계별 에이전트 라우팅을 보강한다.</p>
<p>근거 경로: <code>agents/*.md</code>(19개), <code>skills/team/SKILL.md</code>, <code>skills/omc-teams/SKILL.md</code>, <code>src/team/{stage-router,role-router,git-worktree,tmux-session,phase-controller,followup-planner,unified-team,...}.ts</code>, <code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>, <code>src/agents/preamble.ts</code>(에이전트 프롬프트에서 참조).</p>
</blockquote>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    User["/team 3:executor '...'"] --> Lead["TEAM LEAD (orchestrator, claude 핀고정)"]
    Lead --> Plan["team-plan&#x3C;br/>explore(haiku)+planner(opus)"]
    Plan --> Prd["team-prd&#x3C;br/>analyst(opus) [+critic]"]
    Prd --> Exec["team-exec&#x3C;br/>executor(sonnet) [+debugger/designer/writer/test-engineer]"]
    Exec --> Verify["team-verify&#x3C;br/>verifier(sonnet) [+security-reviewer/code-reviewer]"]
    Verify -->|pass| Done["완료 → shutdown → TeamDelete"]
    Verify -->|fail| Fix["team-fix&#x3C;br/>executor/debugger (bounded)"]
    Fix --> Exec
    Lead -.->|Task spawn| W1["worker-1&#x3C;br/>worktree+branch"]
    Lead -.->|Task spawn| W2["worker-2&#x3C;br/>worktree+branch"]
    Lead -.->|tmux start| WC["codex/gemini CLI 워커&#x3C;br/>(one-shot, tmux pane)"]
    W1 -.->|SendMessage / outbox| Lead
    WC -.->|outbox-reader| Lead
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. OMC 협업 아키텍처의 두 축</h2>
<p>OMC의 협업은 <strong>두 개의 직교하는 메커니즘</strong> 위에 서 있다. 둘 다 Claude Code 네이티브 기능을 호스트로 쓴다.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>축</th>
<th>메커니즘</th>
<th>호스트 기반</th>
<th>격리</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>위임(delegation)</strong></td>
<td><code>agents/*.md</code> 19개 서브에이전트를 <code>Task</code>로 스폰</td>
<td>Claude Code Agent(Task) 도구 ([[05_cc_subagent]] §1)</td>
<td>컨텍스트 격리 (zero-context briefing)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>협응(team)</strong></td>
<td><code>team</code> 스킬 + <code>team</code> MCP 서버로 N개 워커 협응</td>
<td>Claude Code 네이티브 teams (<code>TeamCreate</code>/<code>SendMessage</code>/<code>TaskCreate</code>)</td>
<td>파일 격리 (git worktree + tmux 세션)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>위임은 단발 호출(Claude Code 서브에이전트의 "fresh agent" 모델 — <code>subagent_type</code> 지정, zero-context, [[05_cc_subagent]] §3.1)이고, team은 장기 협응(Claude Code teammate 모델 — <code>name</code>+<code>team_name</code>, mailbox 메시징, [[05_cc_subagent]] §7)이다. OMC는 전자에 <strong>역할/모델/권한 특화</strong>를, 후자에 <strong>물리적 격리·머지·생존성·5단계 파이프라인</strong>을 더한다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>2. 19개 서브에이전트 카탈로그 (<code>agents/*.md</code>)</h2>
<p>각 에이전트는 <code>agents/&#x3C;name>.md</code>의 YAML frontmatter(<code>name</code>, <code>description</code>, <code>model</code>, <code>level</code>, 선택적 <code>disallowedTools</code>)와 <code>&#x3C;Agent_Prompt></code> 본문으로 정의된다. Claude Code의 서브에이전트 정의 스키마([[05_cc_subagent]] §10)의 사용자 정의 에이전트(<code>.claude/agents/*.md</code>, [[05_cc_subagent]] §2 말미)에 해당하며, 호스트가 자동 발견해 <code>Agent</code> 도구의 <code>subagent_type</code>(<code>oh-my-claudecode:&#x3C;name></code>)으로 노출한다.</p>
<table>

</tr>
