<p>Extension aliases (<code>tsx</code>/<code>jsx</code>→<code>typescript</code>, <code>cpp</code>→<code>c</code>, <code>kt</code>/<code>kts</code>→<code>kotlin</code>, <code>exs</code>/<code>heex</code>→<code>elixir</code>, <code>rb</code>/<code>erb</code>→<code>ruby</code>, etc.) are normalized into separate alias maps. Since <code>commandExists()</code> checks for binary existence, language servers that are not installed are quietly skipped. <code>standalone-server.ts</code> cleans up LSP child processes (e.g. <code>jdtls</code>) with <code>disconnectAll()</code> on exit (#768 — Prevent orphan process leaks).</p>
<h3>2.3 AST Tools (<code>src/tools/ast-tools.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>astTools = [astGrepSearchTool, astGrepReplaceTool]</code> — These are two tools:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong><code>ast_grep_search</code></strong>: ast-grep(<code>@ast-grep/napi</code>) syntax tree pattern search. Example) Find structural patterns such as “all async functions without try/catch”.</li>
<li><strong><code>ast_grep_replace</code></strong>: Structure-preserving replacement.</li>
</ul>
<p>Without the native module (<code>@ast-grep/napi</code>), the tool <strong>exists</strong> in the registry but performs graceful degradation, returning a help error when called (<code>tool-registry.ts</code> comment). <code>python_repl</code> (<code>src/tools/python-repl/</code>) is the data analysis execution backend of the <code>scientist</code> agent and operates through a separate bridge process.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. <code>team</code> MCP server — tmux + worktree worker runtime</h2>
<h3>3.1 4 tools and dynamic startup</h3>
<p>The <code>team</code> server (<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>, source <code>src/mcp/team-server.ts</code>) is defined as <code>{ name: 'team', version: '1.0.0' }</code> and exposes only 4 tools:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>tools</th>
<th>CLI response</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_start</code></td>
<td><code>omc team start</code></td>
<td>Start worker job → return <code>jobId</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_status</code></td>
<td><code>omc team status &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>Job Status Polling</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_wait</code></td>
<td><code>omc team wait &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>wait for completion (<code>timeout_ms</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_cleanup</code></td>
<td><code>omc team cleanup &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>Force quit Walker</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><code>handleStart</code> <code>spawns</code> <code>runtime-cli.cjs</code> (team worker runtime bootstrap) to launch a worker as a child process, and identifies the job with <code>OMC_JOB_ID</code>/<code>OMC_JOBS_DIR</code> environment variables. <strong>start immediately returns <code>jobId</code> and workers continue to run in the background</strong> — The schema annotation <code>omc_run_team_start no longer accepts timeoutSeconds ... workers keep running until completion or explicit omc_run_team_cleanup</code> enforces this non-blocking polling model. This is the runtime layer that supports the team pipeline (team-plan→team-exec→team-verify→team-fix) of [[00_omc_overview]] §6.</p>
<h3>3.2 Why not static registration — migrating to CLI-only</h3>
<p><code>.mcp.json</code> does not register <code>team</code>. The reason is beyond simple separation, because <strong>legacy MCP registrations are being intentionally retired</strong>. There is an explicit pattern in <code>src/installer/mcp-registry.ts</code>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">const RETIRED_TEAM_MCP_PATH_PATTERN = /(^|[\\/])bridge[\\/]+team-mcp\.cjs$/i;

function isRetiredTeamMcpEntry(value: unknown): boolean { ... }  // args가 team-mcp.cjs를 가리키면 true

export function stripRetiredTeamMcpServers(settings) {
  // settings.mcpServers에서 team-mcp.cjs 항목을 제거(changed=true)
}
</code></pre>
<p><code>normalizeRegistry()</code> also returns <code>null</code> if <code>isRetiredTeamMcpEntry</code> and is removed from registration. And the dispatch handler in <code>team-server.ts</code> sends this message with <code>DEPRECATION_CODE = 'deprecated_cli_only'</code>:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Legacy team MCP runtime tools are deprecated. Use the omc team CLI instead.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>In other words, the direction of OMC is to statically inject <strong><code>t</code> into the plugin manifest (permanent), and to remove MCP registration for <code>team</code> and call it with the <code>omc team</code> CLI</strong>. This leaves only the code intel/state tools on the surface of the MCP tool, and isolates the heavy worker orchestration outside the CLI process boundaries.</p>
<h3>3.3 Worker isolation (worktree)</h3>
<p>Worker file conflicts are blocked by <code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code> with git worktree (path <code>{repoRoot}/.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}</code>, branch <code>omc-team/{team}/{worker}</code>). The <code>team</code> server tool manages the job lifetime, and the actual workers run as claude/codex/gemini CLI processes in a tmux session (<code>omc-team-{team}-{worker}</code>) ([[00_omc_overview]] §6.2). Job state is persisted in <code>OMC_JOBS_DIR</code> (default global <code>.omc/state/team-jobs</code>).</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> — Block remote MCP/external LLM</h2>
<p>OMC gates MCP/external model usage with a security policy. The source of truth is <code>src/lib/security-config.ts</code>.</p>
<h3>4.1 7 security flags and strict overrides</h3>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">export interface SecurityConfig {
  restrictToolPaths: boolean;     // ast_grep_* 경로를 프로젝트 루트로 제한
  pythonSandbox: boolean;         // python_repl 샌드박스(모듈/builtin 차단)
  disableProjectSkills: boolean;  // .omc/skills/ 로딩 차단
  disableAutoUpdate: boolean;     // 무음 자동 업데이트 차단
  hardMaxIterations: number;      // 영속 루프 하드 상한(0=무제한)
  disableRemoteMcp: boolean;      // 원격 MCP 서버(Exa, Context7) 차단
  disableExternalLLM: boolean;    // 외부 LLM(Codex, Gemini) 차단 (team mode)
}
</code></pre>
<p><code>DEFAULTS</code> is all off (<code>hardMaxIterations: 500</code>), but if <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>, <code>STRICT_OVERRIDES</code> is applied:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">const STRICT_OVERRIDES: SecurityConfig = {
  restrictToolPaths: true,
  pythonSandbox: true,
  disableProjectSkills: true,
  disableAutoUpdate: true,
  hardMaxIterations: 200,         // 500 → 200으로 축소
  disableRemoteMcp: true,         // ← 원격 MCP 차단
  disableExternalLLM: true,       // ← 외부 LLM 차단
};
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Priority is <code>config file > OMC_SECURITY env > defaults</code></strong>. However, in strict mode, the configuration file can <strong>only strengthen</strong> security and not relax it (ORed with <code>base || fileOverride</code>, <code>hardMaxIterations</code> is <code>Math.min</code>). This means that if an organization sets <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> as an environment variable, project <code>.claude/omc.jsonc</code> will not be able to bypass it — a policy lever that can be enforced on employee devices.</p>
<h3>4.2 Remote MCP/external LLM blocking point</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Remote MCP</strong>(<code>createExaServer</code>/<code>createContext7Server</code>/<code>createPlaywrightServer</code>, <code>src/mcp/servers.ts</code>): <code>getDefaultMcpServers()</code> sets Exa(<code>exa-mcp-server</code>)·Context7(<code>@upstash/context7-mcp</code>) with <code>enableExa</code>/<code>enableContext7</code> options. Launch it with stdio. <code>disableRemoteMcp</code>/<code>isRemoteMcpDisabled()</code> is the flag to turn it off. Unlike <code>t</code>/<code>team</code>, these remote servers are separate paths assembled in code (<code>src/index.ts</code>), not in the <strong>plugin manifest</strong>.</li>
<li><strong>External LLM</strong> (Codex/Gemini): Mandatory in two places.
<ul>
<li><code>src/cli/ask.ts:215</code> — Error if <code>provider !== 'claude' &#x26;&#x26; isExternalLLMDisabled()</code> when calling <code>omc ask &#x3C;codex|gemini></code>:</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<pre><code>    [ask] External LLM provider "codex" is blocked by security policy (disableExternalLLM).
    Only "claude" is allowed in the current security configuration.
    ```
- `src/team/model-contract.ts:267` — `getContract()` blocks equally if the team worker type is not claude but `isExternalLLMDisabled()`. In other words, `/team N:codex`/`N:gemini` worker spawning is blocked.

As a result, strict mode **cuts off all external dependencies of OMC (remote MCP API, non-Claude CLI model)** and ensures Claude's independent operation in a closed network/regulated environment. AST path restrictions (`validateToolPath`, `src/tools/ast-tools.ts`) and python sandbox (`OMC_PYTHON_SANDBOX=1` in `bridge-manager.ts`) are also turned on simultaneously with the same switch.

---

## 5. Architecture


```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Host["Claude Code 코어 (호스트) — [[09_cc_mcp]]"]
        Merge["설정 병합 (plugin manifest 포함 6소스)"]
        Surface["도구 표면 mcp__server__tool"]
    end

    subgraph Manifest["OMC 플러그인 매니페스트"]
        Plugin[".claude-plugin/plugin.json&#x3C;br/>mcpServers → .mcp.json"]
        McpJson[".mcp.json&#x3C;br/>서버 t = node bridge/mcp-server.cjs"]
    end

    subgraph TServer["t MCP 서버 (정적 등록, stdio)"]
        LSP["LSP 12도구 / 20 언어서버"]
        AST["ast_grep_search/replace"]
        Py["python_repl"]
        State["state/notepad/project-memory/shared-memory"]
        Wiki["wiki_* / skills_* / trace_* / deepinit"]
    end

    subgraph TeamServer["team MCP 서버 (CLI-only, 동적)"]
        TeamTools["omc_run_team_start/status/wait/cleanup"]
        Runtime["runtime-cli.cjs → tmux + worktree 워커"]
    end

    subgraph Remote["원격/외부 (코드 경로, OMC_SECURITY로 차단)"]
        Exa["Exa / Context7 / Playwright MCP"]
        Ext["Codex / Gemini CLI"]
    end

    Plugin --> McpJson
    McpJson -->|정적 주입| Merge
    Merge --> Surface
    Surface -->|mcp__t__*| TServer
    OmcCli["omc team CLI"] -->|동적 spawn| TeamServer
    TeamTools --> Runtime
    Sec["OMC_SECURITY=strict&#x3C;br/>(src/lib/security-config.ts)"] -.->|disableRemoteMcp| Exa
    Sec -.->|disableExternalLLM| Ext
    Sec -.->|restrictToolPaths/pythonSandbox| AST
    Sec -.->|hardMaxIterations 200| Runtime
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>6. Data flow: <code>t</code> tool injection at session start</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant Inst as 플러그인 설치
    participant CC as Claude Code 코어
    participant Mcp as .mcp.json
    participant T as t 서버 (bridge/mcp-server.cjs)
    participant Reg as tool-registry.ts
    participant Surf as 도구 표면

    Inst->>CC: plugin.json mcpServers 등록
    CC->>Mcp: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} 치환
    CC->>T: stdio spawn (node mcp-server.cjs)
    T->>T: new Server({name:'t'})
    CC->>T: initialize
    T-->>CC: capabilities {tools:{}}
    CC->>T: tools/list
    T->>Reg: buildListToolsResponse()
    Reg-->>T: lsp(12)+ast(2)+python+state+notepad+memory+wiki+skills+trace+deepinit
    T-->>CC: ListTools (Zod→JSON Schema)
    CC->>Surf: mcp__t__lsp_diagnostics, mcp__t__ast_grep_search, ...
    Note over Surf: 모든 세션이 동일한 코드인텔/상태 도구 표면 획득
</code></pre>
<p>The team server is not in this flow — the host does not spawn it, but directly spawns <code>runtime-cli.cjs</code> when the <code>omc team</code> CLI is needed.</p>
<hr>

<td><code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code></td>

<td>4</td>

<hr>
<h2>8. Limits and judgment</h2>
<h3>Limits</h3>
<ol>
<li>Since <code>bridge/*.cjs</code> is a bundle output, line-by-line quotation is based on the source (<code>src/</code>). 1:1 correspondence between bundle and source is confirmed with build scripts (<code>scripts/build-team-server.mjs</code>, etc.).</li>
<li>Decommissioning of the <code>team</code> server is in progress — the handler is alive in <code>team-server.ts</code> (<code>handleStart</code> actually spawns the worker), and <code>installer/mcp-registry.ts</code> is clearing the registration. These two facts point to a transitional phase of "runtime works, but MCP registration is removed".</li>
<li>The remote MCP (Exa/Context7) assembly path (<code>src/index.ts</code>) is OMC's SDK point-of-use code (<code>omc</code> CLI runs itself), a separate surface from <code>t</code>/<code>team</code> injection as a plugin.</li>
</ol>
<h3>Judgment</h3>
<p>OMC treats MCP as <strong>plugin manifest based central injection + security policy gate</strong>.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Central injection</strong>: <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code> → <code>.mcp.json</code> → <code>t</code> With a single server registration, all sessions on the host automatically acquire the same 48 code intel/status tools (only the plugin manifest path is used among the 6 source merges of [[09_cc_mcp]] — simple and uniform).</li>
<li><strong>Dual server separation</strong>: Explicitly separate <code>t</code> (static, lightweight intel/state) vs <code>team</code> (dynamic/CLI-only, heavy worker runtime).</li>
<li><strong>Semantic-based code intel</strong>: LSP 12 tools + 20 language servers + AST grep exposed to MCP — complements vanilla text grep (unlike [[09_cc_mcp]]'s DiagnosticsManager which reads an external LSP, OMC embeds the LSP in the server).</li>
<li><strong>Policy Gate</strong>: <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> blocks remote MCP, external LLM, path, and iteration with one switch, and configuration files can only be strengthened.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Reuse perspective</h2>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li>Uniformization of all session tools with single registration of [x] plugin manifest — a powerful lever for organizational deployment</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> See MCP Server Design for <code>t</code>/<code>team</code> tool surface separation (light static vs heavy dynamic).</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Pattern that adds semantic search by embedding LSP/AST in the MCP server</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> "config only hardens, not relaxes" in <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> See enforcement policy design for priorities.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The timing of completion of CLI-only migration of <code>team</code> MCP is outside the scope of this analysis.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>cli-jaw comparison implications</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Uniform MCP tool surface</strong>: OMC automatically injects <code>t</code> server 48 tools (LSP/AST/python/wiki/state) into all sessions with a single line <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code> → <code>.mcp.json</code>. While cli-jaw lazy-loads tools (<code>ToolSearch</code>) and has different MCP/skill exposure for each employee, the OMC's plugin manifest injection is <strong>"the lever that ensures that all employees have the same code intelligence baseline"</strong> — a model that cli-jaw can directly borrow when forcing a uniform toolset on employee devices.</li>
<li><strong>Compensation for the absence of semantic-based code search</strong>: OMC's LSP 12 tools + 20 language servers + <code>ast_grep_*</code> makes up for the "definition/reference/type diagnosis/structure substitution" where cli-jaw's grep/browser-centered search is weak. The method of condensing (<code>t</code>) these into a single MCP server and registering them at once is the blueprint for cli-jaw when introducing code intel.</li>
<li><strong>Policy kill switch (<code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>)</strong>: Simultaneously locks remote MCP (Exa/Context7), external LLM (Codex/Gemini), path, python sandbox, and iteration upper limit with one environment variable, and only allows strengthening of project settings. When cli-jaw blocks and forces employees to use external models/remote MCPs through organizational policy, it provides an accurate safety model of "env > config first, no mitigation possible."</li>
<li><strong>Separation of heavy worker runtime</strong>: OMC splits code intel (<code>t</code>, static/lightweight) and worker orchestration (<code>team</code>, dynamic/CLI-only) into different injection paths, keeping the MCP tool surface light and isolating heavy process spawns outside the CLI boundary. This is a reference pattern when cli-jaw is designed to separate employee dispatch (heavy) and everyday tools (light) from being mixed on the same surface.</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>009_mcp-orchestration/09_omc_mcp.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) MCP 오케스트레이션</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — MCP orchestration.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>oh-my-claudecode(OMC)는 Claude Code 위에 얹히는 플러그인이므로, MCP에 관한 한 [[09_cc_mcp]]에서 분석한 호스트의 4층 구조(설정 병합 → 연결 → 도구 노출 → 리소스)를 <strong>그대로 빌린다</strong>. OMC가 추가하는 것은 두 개의 자체 MCP 서버를 플러그인 매니페스트로 <strong>중앙 주입</strong>한다는 점이다. 첫째는 <code>t</code> 서버로, 상태/노트패드/프로젝트메모리/공유메모리/세션이력/LSP/AST/python_repl/wiki/skills/trace/deepinit 도구를 단일 stdio 서버에 응집한다. 둘째는 <code>team</code> 서버로, tmux 패널 + git worktree 기반 외부 CLI 워커(claude/codex/gemini)를 띄운다.</p>
<p>핵심은 <strong>주입 경로의 차이</strong>다. <code>t</code> 서버는 <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code>의 <code>mcpServers</code> 키 → <code>.mcp.json</code> → stdio <code>node bridge/mcp-server.cjs</code>로 <strong>플러그인 설치 즉시 정적 등록</strong>된다. <code>team</code> 서버(<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>)는 정적 등록되지 않고 런타임에서 <code>omc team</code> CLI가 동적으로 기동한다. 즉 OMC는 "모든 세션이 동일한 코드 인텔리전스/상태 도구 표면을 갖도록" 플러그인 매니페스트라는 단 하나의 레버로 MCP를 호스트에 주입한다. 이는 바닐라 Claude Code가 사용자별 <code>.mcp.json</code>/CLI/claude.ai 설정에 의존해 서버를 병합하는 것([[09_cc_mcp]] §6)과 대비되는, <strong>조직 차원의 균일성</strong> 설계다.</p>
<p>분석 기준 버전은 <code>plugin.json</code> v4.14.4다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>1. 플러그인 매니페스트를 통한 중앙 MCP 주입</h2>
<h3>1.1 <code>.mcp.json</code> — <code>t</code> 서버 단일 정적 등록</h3>
<p><code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code>은 <code>"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json"</code>로 MCP 서버 설정 파일을 가리킨다. Claude Code는 플러그인 manifest의 <code>mcpServers</code>를 6개 설정 소스 중 하나로 병합한다([[09_cc_mcp]] §6, plugin manifest 항목). <code>.mcp.json</code> 전문은 다음과 같다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "mcpServers": {
    "t": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bridge/mcp-server.cjs"]
    }
  }
}
</code></pre>
<p>여기서 확인되는 설계 결정:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>서버 이름이 <code>t</code></strong> — 한 글자. <code>mcp__t__lsp_diagnostics</code>처럼 도구 namespace가 짧아져 프롬프트 토큰을 아낀다([[09_cc_mcp]]의 <code>mcp__server__tool</code> namespace 조합기가 그대로 적용).</li>
<li><strong>transport는 stdio 하나뿐</strong> — <code>${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}</code>(Claude Code가 플러그인 루트로 치환하는 환경변수)로 절대 경로를 만들어 <code>bridge/mcp-server.cjs</code>를 자식 프로세스로 띄운다.</li>
<li><strong>단 하나의 서버만 등록</strong> — LSP·AST·python·wiki·state를 별도 서버로 흩지 않고 <code>t</code> 하나에 응집. Claude Code는 health check 시 이 서버를 spawn하고 <code>tools/list</code>로 전체 도구를 받아 <code>mcp__t__*</code>로 도구 표면에 병합한다.</li>
</ul>
<p><code>run-mcp-server.sh</code>는 native 모듈(<code>@ast-grep/napi</code>) 해결을 위해 <code>NODE_PATH</code>에 글로벌 npm root를 추가하는 래퍼지만, <code>.mcp.json</code>은 이를 거치지 않고 <code>node</code>를 직접 호출한다(AST 도구는 native 모듈 부재 시 런타임에 graceful degrade — <code>tool-registry.ts</code> 주석).</p>
<h3>1.2 <code>t</code> 서버 부트스트랩 (<code>bridge/mcp-server.cjs</code>)</h3>
<p><code>bridge/mcp-server.cjs</code>는 <code>src/mcp/standalone-server.ts</code>를 esbuild로 번들한 산출물(28,000줄+)이다. 서버 정체성은 소스에 명시된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">// src/mcp/standalone-server.ts
const server = new Server(
  { name: 't', version: '1.0.0' },
  { capabilities: { tools: {} } }
);
server.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => buildListToolsResponse());
</code></pre>
<p>도구 표면은 <code>src/mcp/tool-registry.ts</code>가 단일 진실원천(single source of truth)으로 모은다. 등록 순서대로:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">export const allTools: ToolDef[] = [
  ...lspTools,            // 12 LSP 도구
  ...astTools,            // ast_grep_search, ast_grep_replace
  pythonReplTool,         // python_repl
  ...stateTools,          // state_read/write/clear/list_active/get_status
  ...notepadTools,        // notepad_*
  ...memoryTools,         // project_memory_*
  ...traceTools,          // trace_summary/timeline
  ...sharedMemoryTools,   // shared_memory_*
  deepinitManifestTool,   // deepinit_manifest
  ...wikiTools,           // wiki_*
  ...skillsTools,         // list_omc_skills, load_omc_skills_*
];
</code></pre>
<p>주목할 점은 <code>tool-registry.ts</code>가 명시적으로 남긴 주석이다:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Team runtime tools (omc_run_team_start, omc_run_team_status) are intentionally excluded: they live in the separate "team" MCP server (bridge/team-mcp.cjs).</p>
</blockquote>
<p>즉 OMC는 도구 표면을 <strong><code>t</code>(코드 인텔/상태) vs <code>team</code>(워커 런타임)</strong> 두 서버로 의도적으로 분리했다. <code>t</code>는 정적 등록, <code>team</code>은 동적 기동이라는 주입 경로 차이가 여기서 비롯된다.</p>
<h3>1.3 Zod → JSON Schema 변환 (wire 포맷)</h3>
<p><code>t</code> 서버는 도구 스키마를 Zod로 정의하고, <code>tool-registry.ts</code>의 <code>zodToJsonSchema()</code>가 MCP wire 포맷(<code>{ type: 'object', properties, required }</code>)으로 변환한다. <code>ZodOptional</code>/<code>ZodDefault</code>/<code>ZodEnum</code>/<code>ZodArray</code>/<code>ZodRecord</code>를 재귀 처리하며, <code>isOptional()</code>이 false인 필드만 <code>required</code>에 넣는다. <code>buildListToolsResponse()</code>가 라이브 서버와 테스트가 <strong>동일 코드 경로</strong>를 타도록 설계되어, <code>ListToolsRequestSchema</code> 핸들러가 이 함수를 직접 호출한다.</p>
<p>각 도구는 <code>annotations</code>로 <code>readOnlyHint</code>/<code>destructiveHint</code>/<code>idempotentHint</code>/<code>openWorldHint</code>를 선언할 수 있다 — 이는 [[09_cc_mcp]]의 <code>isReadOnly</code>/<code>isConcurrencySafe</code> 플래그에 대응하는, MCP 표준 annotation 채널이다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>2. 코드 인텔리전스: LSP + AST를 MCP로 노출</h2>
<p>바닐라 Claude Code는 <code>Grep</code>(ripgrep)/<code>Glob</code>로 <strong>텍스트 매칭</strong> 검색만 한다([[09_cc_mcp]]에는 검색 도구가 별도로 없고, 호스트의 built-in 도구를 씀). OMC의 <code>t</code> 서버는 그 위에 <strong>의미 기반(semantic)</strong> 코드 도구를 MCP로 얹는다. 흥미롭게도 이는 [[09_cc_mcp]] §5의 <code>DiagnosticsManager</code>(Claude Code가 MCP를 IDE 진단 브릿지로 쓰는 패턴)와 방향이 같다 — 다만 Claude Code는 외부 IDE의 LSP를 MCP로 <strong>읽어오고</strong>, OMC는 자체 LSP 클라이언트를 MCP 서버 안에 <strong>내장</strong>한다.</p>
<h3>2.1 12개 LSP 도구 (<code>src/tools/lsp-tools.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>lspTools</code> 배열은 정확히 12개 도구를 노출한다:</p>
<table>

</tr>
<tr>

<td>정의로 점프</td>

<p><code>lsp_diagnostics</code>/<code>lsp_diagnostics_directory</code>는 [[00_omc_overview]] §8.1.1에서 본 대로 <code>verifier</code> 에이전트가 "신선한 증거"로, <code>architect</code>/<code>debugger</code>가 <code>&#x3C;Tool_Usage></code>에서 명시적으로 호출하는 핵심 도구다. 텍스트 grep으로는 잡을 수 없는 타입 오류·심볼 관계를 정확히 다룬다.</p>
<h3>2.2 언어서버 매핑 (<code>src/tools/lsp/servers.ts</code>)</h3>

<p>확장자 별칭(<code>tsx</code>/<code>jsx</code>→<code>typescript</code>, <code>cpp</code>→<code>c</code>, <code>kt</code>/<code>kts</code>→<code>kotlin</code>, <code>exs</code>/<code>heex</code>→<code>elixir</code>, <code>rb</code>/<code>erb</code>→<code>ruby</code> 등)은 별도 alias 맵으로 정규화된다. <code>commandExists()</code>가 바이너리 존재를 검사하므로, 설치되지 않은 언어서버는 조용히 건너뛴다. <code>standalone-server.ts</code>는 종료 시 <code>disconnectAll()</code>로 LSP 자식 프로세스(예: <code>jdtls</code>)를 정리한다(#768 — 고아 프로세스 누수 방지).</p>
<h3>2.3 AST 도구 (<code>src/tools/ast-tools.ts</code>)</h3>
<p><code>astTools = [astGrepSearchTool, astGrepReplaceTool]</code> — 두 도구다:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong><code>ast_grep_search</code></strong>: ast-grep(<code>@ast-grep/napi</code>) 구문트리 패턴 검색. 예) "try/catch 없는 모든 async 함수"처럼 구조적 패턴을 찾는다.</li>
<li><strong><code>ast_grep_replace</code></strong>: 구조 보존 치환.</li>
</ul>
<p>native 모듈(<code>@ast-grep/napi</code>)이 없으면 도구는 레지스트리에 <strong>존재는 하되</strong> 호출 시 도움말 에러를 반환하는 graceful degradation을 한다(<code>tool-registry.ts</code> 주석). <code>python_repl</code>(<code>src/tools/python-repl/</code>)은 <code>scientist</code> 에이전트의 데이터 분석 실행 백엔드로, 별도 bridge 프로세스를 통해 동작한다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. <code>team</code> MCP 서버 — tmux + worktree 워커 런타임</h2>
<h3>3.1 4개 도구와 동적 기동</h3>
<p><code>team</code> 서버(<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>, 소스 <code>src/mcp/team-server.ts</code>)는 <code>{ name: 'team', version: '1.0.0' }</code>로 정의되며 4개 도구만 노출한다:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>도구</th>
<th>CLI 대응</th>
<th>역할</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_start</code></td>
<td><code>omc team start</code></td>
<td>워커 잡 기동 → <code>jobId</code> 반환</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_status</code></td>
<td><code>omc team status &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>잡 상태 폴링</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_wait</code></td>
<td><code>omc team wait &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>완료 대기(<code>timeout_ms</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>omc_run_team_cleanup</code></td>
<td><code>omc team cleanup &#x3C;job_id></code></td>
<td>워커 강제 종료</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><code>handleStart</code>는 <code>runtime-cli.cjs</code>(team 워커 런타임 부트스트랩)를 <code>spawn</code>해 자식 프로세스로 워커를 띄우고, <code>OMC_JOB_ID</code>/<code>OMC_JOBS_DIR</code> 환경변수로 잡을 식별한다. <strong>start는 즉시 <code>jobId</code>를 반환하고 워커는 백그라운드로 계속 돈다</strong> — <code>omc_run_team_start no longer accepts timeoutSeconds ... workers keep running until completion or explicit omc_run_team_cleanup</code>라는 스키마 주석이 이 비차단(non-blocking) 폴링 모델을 강제한다. 이는 [[00_omc_overview]] §6의 team 파이프라인(team-plan→team-exec→team-verify→team-fix)을 받치는 런타임 계층이다.</p>
<h3>3.2 정적 등록이 아닌 이유 — CLI-only로 마이그레이션 중</h3>
<p><code>.mcp.json</code>은 <code>team</code>을 등록하지 않는다. 그 이유는 단순한 분리를 넘어, <strong>레거시 MCP 등록을 의도적으로 폐기(retire)하는 중</strong>이기 때문이다. <code>src/installer/mcp-registry.ts</code>에 명시적 패턴이 있다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">const RETIRED_TEAM_MCP_PATH_PATTERN = /(^|[\\/])bridge[\\/]+team-mcp\.cjs$/i;

function isRetiredTeamMcpEntry(value: unknown): boolean { ... }  // args가 team-mcp.cjs를 가리키면 true

export function stripRetiredTeamMcpServers(settings) {
  // settings.mcpServers에서 team-mcp.cjs 항목을 제거(changed=true)
}
</code></pre>
<p><code>normalizeRegistry()</code>도 <code>isRetiredTeamMcpEntry</code>면 <code>null</code>을 반환해 등록에서 빼버린다. 그리고 <code>team-server.ts</code>의 디스패치 핸들러는 <code>DEPRECATION_CODE = 'deprecated_cli_only'</code>와 함께 이렇게 안내한다:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Legacy team MCP runtime tools are deprecated. Use the omc team CLI instead.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>즉 OMC의 방향성은 <strong><code>t</code>는 플러그인 매니페스트로 정적 주입(영구), <code>team</code>은 MCP 등록을 걷어내고 <code>omc team</code> CLI로 호출</strong>하는 것이다. 이로써 MCP 도구 표면에는 코드 인텔/상태 도구만 남고, 무거운 워커 오케스트레이션은 CLI 프로세스 경계 밖으로 격리된다.</p>
<h3>3.3 워커 격리 (worktree)</h3>
<p>워커 파일 충돌은 <code>src/team/git-worktree.ts</code>가 git worktree로 차단한다(경로 <code>{repoRoot}/.omc/team/{team}/worktrees/{worker}</code>, 브랜치 <code>omc-team/{team}/{worker}</code>). <code>team</code> 서버 도구는 잡 수명을 관리하고, 실제 워커는 tmux 세션(<code>omc-team-{team}-{worker}</code>)에서 claude/codex/gemini CLI 프로세스로 돈다([[00_omc_overview]] §6.2). 잡 상태는 <code>OMC_JOBS_DIR</code>(기본 글로벌 <code>.omc/state/team-jobs</code>)에 영속된다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> — 원격 MCP·외부 LLM 차단</h2>
<p>OMC는 MCP/외부 모델 사용을 <strong>보안 정책으로 게이트</strong>한다. 진실원천은 <code>src/lib/security-config.ts</code>다.</p>
<h3>4.1 7개 보안 플래그와 strict 오버라이드</h3>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">export interface SecurityConfig {
  restrictToolPaths: boolean;     // ast_grep_* 경로를 프로젝트 루트로 제한
  pythonSandbox: boolean;         // python_repl 샌드박스(모듈/builtin 차단)
  disableProjectSkills: boolean;  // .omc/skills/ 로딩 차단
  disableAutoUpdate: boolean;     // 무음 자동 업데이트 차단
  hardMaxIterations: number;      // 영속 루프 하드 상한(0=무제한)
  disableRemoteMcp: boolean;      // 원격 MCP 서버(Exa, Context7) 차단
  disableExternalLLM: boolean;    // 외부 LLM(Codex, Gemini) 차단 (team mode)
}
</code></pre>
<p><code>DEFAULTS</code>는 전부 off(<code>hardMaxIterations: 500</code>)지만, <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>면 <code>STRICT_OVERRIDES</code>가 적용된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-typescript">const STRICT_OVERRIDES: SecurityConfig = {
  restrictToolPaths: true,
  pythonSandbox: true,
  disableProjectSkills: true,
  disableAutoUpdate: true,
  hardMaxIterations: 200,         // 500 → 200으로 축소
  disableRemoteMcp: true,         // ← 원격 MCP 차단
  disableExternalLLM: true,       // ← 외부 LLM 차단
};
</code></pre>
<p>**우선순위는 <code>config file > OMC_SECURITY env > defaults</code>**다. 단 strict 모드에서 설정 파일은 보안을 <strong>강화만</strong> 할 수 있고 완화는 불가하다(<code>base || fileOverride</code>로 OR 결합, <code>hardMaxIterations</code>는 <code>Math.min</code>). 이는 조직이 <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>를 환경변수로 박으면 프로젝트 <code>.claude/omc.jsonc</code>가 이를 뚫지 못한다는 의미다 — 직원 단말에 강제 가능한 정책 레버.</p>
<h3>4.2 원격 MCP / 외부 LLM 차단 지점</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>원격 MCP</strong>(<code>createExaServer</code>/<code>createContext7Server</code>/<code>createPlaywrightServer</code>, <code>src/mcp/servers.ts</code>): <code>getDefaultMcpServers()</code>가 <code>enableExa</code>/<code>enableContext7</code> 옵션으로 Exa(<code>exa-mcp-server</code>)·Context7(<code>@upstash/context7-mcp</code>)를 stdio로 띄운다. <code>disableRemoteMcp</code>/<code>isRemoteMcpDisabled()</code>가 이를 끄는 플래그다. 이 원격 서버들은 <code>t</code>/<code>team</code>과 달리 <strong>plugin manifest가 아닌</strong> 코드(<code>src/index.ts</code>)에서 조립되는 별도 경로다.</li>
<li><strong>외부 LLM</strong>(Codex/Gemini): 두 곳에서 강제된다.
<ul>
<li><code>src/cli/ask.ts:215</code> — <code>omc ask &#x3C;codex|gemini></code> 호출 시 <code>provider !== 'claude' &#x26;&#x26; isExternalLLMDisabled()</code>면 에러:
<pre><code>[ask] External LLM provider "codex" is blocked by security policy (disableExternalLLM).
Only "claude" is allowed in the current security configuration.
</code></pre>
</li>
<li><code>src/team/model-contract.ts:267</code> — <code>getContract()</code>가 team 워커 타입이 claude가 아니고 <code>isExternalLLMDisabled()</code>면 동일하게 차단. 즉 <code>/team N:codex</code>/<code>N:gemini</code> 워커 스폰이 봉쇄된다.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p>이로써 strict 모드는 <strong>OMC의 외부 의존(원격 MCP API, 비-Claude CLI 모델)을 모두 끊어</strong> 폐쇄망/규제 환경에서 Claude 단독 동작을 보장한다. AST 경로 제한(<code>validateToolPath</code>, <code>src/tools/ast-tools.ts</code>)과 python 샌드박스(<code>bridge-manager.ts</code>의 <code>OMC_PYTHON_SANDBOX=1</code>)도 같은 스위치로 동시에 켜진다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. 아키텍처</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph Host["Claude Code 코어 (호스트) — [[09_cc_mcp]]"]
        Merge["설정 병합 (plugin manifest 포함 6소스)"]
        Surface["도구 표면 mcp__server__tool"]
    end

    subgraph Manifest["OMC 플러그인 매니페스트"]
        Plugin[".claude-plugin/plugin.json&#x3C;br/>mcpServers → .mcp.json"]
        McpJson[".mcp.json&#x3C;br/>서버 t = node bridge/mcp-server.cjs"]
    end

    subgraph TServer["t MCP 서버 (정적 등록, stdio)"]
        LSP["LSP 12도구 / 20 언어서버"]
        AST["ast_grep_search/replace"]
        Py["python_repl"]
        State["state/notepad/project-memory/shared-memory"]
        Wiki["wiki_* / skills_* / trace_* / deepinit"]
    end

    subgraph TeamServer["team MCP 서버 (CLI-only, 동적)"]
        TeamTools["omc_run_team_start/status/wait/cleanup"]
        Runtime["runtime-cli.cjs → tmux + worktree 워커"]
    end

    subgraph Remote["원격/외부 (코드 경로, OMC_SECURITY로 차단)"]
        Exa["Exa / Context7 / Playwright MCP"]
        Ext["Codex / Gemini CLI"]
    end

    Plugin --> McpJson
    McpJson -->|정적 주입| Merge
    Merge --> Surface
    Surface -->|mcp__t__*| TServer
    OmcCli["omc team CLI"] -->|동적 spawn| TeamServer
    TeamTools --> Runtime
    Sec["OMC_SECURITY=strict&#x3C;br/>(src/lib/security-config.ts)"] -.->|disableRemoteMcp| Exa
    Sec -.->|disableExternalLLM| Ext
    Sec -.->|restrictToolPaths/pythonSandbox| AST
    Sec -.->|hardMaxIterations 200| Runtime
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>6. 데이터 흐름: 세션 시작 시 <code>t</code> 도구 주입</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant Inst as 플러그인 설치
    participant CC as Claude Code 코어
    participant Mcp as .mcp.json
    participant T as t 서버 (bridge/mcp-server.cjs)
    participant Reg as tool-registry.ts
    participant Surf as 도구 표면

    Inst->>CC: plugin.json mcpServers 등록
    CC->>Mcp: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} 치환
    CC->>T: stdio spawn (node mcp-server.cjs)
    T->>T: new Server({name:'t'})
    CC->>T: initialize
    T-->>CC: capabilities {tools:{}}
    CC->>T: tools/list
    T->>Reg: buildListToolsResponse()
    Reg-->>T: lsp(12)+ast(2)+python+state+notepad+memory+wiki+skills+trace+deepinit
    T-->>CC: ListTools (Zod→JSON Schema)
    CC->>Surf: mcp__t__lsp_diagnostics, mcp__t__ast_grep_search, ...
    Note over Surf: 모든 세션이 동일한 코드인텔/상태 도구 표면 획득
</code></pre>
<p>team 서버는 이 흐름에 없다 — 호스트가 spawn하지 않고, <code>omc team</code> CLI가 필요할 때 <code>runtime-cli.cjs</code>를 직접 띄운다.</p>
<hr>

<hr>
<h2>8. 한계와 판정</h2>
<h3>한계</h3>
<ol>
<li><code>bridge/*.cjs</code>는 번들 산출물이라 줄 단위 인용은 소스(<code>src/</code>)를 기준으로 했다. 번들과 소스의 1:1 대응은 빌드 스크립트(<code>scripts/build-team-server.mjs</code> 등)로 확인된다.</li>
<li><code>team</code> 서버의 폐기는 진행 중 상태다 — <code>team-server.ts</code>에 핸들러가 살아 있고(<code>handleStart</code>가 실제로 워커를 spawn), <code>installer/mcp-registry.ts</code>가 등록을 걷어낸다. 두 사실은 "런타임은 동작하되 MCP 등록은 제거"라는 과도기를 가리킨다.</li>
<li>원격 MCP(Exa/Context7) 조립 경로(<code>src/index.ts</code>)는 OMC의 SDK 사용 시점(<code>omc</code> CLI 자체 실행) 코드로, 플러그인으로서의 <code>t</code>/<code>team</code> 주입과는 별개 표면이다.</li>
</ol>
<h3>판정</h3>
<p>OMC는 MCP를 <strong>플러그인 매니페스트 기반 중앙 주입 + 보안 정책 게이트</strong>로 다룬다.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>중앙 주입</strong>: <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code> → <code>.mcp.json</code> → <code>t</code> 서버 단일 등록으로, 호스트의 모든 세션이 동일한 48개 코드인텔/상태 도구를 자동 획득한다([[09_cc_mcp]]의 6소스 병합 중 plugin manifest 경로만 사용 — 단순·균일).</li>
<li><strong>이중 서버 분리</strong>: <code>t</code>(정적, 경량 인텔/상태) vs <code>team</code>(동적/CLI-only, 무거운 워커 런타임)을 명시적으로 분리.</li>
<li><strong>의미 기반 코드 인텔</strong>: LSP 12도구 + 20 언어서버 + AST grep을 MCP로 노출 — 바닐라의 텍스트 grep을 보완([[09_cc_mcp]]의 DiagnosticsManager가 외부 LSP를 읽는 것과 달리, OMC는 LSP를 서버에 내장).</li>
<li><strong>정책 게이트</strong>: <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>가 원격 MCP·외부 LLM·경로·iteration을 한 스위치로 차단하며, 설정 파일은 강화만 가능.</li>
</ul>
<h2>재사용 관점</h2>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> plugin manifest 단일 등록으로 전 세션 도구 균일화 — 조직 배포의 강력한 레버</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>t</code>/<code>team</code> 도구 표면 분리(경량 정적 vs 무거운 동적)는 MCP 서버 설계 참조</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> LSP/AST를 MCP 서버에 내장해 의미 검색을 더하는 패턴</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>의 "config는 강화만, 완화 불가" 우선순위는 강제 정책 설계 참조</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <code>team</code> MCP의 CLI-only 마이그레이션 완료 시점은 본 분석 범위 밖</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>cli-jaw 비교 시사점</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>균일한 MCP 도구 표면</strong>: OMC는 <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code> → <code>.mcp.json</code> 단 한 줄로 모든 세션에 <code>t</code> 서버 48개 도구(LSP/AST/python/wiki/state)를 자동 주입한다. cli-jaw는 도구를 지연 로딩(<code>ToolSearch</code>)하고 직원별로 MCP/스킬 노출이 갈리는데, OMC의 플러그인 매니페스트 주입은 **"전 직원이 동일한 코드 인텔리전스 기반선을 갖게 만드는 레버"**다 — cli-jaw가 직원 단말에 균일 도구셋을 강제할 때 직접 차용 가능한 모델.</li>
<li><strong>의미 기반 코드 검색의 부재 보완</strong>: OMC의 LSP 12도구 + 20 언어서버 + <code>ast_grep_*</code>는 cli-jaw의 grep/browser 중심 검색이 약한 "정의/참조/타입 진단/구조 치환"을 메운다. 이를 단일 MCP 서버에 응집(<code>t</code>)해 한 번에 등록하는 방식은 cli-jaw가 코드 인텔을 도입할 때의 청사진이다.</li>
<li><strong>정책 킬스위치(<code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>)</strong>: 환경변수 하나로 원격 MCP(Exa/Context7)·외부 LLM(Codex/Gemini)·경로·python 샌드박스·iteration 상한을 동시에 잠그고, 프로젝트 설정은 강화만 허용한다. cli-jaw가 직원에게 외부 모델/원격 MCP 사용을 조직 정책으로 차단·강제할 때, "env > config 우선, 완화 불가"라는 정확한 안전 모델을 제공한다.</li>
<li><strong>무거운 워커 런타임의 분리</strong>: OMC는 코드 인텔(<code>t</code>, 정적·경량)과 워커 오케스트레이션(<code>team</code>, 동적·CLI-only)을 다른 주입 경로로 갈라, MCP 도구 표면을 가볍게 유지하고 무거운 프로세스 spawn을 CLI 경계 밖으로 격리한다. cli-jaw가 직원 디스패치(무거움)와 일상 도구(가벼움)를 같은 표면에 섞지 않도록 분리 설계할 때의 참고 패턴이다.</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<ul>
<li>2026-05-29: OMC v4.14.4 MCP 오케스트레이션을 소스 기반으로 작성. plugin.json→.mcp.json <code>t</code> 서버 정적 주입, tool-registry.ts의 48도구 집계, 12 LSP 도구 + 20 언어서버 + AST grep, team 서버 4도구의 CLI-only 폐기(<code>RETIRED_TEAM_MCP_PATH_PATTERN</code>/<code>deprecated_cli_only</code>), <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>의 7플래그·원격MCP/외부LLM 차단(security-config.ts, ask.ts, model-contract.ts) 정리. <p>이전 문서: [[09_cc_mcp]]
관련 문서: [[00_omc_overview]]</p>
28:["$","$L30","20-axis-09-mcp",{"id":"20-axis-09-mcp","order":26,"tier":"deep","title":"Axis 09: MCP orchestration","description":"009_mcp-orchestration/09_omc_mcp.md","lang":"en","badge":"AXIS","children":["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$4b"}}]}]
4c:T15293,<h2>Overview</h2>
<p><strong>Axis 10: Memory pipeline</strong> — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — memory pipeline.</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li>
<blockquote>
<p>이 문서는 <code>Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode</code>(OMC) 플러그인이 <strong>Claude Code 호스트의 Hook 라이프사이클만으로</strong> 어떻게 "기억"을 턴·세션·압축 경계를 가로질러 유지하는지 소스 기반으로 분석한다. [[10_cc_memory]](바닐라 Claude Code 메모리)가 <strong>CLAUDE.md + agent-memory + auto-compact + Dream</strong>의 4계층이었다면, OMC는 모</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li>
<blockquote>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li>
<blockquote>
<p>⚠️ <strong>소스 기반</strong>: <code>scripts/persistent-mode.mjs</code>(Stop 훅 재차단 루프), <code>scripts/session-start.mjs</code>(SessionStart 복원), <code>scripts/pre-compact.mjs</code> + <code>src/hooks/pre-compact/index.ts</code> + <code>src/hooks/project-memory/pre-compact.ts</code>(PreCompact 체크포인트), `src/too</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li>OMC의 메모리 시스템은 바닐라처럼 "LLM이 무엇을 기억할지 요약·정제"하는 파이프라인이 아니다. OMC의 본질은 <strong>상태 영속(state persistence)</strong> 이다. 즉 (1) 작업의 <em>진행 상태</em>를 <code>.omc/state/*.json</code>에 원자적으로 쓰고, (2) 턴이 끝나려 할 때마다 Stop 훅이 그 디스크 상태를 다시 읽어 "아직 안 끝났다"고 판정하면 턴을 재기동하고, (3) 세션이 새로 뜨거나 컨텍스트가 압축될</li>
<li>graph TD</li>
<li>subgraph Disk[".omc/ 디스크 영속 계층 (atomic write + file lock)"]</li>
<li>ST["state/*-state.json(ralph/ultrawork/autopilot/...)"]</li>
<li>SS["state/sessions/{sessionId}/(세션 격리 상태)"]</li>
<li>NP["notepad.md(priority/working/manual)"]</li>
<li>PM["project-memory.json(directives/notes/techStack)"]</li>
<li>WK["wiki/*.md(키워드 검색 KB, NO 임베딩)"]</li>
</ul>
<h2>Topics covered</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<ol>
<li><code>.omc/</code> 디스크 레이아웃 — 모든 영속의 단일 루트</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>1.1 worktree 경계 안으로 강제 — 경로 탈출 방어</li>
<li>1.2 원자적 쓰기 + 파일 락 — 동시성 안전</li>
<li>
<ol start="2">
<li>3계층 상태 영속 — 턴 / 세션 / 압축</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>
<ol start="3">
<li>계층 ① — Stop 훅 turn-survival (<code>scripts/persistent-mode.mjs</code>)</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>3.1 입력 파싱과 상태 디렉토리 해석</li>
<li>3.2 절대 차단하지 않는 조기 탈출 — 데드락·재인증 보존</li>
<li>3.3 모드 상태 읽기 — 세션 스코프 우선, 레거시 폴백</li>
<li>3.4 차단 판정 — 우선순위 + iteration/reinforcement 카운터</li>
<li>3.5 stale-state 방어 + 직전 Tool 오류 가이던스</li>
<li>
<ol start="4">
<li>계층 ② — SessionStart 복원 (<code>scripts/session-start.mjs</code>)</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>4.1 복원 항목과 <code>&#x3C;session-restore></code> 채널</li>
<li>4.2 6000자 예산 + 우선순위 정렬</li>
<li>4.3 세션 마커와 고아 정리</li>
</ul>

<h3>README / docs</h3>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) memory pipeline</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis Snapshot</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — memory pipeline.</p>
</blockquote>
<blockquote>
<p>This document provides a source-based analysis of how the <code>Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode</code> (OMC) plugin maintains "memory" across turn, session, and compression boundaries <strong>using only the Hook lifecycle of the Claude Code host</strong>. If [[10_cc_memory]] (vanilla Claude Code memory) was 4 layers of <strong>CLAUDE.md + agent-memory + auto-compact + Dream</strong>, OMC adds a separate memory/persistence layer with only <strong><code>.omc/</code> disk state + Hook</strong> without touching the model/harness.</p>
<p>⚠️ <strong>Source-based</strong>: <code>scripts/persistent-mode.mjs</code> (Stop hook re-blocking loop), <code>scripts/session-start.mjs</code> (SessionStart restoration), <code>scripts/pre-compact.mjs</code> + <code>src/hooks/pre-compact/index.ts</code> + <code>src/hooks/project-memory/pre-compact.ts</code>(PreCompact checkpoint), <code>src/tools/state-tools.ts</code>(state_read/write/clear), <code>src/tools/notepad-tools.ts</code>, <code>src/tools/memory-tools.ts</code>(project-memory), Based on <code>src/tools/shared-memory-tools.ts</code>, <code>src/tools/wiki-tools.ts</code> + <code>src/hooks/wiki/query.ts</code>, <code>src/lib/atomic-write.ts</code>, <code>src/lib/file-lock.ts</code>, <code>src/lib/worktree-paths.ts</code>, <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>. For higher operating principles, refer to [[00_omc_overview]] §7~§8.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>OMC's memory system is not a pipeline that "summarizes and refines what the LLM will remember" like vanilla. The essence of OMC is <strong>state persistence</strong>. That is, (1) the <em>progress state</em> of the task is atomically written to <code>.omc/state/*.json</code>, (2) whenever the turn is about to end, the Stop hook rereads the disk state and restarts the turn if it determines that it is "not over yet," and (3) when a new session is opened or the context is compressed, the SessionStart/PreCompact hook reinjects the state and user memory into the new context. Both the <em>content</em> of memory (notepad/project memory/wiki) and the <em>continuity</em> (state) of execution are placed in the same <code>.omc/</code> disk tree, and both are restored by the same Hook lifecycle.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph Disk[".omc/ 디스크 영속 계층 (atomic write + file lock)"]
        ST["state/*-state.json&#x3C;br/>(ralph/ultrawork/autopilot/...)"]
        SS["state/sessions/{sessionId}/&#x3C;br/>(세션 격리 상태)"]
        NP["notepad.md&#x3C;br/>(priority/working/manual)"]
        PM["project-memory.json&#x3C;br/>(directives/notes/techStack)"]
        WK["wiki/*.md&#x3C;br/>(키워드 검색 KB, NO 임베딩)"]
        SM["state/shared-memory/{ns}/{key}.json&#x3C;br/>(에이전트 간 핸드오프, TTL)"]
        CP["state/checkpoints/&#x3C;br/>(PreCompact 체크포인트)"]
    end

    subgraph Turn["① 턴 경계 (Stop 훅)"]
        Stop["persistent-mode.mjs&#x3C;br/>디스크 state 읽기 → decision:block / continue"]
    end
    subgraph Session["② 세션 경계 (SessionStart 훅)"]
        Restore["session-start.mjs +&#x3C;br/>project-memory-session.mjs +&#x3C;br/>wiki-session-start.mjs"]
    end
    subgraph Compact["③ 압축 경계 (PreCompact 훅)"]
        Ckpt["pre-compact.mjs +&#x3C;br/>project-memory-precompact.mjs +&#x3C;br/>wiki-pre-compact.mjs"]
    end

    ST --> Stop
    SS --> Stop
    Stop -->|"미완 → block → 턴 재진입"| ST
    ST --> Restore
    PM --> Restore
    NP --> Restore
    Restore -->|"&#x3C;session-restore> 주입"| Session
    PM --> Ckpt
    NP --> Ckpt
    ST --> Ckpt
    Ckpt -->|"systemMessage 재주입 + checkpoint 기록"| CP
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>1. <code>.omc/</code> disk layout — single root for all persistence</h2>
<p>All persistent data in the OMC is gathered in one place, the <code>.omc/</code> tree. Path constants are declared in <code>OmcPaths</code> in <code>src/lib/worktree-paths.ts</code>:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>path constant</th>
<th>actual path</th>
<th>Use</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>ROOT</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/</code></td>
<td>persistent route</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>STATE</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/state/</code></td>
<td>mode state(<code>{mode}-state.json</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>SESSIONS</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/state/sessions/{sessionId}/</code></td>
<td>Isolation status by session</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>NOTEPAD</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/notepad.md</code></td>
<td>Inter-session work notes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>PROJECT_MEMORY</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/project-memory.json</code></td>
<td>project memory</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>SHARED_MEMORY</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/state/shared-memory/{ns}/{key}.json</code></td>
<td>Inter-Agent Handoff</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>PLANS</code> / <code>RESEARCH</code> / <code>LOGS</code> / <code>NOTEPADS</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/plans/</code> etc</td>
<td>Planning·Research·Log·Plan-specific notepad</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>(wiki)</td>
<td><code>.omc/wiki/*.md</code></td>
<td>LLM Wiki Knowledge Base</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>(checkpoints)</td>
<td><code>.omc/state/checkpoints/</code></td>
<td>PreCompact Checkpoint</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>1.1 Force into worktree boundary — path escape defense</h3>
<p><code>getOmcRoot()</code> returns <code>{worktreeRoot}/.omc</code> by default. If there is an <code>OMC_STATE_DIR</code> environment variable, it switches to <strong>centralized storage</strong> with <code>{OMC_STATE_DIR}/{projectId}</code>, and if the legacy <code>.omc/</code> and the central directory exist at the same time, a warning is output once (<code>worktree-paths.ts:getOmcRoot</code>). <code>resolveOmcPath()</code> seals the path injection by throwing a <code>Path escapes omc boundary</code> exception if the resolved absolute path goes outside the omc root (<code>relativeToOmc.startsWith('..')</code>). All MCP tools (state/notepad/memory/wiki) operate only within this boundary via <code>validateWorkingDirectory()</code>.</p>
<h3>1.2 Atomic Write + File Lock — Concurrency Safety</h3>
<p>Since state file corruption is "memory loss", OMC has two concurrency safeguards:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Atomic Write</strong>(<code>src/lib/atomic-write.ts</code>): <code>atomicWriteJson()</code>/<code>atomicWriteSync()</code> creates an exclusive with <code>O_CREAT|O_EXCL</code>(wx, mode <code>0o600</code>) in the temporary file <code>.{base}.tmp.{uuid}</code> → <code>fd.sync()</code>(fsync) → Used in the following order: <code>rename()</code> (atomic replacement) → directory fsync. In case of failure, clean up temporary files. <code>state_write</code>/cancel-signal/mission-state writes this (<code>atomicWriteJsonSync</code> in <code>state-tools.ts</code>).</li>
<li><strong>File Lock</strong> (<code>src/lib/file-lock.ts</code>): <code>withFileLockSync</code>/<code>withFileLock</code> uses the kernel-guaranteed “at most one process creation success” semantics by making adjacent lock files <code>O_CREAT|O_EXCL</code>. The PID is recorded in the lock payload, and if <code>staleLockMs</code> (default 30,000ms) is exceeded + the owning PID dies (<code>isProcessAlive</code>), it is considered a stale lock and is automatically recovered (reaped). Retry interval <code>retryDelayMs</code> (default 50ms). Used by notepad (based on <code>readFileSync</code>)·project-memory·shared-memory.</li>
<li>However, <code>persistent-mode.mjs</code> (Stop hook) uses its own <code>writeJsonFile</code> without lib import for reliability, and this also follows the atomic write pattern of <code>.{pid}.{time}.tmp</code> → <code>rename</code> (<code>persistent-mode.mjs:88</code>).</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>2. Three-layer state persistence — Turn / Session / Compression</h2>
<p>OMC's core argument is <em>"Vanilla Claude Code ends a turn when <code>stop_reason=end_turn</code>, but OMC restarts itself across turn boundaries while the disk state is incomplete"</em>([[00_omc_overview]] §7.1), and this is shared among the three Hooks. Mapping for <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>border</th>
<th>Hook</th>
<th>script</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>① Turn</strong></td>
<td><code>Stop</code></td>
<td><code>context-guard-stop.mjs</code>, <strong><code>persistent-mode.mjs</code></strong>, <code>code-simplifier.mjs</code></td>
<td>Read disk state, if incomplete, re-enter turn with <code>decision:block</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>② Session</strong></td>
<td><code>SessionStart</code></td>
<td><code>session-start.mjs</code>, <code>project-memory-session.mjs</code>, <code>wiki-session-start.mjs</code></td>
<td>Reinject active mode status·project memory·wiki into new session</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>③ Compression</strong></td>
<td><code>PreCompact</code></td>
<td><code>pre-compact.mjs</code>, <code>project-memory-precompact.mjs</code>, <code>wiki-pre-compact.mjs</code></td>
<td>Pre-compression mode status·directive·notepad wisdom checkpoint + post-compaction reinjection</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Additionally, <code>PostToolUse</code> (<code>project-memory-posttool.mjs</code>) updates the project memory after each tool execution, and <code>SessionEnd</code> (<code>session-end.mjs</code>) takes care of session cleanup. Since the three boundaries share the same <code>.omc/</code> disk, a single invariant holds: "Restore from disk no matter where it breaks."</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant CC as Claude Code 코어 루프
    participant Stop as Stop 훅 (persistent-mode.mjs)
    participant SS as SessionStart 훅
    participant PC as PreCompact 훅
    participant Disk as .omc/state/*.json + memory

    Note over CC,Disk: 스킬 활성 시 state_write로 {mode}-state.json{active:true} 기록
    loop 매 턴 종료
        CC->>Stop: stop_reason=end_turn (+ transcript_path)
        Stop->>Disk: 모드 상태 읽기 (세션 스코프 우선)
        alt 컨텍스트≥95% / 컨텍스트한계 / 401·403 / user abort / cancel
            Stop-->>CC: {continue:true, suppressOutput:true}
        else 미완 &#x26; iteration&#x3C;max &#x26; 신선 &#x26; 현재 프로젝트·세션
            Stop->>Disk: iteration++ / reinforcement_count++ 기록
            Stop-->>CC: {decision:"block", reason:"[RALPH LOOP #n/max] continue..."}
            CC->>CC: 같은 세션에서 턴 재진입
        end
    end
    Note over CC,PC: 컨텍스트 압축 직전
    CC->>PC: PreCompact(trigger=auto/manual)
    PC->>Disk: 체크포인트 기록 + directive/notepad 재주입(systemMessage)
    Note over CC,SS: 새 세션 시작
    CC->>SS: SessionStart(session_id, cwd)
    SS->>Disk: active 모드·project-memory·notepad priority 읽기
    SS-->>CC: &#x3C;session-restore> additionalContext 주입 (6000자 예산)
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>3. Layer ① — Stop hook turn-survival (<code>scripts/persistent-mode.mjs</code>)</h2>
<p>This is the <strong>most essential memory mechanism</strong> that OMC adds to vanilla: leave execution state on disk, and read that state back at the end of each turn, continuing the loop.</p>
<h3>3.1 Input parsing and state directory interpretation</h3>
<p>The hook receives <code>{cwd, session_id, stop_reason, transcript_path, ...}</code> as stdin (<code>main()</code>, <code>persistent-mode.mjs:880</code>). <code>sanitizeSessionId()</code> verifies the session ID with the <code>^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]{0,255}$</code> whitelist, sets the omc root with <code>resolveOmcStateRoot(directory)</code>, and sets <code>stateDir = {omcRoot}/state</code>, <code>globalStateDir = ~/.omc/state</code> decide</p>
<h3>3.2 Early escape without ever blocking — preserving deadlock and re-authentication</h3>
<p>Before blocking, the host's compression/reauthentication/cancellation flow is preserved by unconditionally passing it to <code>{continue:true, suppressOutput:true}</code> in the following cases (in order of priority):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Conditions</th>
<th>function</th>
<th>Evidence</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>context limit stop</td>
<td><code>isContextLimitStop</code></td>
<td>When blocking, “cannot be compressed → cannot be stopped” deadlock (issue #213)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Transcript context ≥95%</td>
<td><code>estimateContextPercent</code> ≥ <code>CRITICAL_CONTEXT_STOP_PERCENT(95)</code></td>
<td>Estimate <code>input_tokens/context_window</code> by reading only the last 4KB (prevent OOM of large transcripts)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>User Abort (Ctrl+C/cancel)</td>
<td><code>isUserAbort</code></td>
<td>Respect user intent</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Authentication Error (401/403/OAuth Expired)</td>
<td><code>isAuthenticationError</code></td>
<td>Re-authentication flow preservation</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Reservation wakeup stop</td>
<td><code>isScheduledWakeupStop</code></td>
<td>ScheduleWakeup/loop resume passed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Pending asynchronous operation</td>
<td><code>hasPendingOwnedAsyncWork</code></td>
<td>If the background task/scheduled-wakeup state is alive, turn on</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Session cancellation in progress</td>
<td><code>isSessionCancelInProgress</code></td>
<td><code>cancel-signal-state.json</code> TTL passes within 30 seconds</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>This defense layer is a codification of the safety principle of “do not destroy the host while protecting the memory.”</p>
<h3>3.3 Read mode state — session scope first, legacy fallback</h3>
<p>The hook reads the state of 9 modes with <code>readStateFileWithSession()</code>: <code>ralph / ultragoal / autopilot / ultrapilot / ultrawork / ultraqa / pipeline / team / omc-teams</code> (+ marker-based swarm). Search order for this function (<code>persistent-mode.mjs:451</code>):</p>
<ol>
<li><code>{stateDir}/sessions/{sessionId}/{mode}-state.json</code> (correct session scope)</li>
<li>Scan all <code>{stateDir}/sessions/*/</code> and find candidates with <code>state.session_id === sessionId</code></li>
<li>Legacy <code>{stateDir}/{mode}-state.json</code> (but only if <code>session_id</code> matches)</li>
</ol>
<p>Session isolation is a response to issue #311 (Parallel session state leak). Prevents the state of other sessions/projects from incorrectly blocking new sessions.</p>
<h3>3.4 Blocking decision — priority + iteration/reinforcement counter</h3>
<p>Each mode is evaluated in order of priority. If a mode decides to block, it <code>returns</code> immediately.</p>
<p><strong>Priority 1 — Ralph</strong>(<code>persistent-mode.mjs:1004</code>): <code>isAuthoritativeModeActive</code> &#x26;&#x26; !<code>isAwaitingConfirmation</code> &#x26;&#x26; !<code>isStaleState</code> &#x26;&#x26; <code>isStateForCurrentProject</code> &#x26;&#x26; If session matches:</p>
<ul>
<li>If <code>iteration &#x3C; max_iterations</code>, after recording <code>iteration++</code></li>
</ul>
<pre><code class="language-js">  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    decision: "block",
    reason: `[RALPH LOOP - ITERATION ${iteration+1}/${maxIter}] Work is NOT done. Continue working.
  When FULLY complete (after Architect verification), run /oh-my-claudecode:cancel to cleanly exit ralph mode...
  Task: ${ralph.state.prompt}`
  }));
</code></pre>
<ul>
<li>If <code>iteration >= max</code>: automatically disable with <code>active:false</code> (<code>[RALPH LOOP - HARD LIMIT]</code>) when hard upper limit (<code>getHardMaxIterations()</code>) is reached, otherwise expand to <code>max_iterations += 10</code> (<code>[RALPH LOOP - EXTENDED]</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p>Counter/reason templates for the remaining modes:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>mode</th>
<th>counter field</th>
<th>upper limit</th>
<th>reason header</th>
<th>Remarks</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>ralph</td>
<td><code>iteration</code></td>
<td><code>max_iterations</code> (default 100) → +10 extension</td>
<td><code>[RALPH LOOP - ITERATION n/max]</code></td>
<td>The hard upper limit is <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>, which defaults to 200</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ultragoal</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td><code>max_reinforcements</code> (default 50)</td>
<td><code>[ULTRAGOAL #n/max]</code></td>
<td><code>.omc/ultragoal/ledger.jsonl</code>·<code>goals.json</code> Force checkpoint, check terminal status</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>autopilot</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td>20</td>
<td><code>[AUTOPILOT - Phase: x]</code></td>
<td>Only when <code>phase !== "complete"</code>, orphan routing echo state passes after file deletion</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ultrapilot</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td>20</td>
<td><code>[ULTRAPILOT]</code></td>
<td>Based on the number of incomplete workers</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>swarm</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td>15</td>
<td><code>[SWARM ACTIVE]</code></td>
<td><code>swarm-active.marker</code> + <code>swarm-summary.json</code> (SQLite separately)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>pipeline</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td>15</td>
<td><code>[PIPELINE - Stage n/total]</code></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>team / omc-teams</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td>20</td>
<td><code>[TEAM - Phase: x]</code> / <code>[OMC TEAMS - Phase: x]</code></td>
<td>Determine active/terminal phase with <code>normalizeTeamPhase</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ultraqa</td>
<td><code>cycle</code></td>
<td><code>max_cycles</code> (default 10)</td>
<td><code>[ULTRAQA - Cycle n/max]</code></td>
<td>Stop if <code>all_passing</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ultrawork</td>
<td><code>reinforcement_count</code></td>
<td><code>max_reinforcements</code> (default 50)</td>
<td><code>[ULTRAWORK #n/max]</code></td>
<td><strong>Always continue as long as active</strong> (prevents false stops due to transient failure)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>On each block, we update <code>last_checked_at</code> and write the counter back to disk → the next Stop hook sees that increment. This <em>counter written on disk</em> is OMC's "repetition count memory".</p>
<h3>3.5 stale-state defense + previous tool error guidance</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>stale defense</strong>(<code>isStaleState</code>, <code>persistent-mode.mjs:227</code>): If the most recent value among <code>last_checked_at</code>/<code>updated_at</code>/<code>started_at</code> exceeds <strong>2 hours (<code>STALE_STATE_THRESHOLD_MS</code>)</strong>, it is considered inactive. Prevents accidents where the dead state of the previous session blocks the new session indefinitely. <code>isStateForCurrentProject</code> normalizes and compares <code>project_path</code> to prevent contamination of other projects.</li>
<li><strong>Workflow slot tombstone</strong>(<code>isWorkflowSlotTombstonedForMode</code>): If <code>completed_at</code> is marked in the slot of <code>skill-active-state.json</code> and within 24 hours, the mode is considered to have already been completed and is not blocked again.</li>
<li><strong>Last tool error injection</strong> (<code>readLastToolError</code>/<code>getToolErrorRetryGuidance</code>): Read <code>last-tool-error.json</code> and if there is a fresh failure within <strong>60 seconds</strong>, add <code>[TOOL ERROR - RETRY REQUIRED]</code> (or <code>[TOOL ERROR - ALTERNATIVE APPROACH NEEDED]</code> if more than 5 times) in front of reason. In other words, the “memory of the previous failure” is carried to the next turn prompt.</li>
</ul>
<p>The <code>PostToolUseFailure</code> hook (<code>post-tool-use-failure.mjs</code>) uses this <code>last-tool-error.json</code>, and this is the send/receive pair consumed by the Stop hook.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. Layer ② — Restore SessionStart (<code>scripts/session-start.mjs</code>)</h2>
<p>When a new session (or <code>--resume</code>/<code>--continue</code>) pops up, any "memory" left on disk is reinjected into the new context.</p>
<h3>4.1 Restore items and the <code>&#x3C;session-restore></code> channel</h3>
<p><code>main()</code>(<code>session-start.mjs:708</code>) collects <code>messages[]</code> and injects them all at once into <code>hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext</code>. Main items:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Mode state restoration</strong>: Read <code>ultrawork-state.json</code>·<code>ralph-state.json</code> with session scope priority, and if <code>shouldRestoreModeState</code> (not active &#x26;&#x26; tombstone), inject <code>[ULTRAWORK MODE RESTORED]</code>/<code>[RALPH LOOP RESTORED]</code>. The only phrase is <em>"prior-session context only. Prioritizes the user's latest request and resumes only when explicitly told to continue"</em>, which is <strong>soft restoration</strong> that prevents automatic congestion (compared to the hard block of the Stop hook).</li>
<li><strong>Incomplete todo detection</strong>: Only scan project local <code>.omc/todos.json</code>·<code>.claude/todos.json</code> (global todos are intentionally excluded — issue #354 phantom count prevention) → <code>[PENDING TASKS DETECTED]</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Project Memory</strong>: <code>project-memory-session.mjs</code> (or inline <code>resolveProjectMemorySummary</code>) loads <code>project-memory.json</code>, rescans it into <code>detectProjectEnvironment</code> if necessary and injects it into <code>[PROJECT MEMORY]</code> (§6).</li>
<li><strong>Notepad Priority Context</strong>: Extracts the <code>## Priority Context</code> section of <code>.omc/notepad.md</code> using a regular expression, removes comments, and injects it as <code>[NOTEPAD - Priority Context]</code> if it is not empty (§5).</li>
<li>Added: version drift (<code>detectVersionDrift</code>), npm update (24h cache), HUD installation check, model routing override (Bedrock/Vertex/non-Claude detection).</li>
</ul>
<h3>4.2 6000 character budget + priority sorting</h3>
<p>The injection context is limited to <code>SESSION_START_CONTEXT_BUDGET = 6000</code> characters (<code>buildSessionStartAdditionalContext</code>). After sorting in priority order in the following order: <code>MODEL ROUTING OVERRIDE → MODE RESTORED → PROJECT MEMORY → NOTEPAD Priority → PENDING TASKS</code>, items that are about to exceed the budget are cut out with <code>compactBudgetedText</code>, and if any remain, a notice of omission is added. In other words, “what to restore to memory first” is explicitly prioritized.</p>
<h3>4.3 Session markers and orphan cleanup</h3>
<p>SessionStart records <code>{session_id, started_at, cwd, pid, boot_id}</code> (mode <code>0o600</code>) in <code>state/sessions/{sessionId}/session-started.json</code>. <code>reconcileAbandonedSessionStarts</code> looks at the markers of other sessions, but <strong>pid survival checking within the same boot_id is unreliable</strong> (the parent PID of the installable hook is a short-lived process in <code>scripts/run.cjs</code>), so <code>hasDurableAbandonmentEvidence</code> will classify it as an orphan and clean it up <strong>only if the boot IDs are different</strong>. SessionEnd is the main path for cleanup on the same boot — conservative cleanup to avoid erroneous deletion of live session state.</p>
<hr>
<h2>5. Notepad (<code>notepad.md</code> / <code>src/tools/notepad-tools.ts</code>)</h2>
<p>Working notes between sessions. <code>t</code> The MCP server exposes six tools, covering three sections of a single <code>.omc/notepad.md</code>:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>section</th>
<th>tools</th>
<th>Semantics</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Priority Context</strong></td>
<td><code>notepad_write_priority</code></td>
<td><strong>REPLACE</strong>. 500 characters or less is recommended (upper limit is 2000) as it is always loaded in SessionStart.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Working Memory</strong></td>
<td><code>notepad_write_working</code></td>
<td><strong>APPEND</strong>(timestamp). Automatic prune after 7 days. Upper limit is 4000 characters/entry.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>MANUAL</strong></td>
<td><code>notepad_write_manual</code></td>
<td><strong>APPEND</strong>. <strong>Never auto-prune</strong>. Upper limit is 4000 characters.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>(Read/Manage)</td>
<td><code>notepad_read</code>(section: all/priority/working/manual), <code>notepad_prune</code>(daysOld 1~365, default 7), <code>notepad_stats</code></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The key constants are <code>DEFAULT_CONFIG = { workingMemoryDays: 7, ... }</code> and <code>PRIORITY_HEADER = "## Priority Context"</code> in <code>src/hooks/notepad/index.ts</code>. Since SessionStart floats this <code>## Priority Context</code> header as a regular expression and injects it into a new session, Priority Context is in effect "the lightest memory channel for passing sessions." Working Memory is volatile for 7 days, MANUAL is permanent – ​​signal-to-noise is managed with TTL differential.</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. Project memory (<code>project-memory.json</code> / <code>src/tools/memory-tools.ts</code>)</h2>
<p>Structured memory on a per-project basis. <code>t</code> Four MCP server tools:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>project_memory_read</code>(section: all/techStack/build/conventions/structure/notes/directives)</li>
<li><code>project_memory_write</code> (merge option — <code>mergeProjectMemory</code> allows merging with existing, <code>version</code>/<code>lastScanned</code>/<code>projectRoot</code> reinforcement)</li>
<li><code>project_memory_add_note</code> (category + content, 1000 characters)</li>
<li><code>project_memory_add_directive</code> (directive 500 characters + context + priority <code>high|normal</code>)</li>
</ul>
<p>The <code>ProjectMemory</code> structure includes <code>techStack</code> (languages/frameworks), <code>build</code> (buildCommand/testCommand), <code>conventions</code>, <code>structure</code>, <code>hotPaths</code>, <code>customNotes</code>, and <code>userDirectives</code>. The environment is automatically detected and saved as <code>detectProjectEnvironment</code> at SessionStart.</p>
<h3>6.1 directive — User directive to withstand compression</h3>
<p>The tooltip for <code>project_memory_add_directive</code> states: <em>"Directives are instructions that persist across sessions <strong>and survive compaction</strong>."</em> This meshes directly with the PreCompact hook (§7). Accumulated with <code>addDirective</code>, <code>PostToolUse</code> (<code>project-memory-posttool.mjs</code>) updates memory during session.</p>
<hr>
<h2>7. Layer ③ — PreCompact checkpoint (<code>src/hooks/pre-compact/</code>)</h2>
<p>Prevents information loss due to context compression. <code>pre-compact.mjs</code> receives stdin and calls <code>processPreCompact(data)</code> in <code>dist/hooks/pre-compact/index.js</code>.</p>
<h3>7.1 Checkpoint contents</h3>
<p><code>CompactCheckpoint</code> in <code>src/hooks/pre-compact/index.ts</code> writes the following to <code>.omc/state/checkpoints/</code>:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>active_modes</code>: autopilot(phase/originalIdea) · ralph(iteration/prompt) · ultrawork(original_prompt) · ultraqa(cycle/prompt)</li>
<li><code>todo_summary</code>: pending/in_progress/completed count</li>
<li><code>wisdom_exported</code>: "Plan Wisdom" (<code>exportWisdomToNotepad</code>), which collects <code>learnings.md</code>/<code>decisions.md</code>/<code>issues.md</code>/<code>problems.md</code> from <code>.omc/notepads/{plan}/</code></li>
<li><code>background_jobs</code>: Active/recent background jobs + statistics (<code>job-state-db</code>)</li>
</ul>
<p><code>trigger</code> is <code>"manual" | "auto"</code> (same schema as PreCompact trigger in vanilla [[10_cc_memory]] §3.3). To prevent simultaneous compression competition, a <strong>compression mutex</strong> is placed with a per-directory in-flight compression promise (<code>inflightCompactions</code>) and a queue depth counter (<code>compactionQueueDepth</code>) — even if subagent results from ultrawork/team arrive simultaneously, they are compressed only once.</p>
<h3>7.2 directive·memory post-compaction reinjection</h3>
<p>Separate hook <code>project-memory-precompact.mjs</code> → <code>processPreCompact</code> in <code>src/hooks/project-memory/pre-compact.ts</code> loads <code>project-memory.json</code> and reinjects it into <code>systemMessage</code> if there is critical information among <code>userDirectives</code>/<code>hotPaths</code>/<code>techStack</code>/<code>customNotes</code>:</p>
<pre><code># Project Memory (Post-Compaction Recovery)
... contextSummary ...
**IMPORTANT:** These user directives must be followed throughout the session, even after compaction.
</code></pre>
<p><code>wiki-pre-compact.mjs</code> also checkpoints the wiki context in the same way. In other words, even if compression occurs, user directives, project context, and wiki knowledge are restored to the context after compression — it is a form in which OMC specializes vanilla's PreCompact hook custom instruction injection ([[10_cc_memory]] §3.3) for its own memory layer.</p>
<hr>
<h2>8. wiki — Cross-session knowledge base (NO vector embeddings)</h2>
<p>Cumulative markdown knowledge base in <code>.omc/wiki/*.md</code>. <code>t</code> The MCP server exposes 7 tools (<code>src/tools/wiki-tools.ts</code>):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>tools</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>wiki_ingest</code></td>
<td>Processing knowledge into pages. <strong>append strategy</strong> (never replace), multiple pages can be updated simultaneously with cross-references</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>wiki_add</code></td>
<td>Fast creation of single page (reject if exists → induce merge with ingest)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>wiki_query</code></td>
<td>Keyword+tag search, returns snippets of relevance</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>wiki_read</code> / <code>wiki_list</code> / <code>wiki_delete</code></td>
<td>Read Page / Index List / Delete</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>wiki_lint</code></td>
<td>orphan·stale·broken-ref·oversized·contradiction Health check</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>There are 8 page categories: <code>architecture / decision / pattern / debugging / environment / session-log / reference / convention</code>, reliability <code>high|medium|low</code>, content upper limit 50KB.</p>
<h3>8.1 Keyword search without embeddings — hard constraints</h3>
<p>The tooltip for <code>wiki_query</code> and the <code>src/hooks/wiki/query.ts</code> header explicitly nail it: <strong>"NO vector embeddings — search is keyword-based only (hard constraint). The LLM caller synthesizes answers from returned matches."</strong> (The only mention of wiki's embeddings/vectors in the entire repo are these two "none" declarations.) The matching strategy is (1) tag intersection (highest weighting) → (2) title Include → (3) Scoring in order of content inclusion, and <code>tokenize()</code> supports Korean/Japanese/Chinese searches by processing <strong>CJK bi-gram + single character</strong>. The tool only returns raw matches, and <em>answer synthesis and citation are done by LLM directly</em> — this is a division of labor of "search is deterministic, synthesis is LLM", in contrast to Dream consolidation (LLM refines memory in the background) in [[10_cc_memory]] §4.</p>
<hr>
<h2>9. Shared memory — Inter-agent handoff (<code>shared-memory-tools.ts</code>)</h2>
<p>A layer in <code>/team</code>·<code>/pipeline</code> where multiple agents exchange key-values on a session group/pipeline run basis. The repository is <code>.omc/state/shared-memory/{namespace}/{key}.json</code>.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>tools</th>
<th>Role</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>shared_memory_write</code></td>
<td>write namespace·key·value, optional <code>ttl</code>(seconds, max 604800=7 days) → <code>expiresAt</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>shared_memory_read</code></td>
<td>read namespace+key (null on expiration)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>shared_memory_list</code></td>
<td>List of keys or entire namespaces within a namespace</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>shared_memory_delete</code> / <code>shared_memory_cleanup</code></td>
<td>Clean up deleted/expired entries</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p><strong>Configuration Gate</strong>: All tools return <code>DISABLED_MSG</code> unless <code>agents.sharedMemory.enabled = true</code> (<code>~/.claude/.omc-config.json</code>) (<code>isSharedMemoryEnabled</code>). Concurrent writing is protected with a file lock (<code>src/lib/file-lock.ts</code>). If project-memory is <em>a persistent fact of a project</em>, shared-memory is <em>a volatile coordination state during a team run</em> (automatically destroyed with TTL).</p>
<hr>
<h2>10. Mod registry and state cleanup (<code>state-tools.ts</code>)</h2>
<p><code>t</code> The MCP server exposes five state CRUD tools: <code>state_read / state_write / state_clear / state_list_active / state_get_status</code>. The original execution mode is <code>EXECUTION_MODES = autopilot, autoresearch, team, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, deep-interview, self-improve</code>, and state-only modes (<code>ralplan, omc-teams, skill-active</code>) are added (<code>STATE_TOOL_MODES</code>).</p>
<ul>
<li><strong><code>state_write</code></strong>: Merges free-form <code>state</code> records with explicit parameters (active/iteration/max_iterations/current_phase/...), attaches <code>_meta</code> (mode/sessionId/updatedAt/updatedBy), verifies size with <code>validatePayload</code> and writes with <code>atomicWriteJsonSync</code>. If <code>session_id</code> is not provided, it is written to the legacy shared path and a leak warning is issued.</li>
<li><strong><code>state_clear</code></strong>: Clears all session scope, legacy, completed session orphans, local fallbacks, runtime artifacts (<code>{mode}-stop-breaker.json</code>, etc.), and uses <code>cancel-signal-state.json</code> (TTL 30 seconds) <strong>before deletion</strong> to ensure that <code>isSessionCancelInProgress</code> in the Stop hook sees a signal during the deletion window (avoiding a race). Team mode organizes the worktree runtime root and HUD mission entries.</li>
</ul>
<p>All reason strings in a persistent loop indicate <em>"After completion/verification, cleanly exit with <code>/oh-my-claudecode:cancel</code> (<code>--force</code> in case of failure)"</em>, and this cancel calls <code>state_clear</code> to clear the disk state and ensure exit from the loop. It is a double safety device that breaks infinite loops with an iteration/reinforcement upper limit and cancel information.</p>
<hr>
<h2>11. Summary — The essence of the OMC memory pipeline</h2>
<ul>
<li>OMC places <strong>memory contents (notepad/project-memory/wiki/shared-memory)</strong> and <strong>execution continuity (<code>.omc/state/</code>)</strong> in the same disk tree and prevents corruption and contention with <strong>atomic write + file lock</strong>.</li>
<li>Three Hooks share three boundaries: <strong>Stop</strong> (turn — hard block re-entry), <strong>SessionStart</strong> (session — soft restore + 6000 character budget), <strong>PreCompact</strong> (compaction — directive·wisdom checkpoint + post-compaction reinjection).</li>
<li>Counter (iteration/reinforcement/cycle), timestamp (<code>last_checked_at</code>), stale 2-hour threshold, session/project isolation, and tombstone leave an honest memory of “how far we have gone” on disk.</li>
<li>The wiki provides only deterministic search through <strong>keyword search without embedding</strong> (hard constraints), and synthesis is handled by LLM — the opposite division of labor from vanilla's LLM-based Dream consolidation.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>cli-jaw comparison implications</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Difference in turn survival mechanism — Stop hook reblock vs. Target re-injection</strong>: OMC <code>persistent-mode.mjs</code> reads the disk state at the end of each turn and <em>physically re-enters</em> the turn with <code>decision:"block"</code> (no model/harness change). cli-jaw has the same "never die and poll" (MEMORY.md's <em>Use poll for waiting</em>) philosophy, but uses ScheduleWakeup/poll to wait for external events. The strength of OMC is that it runs an internal task completion loop with only a host Stop hook without an external cron, and cli-jaw is strong in waiting for external events. By augmenting cli-jaw's goal-continuation reinjection with OMC-style "disk state + stop blocking", continuity can be achieved without using model tokens.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Three-layer restoration decomposition can be borrowed directly</strong>: A design with a clear three-boundary decomposition of "Stop / SessionStart / PreCompact", with each boundary sharing the same <code>.omc/</code> disk as a single truth, is a blueprint that can be transferred directly to cli-jaw's instance/dashboard memory persistence. In particular, SessionStart's <strong>6000 character budget + priority sorting</strong> (routing > restored mode > project memory > notepad > pending tasks) is a specific solution to the problem of the reinjection context becoming bloated.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Differential strength of re-injection (hard block vs. soft restore)</strong>: Even in the same state, the Stop hook is injected differently as a hard block (immediate re-entry), and the SessionStart is injected differently as a soft restore of <em>"prior-session context only, only when an explicit resume request is requested"</em>. When cli-jaw re-injects a goal, it distinguishes by boundary whether it is "force continue now" or "previous context for reference", which can prevent a deluge of new user requests.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Deterministic search + LLM synthesis, and guaranteed exit</strong>: Wiki's embedding-free keyword search (<code>wiki_query</code>) is a low-cost model that builds knowledge between sessions without infrastructure burden, and serves as a reference to start without a vector DB when cli-jaw attaches memory search. Additionally, the fact that every persistent loop reason guides the exit to <code>/cancel</code> (→ <code>state_clear</code> + cancel-signal TTL) and places an iteration/reinforcement upper limit, 2-hour stale threshold, and 60-second tool error freshness is a set of "prevent infinite loops + automatically ignore dead states" safeguards essential to cli-jaw's <code>/loop</code>·poll design.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>

Related document: [[00_omc_overview]]</p>
