</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>호스트가 제공하는 25개 이벤트([[11_cc_plugin]] §25개 이벤트) 중 OMC가 미사용하는 것: <code>Notification</code>, <code>StopFailure</code>, <code>PostCompact</code>, <code>Setup</code>(별도 init/maintenance 매처로 대체), <code>TeammateIdle</code>, <code>TaskCompleted</code>, <code>Elicitation</code>/<code>ElicitationResult</code>, <code>ConfigChange</code>, <code>Worktree*</code>, <code>InstructionsLoaded</code>, <code>CwdChanged</code>, <code>FileChanged</code>. 즉 OMC는 호스트 라이프사이클의 <strong>핵심 골격(프롬프트·세션·도구·서브에이전트·압축·종료)만 선택적으로 점유</strong>한다.</p>
<h3>2.2 matcher 활용 — SessionStart의 source 분기</h3>
<p>OMC가 호스트 matcher를 가장 정교하게 쓰는 곳은 <code>SessionStart</code>다. [[11_cc_plugin]]에 따르면 <code>SessionStart</code>의 matcher 대상은 <code>source</code>(startup/resume/clear/compact 등)다. OMC는 <code>*</code>, <code>init</code>, <code>maintenance</code> 세 매처를 별도 엔트리로 등록해, <strong>호스트가 같은 이벤트를 source별로 다른 스크립트에 라우팅</strong>하게 한다(<code>hooks/hooks.json:21-62</code>). <code>PermissionRequest</code>는 <code>matcher: "Bash"</code>로 Bash 도구에만 권한 핸들러를 건다.</p>
<h3>2.3 훅 출력 규약 — 호스트 hookSpecificOutput API 준수</h3>
<p>OMC 훅은 [[11_cc_plugin]] §출력 파싱의 호스트 JSON 출력 스키마를 정확히 따른다. <code>keyword-detector.mjs</code>는 잘못된 필드 사용을 명시적으로 경계한다(주석 <code>scripts/keyword-detector.mjs:1040</code>: "The 'message' field is NOT a valid hook output - use hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext"):</p>
<pre><code class="language-js">// scripts/keyword-detector.mjs:1042
function createHookOutput(additionalContext) {
  return {
    continue: true,
    hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: 'UserPromptSubmit', additionalContext }
  };
}
</code></pre>
<p>즉 <code>UserPromptSubmit</code> 훅이 <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: RALPH]</code> 같은 트리거 텍스트를 <code>hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext</code>로 컨텍스트에 주입하면, 호스트가 이를 사용자 프롬프트에 합성한다. 이것이 OMC의 키워드 자동 트리거 UX의 기반이다. 이벤트별 출력 필드(<code>UserPromptSubmit</code>의 <code>additionalContext</code>, <code>Stop</code>의 <code>decision</code>/<code>reason</code>)는 [[11_cc_plugin]]이 정의한 호스트 스키마와 1:1 대응한다.</p>
<h3>2.3.1 UserPromptSubmit 훅의 2단 파이프라인</h3>
<p><code>UserPromptSubmit</code> 이벤트 하나에 OMC는 두 훅을 순차로 건다(<code>hooks/hooks.json:4-20</code>), 각각이 호스트의 <code>additionalContext</code> 주입 경로를 다르게 활용한다:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong><code>keyword-detector.mjs</code></strong>(timeout 5s): 사용자 입력을 정규식으로 스캔해 매직 키워드를 스킬 트리거로 변환한다. 출력은 <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: &#x3C;SKILL>]</code> 텍스트를 <code>hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext</code>에 실어 호스트가 프롬프트에 합성하게 한다(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:959</code>). 이 훅은 동시에 디스크 상태도 쓴다 — <code>ralph</code>/<code>team</code> 등 모드 감지 시 <code>.omc/state/ralph-state.json</code>을 <code>mode: 0o600</code>으로 기록(<code>keyword-detector.mjs:903-913</code>)해, 나중 <code>Stop</code> 훅(§2.4)이 읽을 상태를 미리 심는다. 무한 스폰 방지를 위해 <code>OMC_TEAM_WORKER</code> 환경이면 키워드 탐지를 건너뛴다.</li>
<li><strong><code>skill-injector.mjs</code></strong>(timeout 3s): learner/skillify가 만든 <strong>학습 스킬</strong>을 컨텍스트에 주입한다. 세 위치를 재귀 탐색한다 — 유저(<code>{configDir}/skills/omc-learned</code>), 글로벌(<code>~/.omc/skills</code>), 프로젝트(<code>.omc/skills</code>)(<code>skill-injector.mjs:31-33</code>). 세션당 최대 5개(<code>MAX_SKILLS_PER_SESSION</code>), 컨텍스트 3000자 상한(<code>MAX_LEARNED_SKILLS_CONTEXT_CHARS</code>). 가능하면 컴파일된 <code>dist/hooks/skill-bridge.cjs</code>를 쓰고 없으면 인라인 폴백(<code>skill-injector.mjs:20-27</code>).</li>
</ol>
<p><code>UserPromptSubmit</code>은 매 턴 <strong>새 Node 프로세스</strong>로 뜨므로 인메모리 dedup이 불가능하다. <code>skill-injector.mjs</code>는 이를 <code>{cwd}/.omc/state/skill-sessions-fallback.json</code>에 주입 이력을 파일로 영속(TTL 1시간)해 같은 스킬 재주입을 막는다(<code>skill-injector.mjs:43-58</code>, issue #2577). 이는 "호스트 훅이 stateless 프로세스 모델이라는 제약"을 플러그인이 디스크 상태로 메우는 사례다.</p>
<h3>2.3.2 commands = 스킬 디스패처 (프롬프트 비용 절감)</h3>
<p><code>commands/*.md</code>는 거의 전부 <strong>얇은 디스패처</strong>다. 호스트는 <code>plugin.json:"commands": "./commands/"</code>로 이 디렉토리를 슬래시 명령으로 자동 발견하지만, OMC는 명령 본문에 실제 로직을 넣지 않고 SKILL.md를 지연 로드하도록 지시한다. <code>commands/ccg.md</code>가 표본이다:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>This compatibility command keeps <code>/oh-my-claudecode:ccg</code> available without loading the full <code>ccg</code> skill description in every Claude Code session. ... Read the full bundled skill instructions ... <code>skills/ccg/SKILL.md</code>. Follow that SKILL.md exactly, treating the user's arguments as <code>$ARGUMENTS</code>.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>즉 명령은 호스트의 슬래시 노출용 진입점일 뿐이고 행동은 SKILL.md에 있다. 이렇게 하면 호스트가 매 세션 프롬프트에 모든 스킬 설명을 싣지 않아 <strong>컨텍스트 비용을 절약</strong>한다 — 호스트 확장점(commands 발견)을 쓰되 그 비용 특성을 의식한 최적화다.</p>
<h3>2.4 Stop 훅 = 호스트 확장점으로 만든 "턴 생존" 능력</h3>
<p>OMC의 가장 본질적 능력(ralph/autopilot/ultragoal 자율 루프)은 <strong>호스트 <code>Stop</code> 훅의 <code>decision: "block"</code></strong> 출력 하나로 구현된다. <code>scripts/persistent-mode.mjs</code>는 12곳에서 <code>decision: "block"</code>을 출력한다(<code>grep</code> 결과: 라인 1035, 1055, 1074, 1118, 1160, ...). 핵심 패턴:</p>
<pre><code class="language-js">// scripts/persistent-mode.mjs:1118
console.log(JSON.stringify({ decision: "block", reason }));
// reason 예: "[RALPH LOOP - ITERATION n/max] Work is NOT done. Continue working. ... run /oh-my-claudecode:cancel"
</code></pre>
<p>[[11_cc_plugin]]에 따르면 <code>Stop</code> 이벤트는 exit 2 또는 <code>decision: block</code>으로 "대화 계속"을 요청할 수 있는 블로킹 이벤트다. OMC는 디스크 상태(<code>.omc/state/*-state.json</code>)가 "미완"인 동안 이 확장점을 반복 트리거해 <strong>호스트가 턴을 끝내지 못하게</strong> 만든다. 안전장치도 호스트 규약 위에서 구현된다:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>컨텍스트/인증 실패는 차단하지 않음</strong>: <code>persistent-mode.mjs:749</code>의 <code>contextPatterns</code>(<code>context_limit</code>, <code>context_window</code>, <code>context_exceeded</code>, <code>max_context</code> 등)나 401/403/OAuth 만료가 감지되면 <code>{ continue: true, suppressOutput: true }</code>로 통과시켜 호스트의 재압축/재인증 흐름을 보존한다.</li>
<li><strong>하드 상한</strong>: <code>getHardMaxIterations</code>(<code>persistent-mode.mjs:52</code>)는 <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>일 때 기본 200 iteration 상한을 켠다(<code>.claude/omc.jsonc</code>의 <code>security.hardMaxIterations</code>로 오버라이드).</li>
</ul>
<p>이것이 [[00_omc_overview]] §7.1이 말한 "외부 cron 없이 호스트 라이프사이클 훅만으로 턴을 넘는 실행"의 정확한 메커니즘이다.</p>
<p><code>Stop</code> 이벤트에는 세 훅이 순서대로 걸려 협력한다(<code>hooks/hooks.json:172-192</code>):</p>
<ol>
<li><strong><code>context-guard-stop.mjs</code></strong>(timeout 5s): 영속 모드가 처리하기 <strong>전에</strong> 먼저 발화해 컨텍스트 사용률(기본 75%, <code>OMC_CONTEXT_GUARD_THRESHOLD</code>)이 임계치를 넘으면 세션 리프레시를 권한다. 출력 규약은 persistent-mode와 동일(<code>{decision:"block"}</code> 또는 <code>{continue:true,suppressOutput:true}</code>)하나, 안전장치로 (a) <code>context_limit</code> 스톱은 절대 차단 안 함(압축 데드락 방지), (b) 사용자 요청 스톱(Ctrl+C) 존중, (c) 트랜스크립트당 최대 2회 차단(<code>MAX_BLOCKS</code>, 무한 루프 가드)을 둔다(<code>context-guard-stop.mjs:14-32</code>).</li>
<li><strong><code>persistent-mode.mjs</code></strong>(timeout 10s): §2.4 본문의 자율 루프 본체.</li>
<li><strong><code>code-simplifier.mjs</code></strong>(timeout 5s): 턴 종료 시 최근 변경분 단순화 패스를 트리거.</li>
</ol>
<p>세 훅이 같은 <code>Stop</code> 이벤트에 차례로 발화하면서, <strong>context-guard가 먼저 컨텍스트 위생을 점검 → persistent-mode가 미완 작업 재진입 → code-simplifier가 마무리</strong>라는 순서를 호스트 훅 배열 순서만으로 표현한다. 이것이 [[11_cc_plugin]] §훅 실행의 "이벤트당 다중 훅을 병렬/순차 합산"을 OMC가 활용하는 방식이다.</p>
<h3>2.5 크로스플랫폼 런처 run.cjs — 훅 견고성 계층</h3>
<p>OMC는 호스트 훅 엔진과 자기 스크립트 사이에 **얇은 런처 <code>scripts/run.cjs</code>**를 끼운다. hooks.json의 모든 command가 <code>node run.cjs &#x3C;hook>.mjs</code>로 호출되는 이유는 세 가지 견고성 요구 때문이다:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Windows 호환</strong>: <code>process.execPath</code>(이미 실행 중인 Node 바이너리)로 대상 <code>.mjs</code>를 spawn해 <code>/bin/sh</code> 없이도 동작(<code>run.cjs:6-16</code>, issues #909/#899/#892/#869).</li>
<li><strong>stale <code>CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT</code> 복구</strong>: 플러그인 업데이트로 버전 디렉토리가 바뀌어 <code>MODULE_NOT_FOUND</code>가 나면, <code>resolveTarget</code>이 (a) 그대로 → (b) <code>realpathSync</code> → (c) 플러그인 캐시(<code>~/.claude/plugins/cache/omc/170_oh-my-claudecode/&#x3C;ver></code>)에서 <strong>최신 semver 버전 디렉토리를 스캔</strong>해 같은 스크립트를 찾는다(<code>run.cjs:44-91</code>, issue #1007).</li>
<li><strong>fail-open</strong>: 대상을 못 찾거나 타임아웃이면 <code>process.exit(0)</code>으로 깨끗이 빠져 <strong>호스트 훅을 절대 블로킹하지 않는다</strong>(<code>run.cjs:131-159</code>). 타임아웃 값은 hooks.json의 <code>timeout</code> 필드를 역파싱해 적용한다(<code>resolveHookTimeoutMs</code>).</li>
</ol>
<p>이 런처는 "플러그인이 호스트 확장점을 쓸 때, 버전 드리프트·플랫폼 차이에 대비한 방어 계층을 직접 둔다"는 좋은 사례다. [[11_cc_plugin]]이 호스트 측의 silent-failure(잘못된 JSON은 조용히 실패)를 지적했듯, OMC는 플러그인 측에서 fail-open 방어로 대칭을 맞춘다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. 호스트 MCP 확장점 사용 (.mcp.json + 두 서버)</h2>
<h3>3.1 매니페스트 등록 서버 — <code>t</code></h3>
<p><code>.mcp.json</code>은 stdio MCP 서버 단 하나만 직접 등록한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-jsonc">// .mcp.json
{ "mcpServers": { "t": { "command": "node", "args": ["${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bridge/mcp-server.cjs"] } } }
</code></pre>
<p>[[11_cc_plugin]]의 비교표에서 "Codex는 MCP 도구를 노출하지만 Claude Code 훅은 도구를 노출하지 않는다"고 했는데, OMC는 <strong>호스트의 MCP 확장점을 빌려 도구를 노출</strong>함으로써 훅이 못 하는 "도구 추가"를 달성한다. <code>src/mcp/tool-registry.ts</code>의 <code>allTools</code> 배열이 단일 진리원(single source of truth)으로 도구 군을 집계한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-ts">// src/mcp/tool-registry.ts:50
export const allTools: ToolDef[] = [
  ...lspTools, ...astTools, pythonReplTool,
  ...stateTools, ...notepadTools, ...memoryTools,
  ...traceTools, ...sharedMemoryTools, deepinitManifestTool,
  ...wikiTools, ...skillsTools,
];
</code></pre>
<p>등록은 호스트 MCP 프로토콜 그대로다 — <code>src/mcp/standalone-server.ts</code>가 <code>new Server(...)</code> + <code>StdioServerTransport</code>로 띄우고, <code>setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, ...)</code>로 도구 목록을 응답한다(<code>standalone-server.ts:33-112</code>). 각 <code>ToolDef</code>는 <code>name</code>/<code>description</code>/<code>schema</code>(zod)/<code>handler</code>와 함께 호스트 MCP의 <strong>annotations</strong>(<code>readOnlyHint</code>, <code>destructiveHint</code>, <code>idempotentHint</code>, <code>openWorldHint</code>)를 채운다(<code>tool-registry.ts:36</code>) — 호스트가 권한·안전 판단에 쓰는 메타데이터를 플러그인이 성실히 제공하는 사례다.</p>
<h3>3.2 매니페스트 외 서버 — <code>team</code></h3>
<p><code>team</code> MCP 서버(<code>bridge/team-mcp.cjs</code>, <code>omc_run_team_start/status/wait/cleanup</code>)는 <code>.mcp.json</code>에 없다. 런타임에서 동적으로 기동된다(<code>tool-registry.ts:11</code>의 주석: "Team runtime tools ... are intentionally excluded: they live in the separate 'team' MCP server"). 즉 OMC는 <strong>매니페스트 정적 등록(<code>t</code>)과 런타임 동적 등록(<code>team</code>)을 의도적으로 분리</strong>한다. 정적 서버는 항상 켜지는 코드 인텔/상태 도구, 동적 서버는 <code>/team</code> 사용 시에만 필요한 무거운 tmux 워커 제어로 나뉜다.</p>
<h3>3.3 서브에이전트용 in-process 서버</h3>
<p>세 번째 변형으로 <code>src/mcp/omc-tools-server.ts</code>가 있다. <code>@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk</code>의 <code>createSdkMcpServer</code>로 같은 도구군(주석: "18 custom tools (12 LSP, 2 AST, 1 python_repl, 3 skills)")을 <strong>in-process MCP 서버</strong>로 노출한다 — 서브에이전트가 별도 프로세스 spawn 없이 도구를 쓰게 하는 경로다. <code>OMC_DISABLE_TOOLS</code> 환경변수로 그룹 단위(lsp/ast/python/trace/state/notepad/memory) 비활성화도 지원한다(<code>omc-tools-server.ts:DISABLE_TOOLS_GROUP_MAP</code>).</p>
<p>이로써 OMC의 도구 표면은 <strong>(1) <code>.mcp.json</code> 정적 stdio 서버 <code>t</code>, (2) 런타임 동적 <code>team</code> 서버, (3) 서브에이전트용 in-process 서버</strong> 세 채널로 호스트 MCP 확장점에 올라탄다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. 호스트 서브에이전트 발견 확장점 (agents/*.md)</h2>
<p>OMC의 19개 에이전트는 <code>plugin.json</code>에도 <code>marketplace.json</code>에도 선언되지 않는다. 호스트가 플러그인 루트의 <code>agents/</code> 디렉토리를 <strong>자동 발견</strong>하는 규약에 전적으로 의존한다(<code>agents/</code> 19개 <code>.md</code> 파일). 각 파일은 YAML frontmatter로 호스트 서브에이전트(Task) 메커니즘에 메타데이터를 넘긴다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-yaml"># agents/architect.md
---
name: architect
description: Strategic Architecture &#x26; Debugging Advisor (Opus, READ-ONLY)
model: opus            # 호스트 모델 라우팅
level: 3               # OMC 자체 무게 표시 (호스트는 무시)
disallowedTools: Write, Edit   # 호스트 권한 박탈
---
</code></pre>
<p>여기서 플러그인 시스템 관점의 핵심은 **<code>disallowedTools</code>**다. 호스트는 이 frontmatter 키를 읽어 해당 서브에이전트의 도구 세트에서 Write/Edit를 <strong>실제로 박탈</strong>한다 — OMC가 프롬프트 텍스트(<code>&#x3C;Constraints></code>)로 "READ-ONLY"를 지시하는 것과 별개로, 호스트 권한 계층에서 한 번 더 봉인하는 "규약+권한 이중 봉인"이다(읽기 전용 에이전트: architect/analyst/critic/explore/code-reviewer/security-reviewer/document-specialist/scientist). <code>model</code>/<code>level</code>은 호스트 모델 라우팅(opus/sonnet/haiku)과 OMC 자체 무게 메타데이터로 나뉜다. 자세한 카탈로그는 [[00_omc_overview]] §3 참조.</p>
<p>이로써 OMC가 쓰는 호스트 발견 확장점은 세 갈래로 정리된다: (1) <code>plugin.json:skills</code> 명시 배열, (2) <code>plugin.json:commands</code> 디렉토리 발견, (3) <code>agents/</code> 디렉토리 무선언 발견. skills만 매니페스트에 못박고, commands/agents는 디렉토리 컨벤션에 맡기는 비대칭이 특징이다.</p>
<h2>5. 시스템 프롬프트 확장점 (CLAUDE.md 마커 주입)</h2>
<p>OMC는 호스트의 프롬프트 조립 확장점(<code>CLAUDE.md</code> 프로젝트 지시 계층)을 <strong>마커 경계 비파괴 주입</strong>으로 쓴다. <code>CLAUDE.md:1-2</code>:</p>
<pre><code>&#x3C;!-- OMC:START -->
&#x3C;!-- OMC:VERSION:4.9.1 -->
</code></pre>
<p>설치/갱신 시 이 <code>OMC:START</code>/<code>OMC:END</code> 마커 사이 블록만 교체되어 사용자 기존 <code>CLAUDE.md</code> 내용을 보존한다. 이 블록은 위임 규칙(<code>&#x3C;delegation_rules></code>)·모델 라우팅·에이전트 카탈로그·훅 규약을 담는다. 훅과 프롬프트의 접점도 여기 명시된다(<code>CLAUDE.md:99</code>):</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Hooks inject <code>&#x3C;system-reminder></code> tags. Key patterns: <code>hook success: Success</code> (proceed), <code>[MAGIC KEYWORD: ...]</code> (invoke skill), <code>The boulder never stops</code> (ralph/ultrawork active).</p>
</blockquote>
<p>즉 훅이 호스트 확장점을 통해 주입하는 텍스트와, 프롬프트가 그 텍스트를 해석하는 규약이 <code>CLAUDE.md</code>라는 또 다른 호스트 확장점에서 짝을 이룬다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. 킬 스위치와 그레이스풀 디그레이드</h2>
<p>플러그인으로서 OMC는 호스트에 부담을 주지 않도록 다층 끄기 경로를 둔다(<code>CLAUDE.md:101</code>, <code>keyword-detector.mjs:main</code>):</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>스위치</th>
<th>범위</th>
<th>동작</th>
<th>근거</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>DISABLE_OMC=1</code></td>
<td>전체</td>
<td>모든 훅이 즉시 <code>{continue:true}</code>로 무동작 통과</td>
<td><code>keyword-detector.mjs</code> main 가드</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>OMC_SKIP_HOOKS</code> (콤마구분)</td>
<td>훅별</td>
<td>지정 훅만 스킵 (<code>keyword-detector</code> 등)</td>
<td>issue #838</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>OMC_DISABLE_TOOLS</code> (그룹)</td>
<td>MCP 도구별</td>
<td>lsp/ast/python/... 그룹 비활성</td>
<td><code>omc-tools-server.ts</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>OMC_TEAM_WORKER</code></td>
<td>팀 워커 내부</td>
<td>키워드 탐지 차단(무한 스폰 방지)</td>
<td><code>keyword-detector.mjs</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code></td>
<td>영속 루프</td>
<td>하드 iteration 상한 200</td>
<td><code>persistent-mode.mjs:53</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>런처 <code>run.cjs</code>의 fail-open과 합쳐, OMC는 "호스트 확장점을 빌려 쓰되, 자기 실패가 호스트를 막지 않는다"는 좋은 플러그인 시민(good plugin citizen) 원칙을 일관되게 지킨다. [[11_cc_plugin]] §관리 정책의 <code>disableAllHooks</code>/<code>allowManagedHooksOnly</code>가 <strong>호스트 측</strong> 끄기라면, OMC의 스위치는 <strong>플러그인 자체 측</strong> 끄기로 대칭을 이룬다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>7. 호스트 확장점별 사용 강도 매핑</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    subgraph CC["Claude Code 확장점 (11_cc_plugin)"]
        E1["25 라이프사이클 이벤트"]
        E2["6 훅 타입"]
        E3["plugin manifest hooksConfig"]
        E4["MCP 서버 등록"]
        E5["skills/commands/agents 발견"]
        E6["CLAUDE.md 프롬프트 계층"]
    end
    subgraph OMC["OMC 사용 강도"]
        U1["10/25 이벤트만 사용&#x3C;br/>(핵심 골격)"]
        U2["command 1종만&#x3C;br/>(prompt/agent/http/callback 미사용)"]
        U3["hooks.json → 16 스크립트"]
        U4["t(정적)+team(동적)+in-process"]
        U5["39 skills / N commands / 19 agents"]
        U6["OMC:START 마커 비파괴 주입"]
    end
    E1 --> U1
    E2 --> U2
    E3 --> U3
    E4 --> U4
    E5 --> U5
    E6 --> U6
</code></pre>
<p>핵심 관찰: OMC는 호스트 플러그인 시스템의 <strong>넓이가 아니라 깊이</strong>를 택했다. 6가지 훅 타입 중 <code>command</code> 하나, 25개 이벤트 중 10개만 쓰되, 그 <code>command</code> 훅 안에 16개 Node 스크립트와 디스크 상태 머신을 쌓아 호스트가 의도하지 않았을 수준의 자율 오케스트레이션을 끌어낸다. <code>Stop</code> 훅 하나로 "턴 생존 루프"를 만든 것이 그 압축적 사례다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>8. 한계와 포크 관점</h2>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <strong>단일 훅 타입 집중</strong>: <code>command</code>만 써 디버깅/이식이 단순하다. 호스트 <code>prompt</code>/<code>agent</code>/<code>http</code> 훅을 안 써 LLM 호출 비용 없는 결정만 훅에서 처리한다.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <strong>fail-open 런처</strong>: <code>run.cjs</code>의 stale-path 복구와 fail-open은 플러그인 견고성의 모범. 포크 시 그대로 차용 가치.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <strong>MCP 3채널 분리</strong>(정적/동적/in-process)는 도구 표면을 무게별로 나누는 좋은 패턴.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <strong>버전 드리프트</strong>: <code>plugin.json</code>(39 skills)·<code>marketplace.json</code>("32 skills"·"28 agents")·<code>CLAUDE.md</code>(<code>OMC:VERSION:4.9.1</code>)·<code>plugin.json version</code>(4.14.4)이 서로 셈/버전이 달라 매니페스트 정합성 검증이 약하다.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <strong>호스트 훅 출력 스키마 의존</strong>: <code>hookSpecificOutput</code>/<code>decision:block</code> 규약은 [[11_cc_plugin]]이 지적한 대로 공개 문서가 부족한 호스트 내부 계약이다. 호스트 변경 시 OMC가 깨질 위험.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <strong>이름 이원성</strong>: 플러그인명(<code>oh-my-claudecode</code>)과 npm 패키지명(<code>oh-my-claude-sisyphus</code>)이 달라 추적이 혼란스럽다.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>9. cli-jaw 비교 시사점</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>확장점 소비자 vs 디스패치 호스트</strong>: OMC는 <em>단일 호스트(Claude Code)의 플러그인 확장점</em>을 깊게 파고드는 소비자다. cli-jaw는 반대로 여러 CLI를 디스패치하는 <em>메타 하니스</em>로, 자신이 곧 호스트에 가깝다. OMC가 보여주는 "호스트 확장점 깊이 우선" 전략(6개 훅 타입 중 1개만, 25개 이벤트 중 10개만 쓰되 그 안에 상태 머신을 쌓음)은, cli-jaw가 각 대상 CLI의 확장점(예: Claude Code hooks)을 <em>얇게 다 쓰기</em>보다 <em>핵심 몇 개를 깊게</em> 쓰는 설계 판단의 참고점이다.</li>
<li><strong>훅 기반 턴 생존 vs poll/Wakeup</strong>: OMC는 호스트 <code>Stop</code> 훅 <code>decision:block</code> 하나로 "절대 죽지 않는 루프"를 외부 cron 없이 얻는다. cli-jaw의 MEMORY 규약(<code>feedback_use_poll_for_waiting</code>: "대기 시 절대 종료 말고 poll/ScheduleWakeup")과 목표가 동일하다 — 차이는 OMC가 <em>호스트 라이프사이클 훅 안</em>에서, cli-jaw가 <em>하니스 스케줄러</em>에서 달성한다는 점. 멀티-CLI 디스패치 중 특정 CLI에 작업을 위임할 때, OMC식 훅 기반 자기 재진입을 그 CLI 안에 심으면 cli-jaw의 폴링 부담을 위임 대상으로 분산할 수 있다.</li>
<li><strong>fail-open 런처 = 멀티채널 견고성 모델</strong>: <code>run.cjs</code>의 stale-path 복구 + fail-open(<code>exit 0</code>)은 "확장 코드가 실패해도 호스트를 막지 않는다"는 원칙의 구현이다. cli-jaw가 여러 채널/CLI를 오케스트레이션할 때, 한 채널/CLI 어댑터 실패가 전체 디스패치를 막지 않도록 하는 fail-open 어댑터 계층의 직접적 청사진이다.</li>
<li><strong>MCP 3채널 분리 + 매니페스트 정합성 교훈</strong>: OMC의 정적(<code>.mcp.json</code>)/동적(<code>team</code>)/in-process MCP 분리는 cli-jaw가 도구를 지연 로드(<code>ToolSearch</code>)하는 것과 같은 "무게별 도구 표면 분할" 철학이다. 동시에 OMC의 매니페스트 버전 드리프트(39 vs 32 skills, 4.14.4 vs 4.9.1)는, 다채널·파일 메모리·PABCD를 가진 cli-jaw가 <em>카탈로그/매니페스트를 단일 진리원에서 생성</em>해야 한다는 반면교사다.</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<h2>변경 기록</h2>
<ul>
<li>2026-05-29: OMC v4.14.4의 플러그인 시스템 차원을 소스 기반으로 작성. 핵심 발견은 "OMC는 자체 플러그인 시스템이 없으며, Claude Code 호스트의 5개 확장점(plugin.json 매니페스트, hooks.json, MCP 등록, skills/commands/agents 발견, CLAUDE.md 프롬프트 계층)을 소비자로서 깊게 사용한다"는 것. plugin.json/marketplace.json/.mcp.json 매니페스트 표면, hooks.json의 command-훅 단일 타입과 10/25 이벤트 점유, Stop 훅 decision:block 기반 턴 생존, run.cjs fail-open 런처, MCP 3채널(정적 t / 동적 team / in-process), 킬 스위치 다층, 매니페스트 버전 드리프트 한계, cli-jaw 비교 4항목 포함.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>oh-my-claudecode (omc) guardian / approved</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis Snapshot</strong>: <code>170_oh-my-claudecode/</code> — guardian and approval.</p>
</blockquote>
<blockquote>
<p>This document analyzes the <strong>approval/guardian layer</strong> of the <code>Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode</code> (OMC) plugin based on the source. OMC does not have its own permission engine — Claude Code's rule-based 5-mode Enforce a policy to <strong>deny</strong> model routing·ultragoal·team with a hook (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code>), (3) add a 7-feature security policy table (<code>security-config.ts</code>) that is turned on by the <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> master switch. The analysis standard version is <code>plugin.json</code> v4.14.4.</p>
<p>Source paths: <code>src/hooks/permission-handler/index.ts</code>, <code>scripts/permission-handler.mjs</code>, <code>scripts/pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code>, <code>src/lib/security-config.ts</code>, <code>hooks/hooks.json</code>, <code>SECURITY.md</code>, and policy consumer <code>src/tools/ast-tools.ts</code>, <code>src/tools/python-repl/bridge-manager.ts</code>, <code>src/team/model-contract.ts</code>, <code>src/hooks/persistent-mode/index.ts</code>, <code>src/utils/ssrf-guard.ts</code>, <code>src/features/delegation-enforcer.ts</code>.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>The OMC's approval model can be summarized in one sentence: <strong>Rejection and final decision are left to Claude Code's Tengu/rule engine, and OMC only intervenes in two directions: "whitelist that automatically passes safe things" and "deny hook that proactively blocks risky/invalid calls."</strong> Neither Codex's AI risk-scoring guardian ([[12_cc_guardian]] §9) nor Claude Code's 19 Tengu checks are reimplemented. No.</p>
<hr>
<h2>1. Guardian hierarchy overall structure</h2>
<p>There are only two hooks in <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> that the OMC hooks to the host permission system — <code>PreToolUse</code> (all tools, <code>matcher:"*"</code>, timeout 3s) and <code>PermissionRequest</code> (Bash only, <code>matcher:"Bash"</code>, timeout 5s). Both launch <code>.mjs</code> through the <code>scripts/run.cjs</code> launcher.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A["도구 호출 요청"] --> B{"PreToolUse Hook&#x3C;br/>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs"}
    B -->|"ultragoal /goal 불일치"| D1["permissionDecision: deny"]
    B -->|"forceInherit + 잘못된 model"| D2["permissionDecision: deny"]
    B -->|"team 활성 + team_name 없음"| W1["additionalContext 경고(차단 아님)"]
    B -->|"fallback/workaround 언어"| W2["[SLOP WARNING] 경고"]
    B -->|"통과"| C{"도구 = Bash?"}
    C -->|"아니오"| H["Claude Code 규칙 엔진&#x3C;br/>(checkPermissions)"]
    C -->|"예"| P{"PermissionRequest Hook&#x3C;br/>permission-handler.mjs"}
    P -->|"ask 규칙 매칭"| H
    P -->|"SAFE_PATTERNS / repo-inspection /&#x3C;br/>targeted-test / safe-heredoc"| AL["behavior: allow (자동승인)"]
    P -->|"그 외"| H
    H --> T["Tengu 보안 체인 + allow/deny/ask"]
    T --> R["실행 / 사용자 프롬프트"]
</code></pre>
<p>Key asymmetry: <strong>PermissionRequest Hook only returns <code>allow</code></strong> (auto-approval or passthrough). It does not deny the command directly, and if it is not safe, it just passes it as <code>{continue:true}</code> and lets the host's native permission flow decide (<code>return { continue: true }</code> at the end of <code>processPermissionRequest</code>, <code>index.ts:672</code>). Conversely, <strong>deny only appears in PreToolUse Hook</strong> (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code>), and its target is model routing/ultragoal gating, not Bash risk.</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Hook</th>
<th>Matcher</th>
<th>script</th>
<th>Returnability Decision</th>
<th>Purpose</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>PreToolUse</code></td>
<td><code>*</code></td>
<td><code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code></td>
<td><code>deny</code> / <code>additionalContext</code> warning / <code>continue</code></td>
<td>Model routing enforcement, ultragoal·team gating, SLOP warning</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>PermissionRequest</code></td>
<td><code>Bash</code></td>
<td><code>permission-handler.mjs</code> → <code>permission-handler/index.ts</code></td>
<td><code>allow</code> / <code>continue</code> (passthrough)</td>
<td>Safety Bash command automatic approval</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<hr>
<h2>2. Bash automatic approval whitelist (<code>permission-handler/index.ts</code>)</h2>
<p>The main body of the <code>PermissionRequest</code> Hook is <code>processPermissionRequest()</code> in <code>src/hooks/permission-handler/index.ts</code> (build output <code>dist/hooks/permission-handler/index.js</code> is dynamically imported by <code>permission-handler.mjs:12</code>). The flow is <code>index.ts:640~674</code>:</p>
<ol>
<li>Normalize <code>tool_name</code> by removing <code>proxy_</code> prefix — if not <code>Bash</code>, immediately pass as <code>{continue:true}</code> (<code>index.ts:643~645</code>).</li>
<li>Use <code>hasClaudePermissionAsk(cwd, 'Bash', command)</code> to check whether the user has <strong>explicitly specified a command</strong> with ask. If the ask rule is met, automatic approval is not granted (respecting user intent, <code>index.ts:653</code>).</li>
<li>If it is not an ask and <code>isSafeAutoApprovedCommand(command, cwd)</code> is true, it is automatically approved with <code>behavior:'allow'</code> (<code>index.ts:656~670</code>).</li>
<li>Otherwise <code>{continue:true}</code> — Passthrough to host native permission flow.</li>
</ol>
<h3>2.1 4-Prong Safety Verification (<code>isSafeAutoApprovedCommand</code>)</h3>
<p><code>index.ts:544~549</code> OR's four types of safety commands:</p>
<pre><code>isSafeCommand              → SAFE_PATTERNS 화이트리스트 (메타문자 0)
isSafeRepoInspectionCommand → cat/head/tail/sed -n/rg, repo 내부 경로만
isSafeTargetedLocalTestCommand → vitest run / npm·pnpm·yarn test --run / node --test, 단일 repo 경로
isHeredocWithSafeBase      → git commit/git tag heredoc
</code></pre>
<p><strong>(a) SAFE_PATTERNS (<code>index.ts:33~45</code>)</strong> — Regular expression whitelist of read-only/build/lint commands:</p>
<pre><code>^git (status|diff|log|branch|show|fetch)
^npm run (lint|build|check|typecheck)
^pnpm|yarn (lint|build|check|typecheck|run …)
^tsc / ^eslint / ^prettier / ^cargo (check|clippy|build) / ^ls / ^gh (issue|pr) (view|list|status)
</code></pre>
<p>As the comment states, <code>cat/head/tail</code> has been <strong>intentionally removed</strong> from SAFE_PATTERNS ("they allow reading arbitrary files", <code>index.ts:44</code>) — separated into a separate path validation path (2.2) to prevent exposure of arbitrary files.</p>
<p><strong>(b) DANGEROUS_SHELL_CHARS guard (<code>index.ts:51</code>)</strong> — <code>isSafeCommand</code>(<code>index.ts:554~564</code>) rejects shell metacharacters <strong>before</strong> matching SAFE_PATTERN:</p>
<pre><code>/[;&#x26;|`$()&#x3C;>\n\r\t\0\\{}\[\]*?~!#]/
</code></pre>
<p>This single line blocks all command chaining, substitution, redirection, globbing, and home expansion. <code>git status; rm -rf /</code> is rejected because of <code>;</code> (validated by test <code>index.test.ts:184</code>), and quotes (<code>"'</code>) are <strong>intentionally excluded</strong> for paths containing spaces (substitution is already taken by <code>$</code>, <code>index.ts:49</code>). Based on GitHub Issue #146.</p>
<h3>2.2 Repo path verification (<code>isSafeRepoInspectionCommand</code>)</h3>
<p><code>cat/head/tail/sed -n/rg</code> is omitted from the whitelist, but is automatically approved when <strong>verifying that the target being read is a non-sensitive file inside the worktree</strong> (<code>index.ts:511~526</code>). The key is <code>isSafeRepoPath</code>(<code>index.ts:338~391</code>):</p>
<ul>
<li>Get the worktree boundary with <code>getWorktreeRoot(cwd)</code>, resolve the symbolic link to a <strong>canonical path</strong> with <code>fs.realpathSync</code>, and then reject anything outside the worktree (<code>..</code> or absolute path) — Symlink escape defense.</li>
<li>Block <code>.git/</code>, <code>.ssh/</code>, <code>secrets/</code>, <code>.env*</code>, <code>node_modules/.cache/</code> with <code>isSensitiveRepoRelativePath</code> (<code>index.ts:312~336</code>). <code>cat .env.local</code>·<code>rg -n value .git</code> is rejected (test <code>index.test.ts:211~216</code>).</li>
<li>Prohibit full directory search (<code>allowDirectory:false</code>) — If a directory is given, such as <code>rg -n value src</code>, it will be rejected, and only a single file will be allowed.</li>
<li><code>rg</code> only allows <code>SAFE_RIPGREP_FLAGS</code> (<code>-n/-S/-F/-i/--no-heading</code>, etc., <code>index.ts:66~76</code>) — <code>--hidden</code> is rejected.</li>
</ul>
<p>The command tokenizer (<code>tokenizeShellCommand</code>, <code>index.ts:270~310</code>) recognizes quotes and decomposes arguments, but rejects unclosed quotes by returning <code>null</code>.</p>
<h3>2.3 heredoc guard (<code>isHeredocWithSafeBase</code>, Issue #608)</h3>
<p>Heredoc commands containing metacharacters such as <code>&#x3C;&#x3C;</code>, newlines, and <code>$</code> are unconditionally rejected by <code>isSafeCommand</code>. Then, when the user presses "Always allow" in the native permission flow, a corruption occurs in which <strong>the entire heredoc body (several hundred lines) is stored in <code>settings.local.json</code>.</strong> To prevent this, <code>index.ts:577~595</code> is:</p>
<ol>
<li>Check multiline + <code>HEREDOC_PATTERN</code>(<code>&#x3C;&#x3C;[-~]?\s*['"]?\w+['"]?</code>, <code>index.ts:54</code>) matching.</li>
<li>Extract the <strong>first line</strong> (excluding the heredoc body) using the base command.</li>
<li>If the first line is <code>SAFE_HEREDOC_PATTERNS</code>(<code>^git commit</code>, <code>^git tag</code>, <code>index.ts:61~64</code>), automatic approval → <code>settings.local.json</code> passes without contamination.</li>
</ol>
<h3>2.4 Target test automatic approval</h3>
<p><code>isSafeTargetedLocalTestCommand</code> (<code>index.ts:528~542</code>) only allows <code>vitest run &#x3C;file></code>, <code>npm/pnpm/yarn test -- --run &#x3C;file></code>, and <code>node --test &#x3C;file></code> with <strong>a single repo path argument</strong> (<code>index.ts:464~509</code>). Immediately reject any metacharacters. Persistent mode (ralph/autopilot) is a practical whitelist that prevents permission prompts from appearing every time you run a test repeatedly.</p>
<h3>2.5 Background fallback (<code>getBackgroundBashPermissionFallback</code>)</h3>
<p>When <code>BACKGROUND_MUTATION_SUBAGENTS</code>(executor/designer/writer/debugger/git-master/test-engineer/qa-tester/document-specialist, <code>index.ts:78~87</code>) runs in the background, if there is no Edit/Write/Bash authorization, it triggers a fallback (<code>index.ts:230~268</code>). In the case of Bash, if it is a ask rule matching, it is judged as a fallback, and if it is a safety automatic approval command, it is judged as not requiring a fallback. Fallback is a fail-safe that prevents the background subagent from being stuck waiting indefinitely because there is no one to respond to the permission prompt — if there is no permission, it will rather drop to the foreground and allow the user to respond.</p>
<h3>2.6 Test sealing of auto-approval contracts</h3>
<p>Auto-approval whitelists are very sensitive to regressions (even one line being loose can lead to arbitrary command execution), so <code>src/hooks/permission-handler/__tests__/index.test.ts</code> anchors the contract. Safe cases (<code>git status</code>, <code>npm run build</code>, <code>tsc --noEmit</code>, <code>gh pr list --state open</code>, quoted path <code>ls "my folder"</code>, etc.) and rejection cases are listed in a table. The deny case (<code>index.test.ts:60~120</code>) exposes the attack surface that the OMC is trying to block:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Attack Type</th>
<th>Example</th>
<th>Grounds for blocking</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Semicolon chaining</td>
<td><code>git status; rm -rf /</code></td>
<td><code>;</code> ∈ DANGEROUS_SHELL_CHARS</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>pipe chaining</td>
<td><code>git status | sh</code></td>
<td><code>|</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>AND/OR chaining</td>
<td><code>git status &#x26;&#x26; rm -rf /</code></td>
<td><code>&#x26;</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>command substitution</td>
<td><code>git status $(whoami)</code>, <code>`whoami`</code></td>
<td><code>$</code>, <code>()</code>, <code>`</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>redirection</td>
<td><code>git status > /etc/passwd</code>, <code>&#x3C; /etc/shadow</code></td>
<td><code>&#x3C;</code>, <code>></code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>subshell</td>
<td><code>(git status)</code>, <code>git status()</code></td>
<td><code>()</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Newline/tab/CR injection</td>
<td><code>git status\nrm -rf /</code>, <code>\t</code>, <code>\r\n</code></td>
<td><code>\n\r\t</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>brace/bracket extension</td>
<td><code>echo {a,b}</code>, <code>ls [a-z]*</code></td>
<td><code>{}</code>, <code>[]</code>, <code>*</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>This table is essentially the "anti-pattern specification" of OMC's auto-approval — it has the same purpose as Tengu's 19 checks ([[12_cc_guardian]] §5), but OMC took the conservative approach of rejecting them all with just one regular expression (<code>DANGEROUS_SHELL_CHARS</code>).</p>
<h3>2.7 fail-open launcher and timeout</h3>
<p>Both hooks go through <code>scripts/run.cjs</code> (crossplatform launcher). The design is consistently <strong>fail-open</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>If there is no target script, <code>run.cjs</code> terminates cleanly with <code>process.exit(0)</code>, so the Hook never blocks the tool. The plugin version directory has been replaced/deleted with a symlink, so even if <code>CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT</code> is stale, the cache is scanned to find the latest version script (issue #1007).</li>
<li><code>permission-handler.mjs</code> passes as <code>{continue:true, suppressOutput:true}</code> when import/parse fails (<code>permission-handler.mjs:15~18</code>).</li>
<li>In <code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code>, the top-level try/catch absorbs all exceptions as <code>{continue:true}</code> (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs:1262~1265</code>).</li>
<li>The timeout in hooks.json is 3s/5s, respectively — if exceeded, the tool proceeds without Hook results.</li>
</ul>
<p>In other words, <strong>if the OMC guardian dies, the host's native permission flow continues</strong> (safety is guaranteed by the host even in the absence of the guardian). However, since it is fail-open, it is clear that OMC's automatic approval/deny is an “additional safety net” and not the “only line of defense.”</p>
<hr>
<h2>3. PreToolUse deny rule (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code>)</h2>
<p><code>scripts/pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code> (about 1,269 lines) is mostly a <strong>context reminder injector</strong> despite its name, but it has three actual <strong>deny gates</strong>. All output <code>permissionDecision:"deny"</code>. If there is <code>pre-tool-use</code> in <code>DISABLE_OMC=1</code> or <code>OMC_SKIP_HOOKS</code> at the beginning, it passes unconditionally as <code>{continue:true}</code> (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs:1030~1035</code>).</p>
<h3>3.1 ultragoal /goal gate (deny)</h3>
<p><code>evaluateUltragoalPreToolEnforcement</code>(<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs:591~617</code>): If ultragoal mode is active and Claude's native <code>/goal</code> snapshot <strong>mismatches</strong> the ultragoal objective, deny all tools (<code>:1078~1089</code>). Safety device:</p>
<ul>
<li>The bootstrap tool (<code>omc ultragoal create/complete/next</code> or <code>Skill:ultragoal</code>) is an exception (<code>isUltragoalBootstrapTool</code>, <code>:584~589</code>).</li>
<li>Stale status (exceeding 2 hours, <code>STATE_STALE_MS</code>, <code>:469</code>), other projects, and terminal status (<code>:500~517</code>) are not denied.</li>
<li>Can be bypassed explicitly with <code>ALLOW_ULTRAGOAL_WITHOUT_GOAL=1</code> (<code>:592</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p>The purpose is a <strong>goal consistency gate</strong> that prevents the model from being tooled with the wrong goal in persistent goal mode.</p>
<h3>3.2 Model routing deny (forceInherit)</h3>
<p>Verify the <code>model</code> parameter of <code>Task</code>/<code>Agent</code> calls in Bedrock/Vertex/proxy environments (<code>isForceInheritEnabled</code>, <code>:891~895</code>) (<code>:1100~1193</code>, issues #1135/#1201/#1767/#1868):</p>
<ul>
<li>If <code>model</code> is a tier alias (sonnet/opus/haiku) and can be resolved with a safe model, pass; If it cannot be resolved or the suffix <code>[1m]</code> is added, <strong>deny</strong>(<code>:1124~1139</code>).</li>
<li>If the session model without <code>model</code> has the <code>[1m]</code> suffix, the subagent inherits it and receives 400 from Bedrock, so it is denied and instructed to use a tier alias (<code>:1141~1161</code>).</li>
<li>When there is only <code>subagent_type</code> without <code>model</code>, if the agent definition (<code>model:</code> in <code>agents/&#x3C;name>.md</code> frontmatter) is a Bedrock non-compliant ID, deny — but only if a safe routing target exists (avoiding infinite retry loop, <code>:1167~1189</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p>Rather than security, this is a guardian that prevents runtime failures due to incorrect routing. Additionally, <code>src/features/delegation-enforcer.ts</code> injects/removes model parameters in Task/Agent calls.</p>
<h3>3.3 team routing warning (not deny)</h3>
<p>If a team is active and <code>Task</code> is called without <code>team_name</code>, <code>[TEAM ROUTING REQUIRED]</code> <code>additionalContext</code> is injected (<code>generateAgentSpawnMessage</code>, <code>:776~785</code>, issue #1006). This is <strong>not a block, but a strong warning</strong> — it encourages users to add <code>team_name</code> after TeamCreate, but does not prevent execution.</p>
<h3>3.4 SLOP warning (not deny)</h3>
<p><code>generateSlopWarning</code>(<code>:286~303</code>): If fallback/workaround language is seen in the input of a risky tool such as Task/Bash/Edit/Write, <code>[SLOP WARNING]</code> is injected. Look at behavioral intent patterns (<code>SLOP_FALLBACK_ACTION_PATTERNS</code>) rather than simple keywords, and exclude document context (<code>.md</code>), self-references (enforcer itself), and technical benign expressions (<code>fallback value</code>, <code>sqlite workaround</code>) (<code>:185~284</code>). It is a quality guardian that suppresses the mass production of makeshift codes.</p>
<hr>
<h2>4. <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> security policy table (<code>security-config.ts</code>)</h2>
<p><code>src/lib/security-config.ts</code> is the <strong>single entry point</strong> for OMC Security. It consists of two layers: the <code>OMC_SECURITY</code> environment variable (master switch) and the <code>security</code> section of the config file (<code>.claude/omc.jsonc</code> or <code>~/.config/claude-omc/config.jsonc</code>). The priority is <strong>config file > OMC_SECURITY env > defaults</strong>(<code>security-config.ts:14</code>).</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Features</th>
<th>interface key</th>
<th align="center">default</th>
<th align="center">strict</th>
<th>force branch</th>
<th>effect</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>AST path restrictions</td>
<td><code>restrictToolPaths</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td><code>ast-tools.ts:63~76</code></td>
<td>Restrict <code>ast_grep_search/replace</code> to the worktree root, otherwise <code>throw</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>python sandbox</td>
<td><code>pythonSandbox</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td><code>python-repl/bridge-manager.ts:394</code></td>
<td>Inject <code>OMC_PYTHON_SANDBOX=1</code> → os/subprocess/socket… + block exec/eval/open</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Project skill inactive</td>
<td><code>disableProjectSkills</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td>skill loader</td>
<td>Block <code>.omc/skills/</code> loading (prevent injection of untrusted learning skills)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Disable automatic updates</td>
<td><code>disableAutoUpdate</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td><code>features/auto-update.ts</code></td>
<td><code>isSilentAutoUpdateEnabled()</code> forces false → blocks unverified npm installation</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>persistent loop hard upper limit</td>
<td><code>hardMaxIterations</code></td>
<td align="center">500</td>
<td align="center"><strong>200</strong></td>
<td><code>persistent-mode/index.ts:1166,1770</code></td>
<td>Auto disable mode on reach (<code>[RALPH/ULTRAWORK - HARD LIMIT]</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>remote MCP inactive</td>
<td><code>disableRemoteMcp</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td><code>index.ts:306~307</code>(Exa/Context7 enable)</td>
<td>Exa (web search)·Context7 (external document) MCP server not started</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>external LLM inactive</td>
<td><code>disableExternalLLM</code></td>
<td align="center">false</td>
<td align="center"><strong>true</strong></td>
<td><code>team/model-contract.ts:267~272</code></td>
<td>Codex/Gemini CLI blocked on team workers, only Claude allowed</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The basis is <code>DEFAULTS</code> (<code>security-config.ts:39~47</code>) and <code>STRICT_OVERRIDES</code> (<code>:49~57</code>).</p>
<h3>4.1 Unidirectionality of strict: config can only tighten</h3>
<p>The core design of <code>getSecurityConfig()</code>(<code>:92~123</code>): <strong>In strict mode, the config file cannot be unsecured, it can only be tightened.</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>The boolean flag is <code>base.X || (fileOverrides.X ?? false)</code> — true can never be lowered to false (<code>:102~107</code>).</li>
<li><code>hardMaxIterations</code> is <code>Math.min(base, fileOverride)</code> — the upper bound is only reduced (<code>:108</code>). You cannot increase strict's 200 to 500 with config.</li>
</ul>
<p>Only in non-strict mode does the config freely override default (<code>:111~119</code>). The resulting config is cached with <code>cachedConfig</code> (<code>:60</code>, <code>:93</code>).</p>
<h3>4.2 Force branch code verification</h3>
<pre><code class="language-ts">// ast-tools.ts:63~76 — 경로 탈출 시 즉시 throw
if (!isToolPathRestricted()) return;          // 비활성이면 패스
const projectRoot = getWorktreeRoot() || process.cwd();
if (!normalize(resolved).startsWith(normalizedRoot))
  throw new Error(`Path restricted: '${inputPath}' is outside the project root …`);

// team/model-contract.ts:267~272 — getContract() 레벨에서 거부
if (agentType !== 'claude' &#x26;&#x26; isExternalLLMDisabled())
  throw new Error(`External LLM provider "${agentType}" is blocked by security policy (disableExternalLLM). …`);

// persistent-mode/index.ts:1166~1180 — 하드 상한 도달 시 모드 자동 비활성
const hardMax = getHardMaxIterations();
if (hardMax > 0 &#x26;&#x26; state.iteration >= hardMax) { /* disable + [RALPH - HARD LIMIT] */ }
</code></pre>
<p>The python sandbox injects the env flag (<code>OMC_PYTHON_SANDBOX=1</code>) when spawning a child process (<code>bridge-manager.ts:394</code>), so the actual blocking occurs on the Python bridge (<code>gyoshu_bridge.py</code>) side. List of blocks specified by <code>SECURITY.md</code>:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Blocking modules</strong>: <code>os</code>, <code>subprocess</code>, <code>shutil</code>, <code>socket</code>, <code>ctypes</code>, <code>multiprocessing</code>, <code>webbrowser</code>, <code>http.server</code>, <code>xmlrpc.server</code>, <code>importlib</code>, <code>sys</code>, <code>io</code>, <code>pathlib</code>, <code>signal</code></li>
<li><strong>Block builtin</strong>: <code>exec</code>, <code>eval</code>, <code>compile</code>, <code>__import__</code>, <code>open</code>, <code>breakpoint</code></li>
</ul>
<p><code>sys</code>/<code>io</code>/<code>pathlib</code> are intentionally blocked (defense-in-depth trade-off), even if it limits normal REPL use to some extent. As <code>SECURITY.md</code> specifies, this blocklist is <strong>defense-in-depth</strong>, not a standalone security boundary**, and OS-level isolation is recommended for untrusted code.</p>
<h3>4.4 Summary of actions by force point</h3>
<p>To break down where and how each of the seven functions are enforced:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Reject with throw (hard fail)</strong>: <code>restrictToolPaths</code> (AST path escape → <code>ast-tools.ts:74</code> throw), <code>disableExternalLLM</code> (<code>model-contract.ts:268</code> getContract throw). The call aborts with an exception.</li>
<li><strong>Delegate to env injection</strong>: <code>pythonSandbox</code> (<code>OMC_PYTHON_SANDBOX=1</code> in child process). Blocking is done at the lower runtime.</li>
<li><strong>Force settings override</strong>: <code>disableAutoUpdate</code> (force <code>isSilentAutoUpdateEnabled()</code> to false), <code>disableProjectSkills</code> (skip loading <code>.omc/skills/</code> with <code>isProjectSkillsDisabled()</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Server not running</strong>: <code>disableRemoteMcp</code> (Turn off Exa/Context7 enable flag in <code>src/index.ts:306~307</code> → Server itself does not start).</li>
<li><strong>Auto disable after upper limit</strong>: <code>hardMaxIterations</code> (mode disable + <code>[HARD LIMIT]</code> message when iteration·reinforcement_count reaches upper limit in <code>persistent-mode/index.ts:1166,1770</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p>Why <code>disableProjectSkills</code> is a security feature: OMC's <code>skill-injector.mjs</code> (UserPromptSubmit Hook, [[00_omc_overview]] §4.4) automatically injects learning skills from project <code>.omc/skills/</code> into the context, and cloning an untrusted repo can inject malicious prompts. strict mode closes this channel.</p>
<h3>4.5 Secondary Guardian: SSRF guard</h3>
<p><code>src/utils/ssrf-guard.ts</code> verifies URLs and blocks internal network requests (<code>BLOCKED_HOST_PATTERNS</code>):</p>
<ul>
<li>Private IP: <code>10.x</code>, <code>172.(16-31).x</code>, <code>192.168.x</code>, link-local <code>169.254.x</code></li>
<li>loopback: <code>127.x</code>, <code>localhost</code>, IPv6 <code>::1</code></li>
<li>IPv6 unique-local <code>fc00:</code>, link-local <code>fe80:</code>, <strong>IPv6-mapped IPv4</strong> <code>::ffff:</code> (all private ranges are accessible with this prefix, so explicitly block)</li>
<li>multicast/reserved <code>224-239.x</code>, <code>0.x</code></li>
</ul>
<p><code>ALLOWED_SCHEMES</code> only accepts <code>http(s)</code> — rejects <code>file:</code>/<code>gopher:</code> etc. It <strong>always</strong> operates independently of the strict policy table above, and is the SSRF defense line for web fetch and external context tools.</p>
<h3>4.6 team worker permissions (advisory only)</h3>
<p><code>src/team/permissions.ts</code> provides per-worker RBAC scoping (<code>WorkerPermissions</code>: <code>allowedPaths</code>/<code>deniedPaths</code>/<code>allowedCommands</code>/<code>maxFileSize</code>), but the file header clearly warns — <strong>"advisory layer only. MCP workers run in full-auto mode and cannot be mechanically restricted. Permissions are injected into prompts as instructions for the LLM. to follow."</strong> In other words, the tmux CLI worker (codex/gemini) cannot be blocked mechanically, and permissions are only injected as instructions at the prompt. Glob matching is implemented using character-by-character repetition instead of regular expressions to avoid ReDoS (<code>matchGlob</code>, <code>permissions.ts:27</code>). This is the specific basis for the "lack of security boundaries between agents" limitation of <code>SECURITY.md</code> — worker isolation is just a git worktree (to prevent file conflicts), not a security sandbox.</p>
<h3>4.7 Host rules read by OMC</h3>
<p>The OMC does not have its own rule storage, but **reads the host's <code>settings.local.json</code>/<code>settings.json</code> and reflects it in the automatic approval decision (<code>index.ts:107~223</code>):</p>
<ul>
<li><code>getClaudePermissionAllowEntries</code>/<code>getClaudePermissionAskEntries</code> collects allow/ask entries in the order of project <code>.claude/settings.local.json</code> → global <code>settings.local.json</code> → global <code>settings.json</code> (<code>index.ts:110~114</code>).</li>
<li><code>Bash</code> entries support exact matching or <code>Bash(pattern)</code> format, and the <code>*</code> wildcard is interpreted as a prefix match, but matches only when the next character is a space/delimiter to prevent partial word mismatching (<code>commandMatchesPermissionPattern</code>, <code>index.ts:173~194</code>).</li>
<li><strong>Key</strong>: The OMC does not automatically approve commands specified by the user as ask (check <code>shouldAskBashPermission</code> in <code>processPermissionRequest</code>, <code>index.ts:653</code>). User intent takes precedence over OMC whitelist.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>5. Host delegation vs OMC itself — borderline</h2>
<p>What does OMC delegate <strong>and what it adds</strong> compared to the Claude Code permission system analyzed by [[12_cc_guardian]]:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Item</th>
<th>Claude Code (host, [[12_cc_guardian]])</th>
<th>Delegated/Added by OMC</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Privileged Mode</td>
<td>5-mode(default/plan/acceptEdits/dontAsk/auto)</td>
<td><strong>Delegation</strong> — OMC does not create mods</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3-tier judgment</td>
<td>allow/deny/ask + passthrough</td>
<td><strong>Delegation</strong> — Final decision is made by host <code>checkPermissions</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Bash risk check</td>
<td>Tengu 19 Security Chain</td>
<td><strong>Delegate</strong> — OMC passes through critical commands without denying</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>rule source</td>
<td>user/project/local/session/enterprise</td>
<td><strong>Read Only</strong> — See allow/ask in <code>settings.local.json</code> (<code>index.ts:107~171</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Bash automatic approval</td>
<td>User rule·classifier</td>
<td><strong>Add</strong> — SAFE_PATTERNS + repo-inspection + targeted-test whitelist safe command immediately allow</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tool Dictionary Blocking</td>
<td>PreToolUse hook(decision)</td>
<td><strong>ADD</strong> — deny/warn model routing·ultragoal consistency·SLOP</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Policy Master Switch</td>
<td>(none, rule accumulation)</td>
<td><strong>Add</strong> — <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code> 7-feature bulk policy</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>AST/python/MCP boundary</td>
<td>(no host tools)</td>
<td><strong>ADD</strong> — Route restrictions/sandboxes/remote blocking in OMC's own tools</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>persistent loop upper limit</td>
<td>(no persistent loop)</td>
<td><strong>Added</strong> — Prevent runaway with <code>hardMaxIterations</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>Bottom line: <strong>OMC almost delegates “deny authority” to the host.</strong> For Bash dangerous commands, OMC does not deny them (if they are not on the auto-approval whitelist, they just pass through), and leaves the determination of danger to Tengu. The only things that the OMC directly denies are (1) the consistency of the persistence mode it introduces (ultragoal), (2) its own routing mechanism failure prevention (forceInherit model), and (3) its own tool surface (AST/python/external LLM). In other words, <strong>OMC Guardian is only responsible for “OMC-specific risks that the host is not aware of.”</strong></p>
<h3>5.1 Directional symmetry: Tengu rejects, OMC accepts</h3>
<p>Host (Tengu) and OMC Guardian handle the same Bash commands in opposite directions:</p>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>perspective</th>
<th>Tengu (host, [[12_cc_guardian]] §5)</th>
<th>OMC PermissionRequest Hook</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Basic admission</td>
<td>“Ask until safety is proven”</td>
<td>“Allow if there is no danger signal”</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>output</td>
<td>allow→passthrough, violation→ask</td>
<td>allow or passthrough (no deny)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Inspection method</td>
<td>19 segmentation checks (including AST parsing)</td>
<td>Single metacharacter regular expression + whitelist</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Purpose</td>
<td>prompt injection·hidden command defense</td>
<td>Reduce permission prompt fatigue (remove automation friction)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>In case of failure</td>
<td>fail-closed (fallback to ask)</td>
<td>fail-open (passthrough to host)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<p>The two layers do not conflict — if the OMC says allow, the host respects it, and if the OMC is silent (passthrough), Tengu makes his decision. In other words, <strong>OMC's role is to reduce automation friction by raising safety commands that Tengu would otherwise drop to "ask" to "allow" in advance</strong>, and blocking actual risks is still Tengu's responsibility. This division of labor is the key to preventing the permission prompt from appearing every time for hundreds of read and test commands in persistent mode (ralph/autopilot), while allowing the host to catch dangerous commands.</p>
<p>The operation guide for <code>SECURITY.md</code> also reflects this delegation philosophy — "Do not use <code>{"*":"allow"}</code> in Claude Code settings and prefer <code>ask</code> mode," "Avoid <code>hook.command</code> as it is executed with <code>shell:true</code>," and "MCP processes inherit env, so minimize sensitive variables," emphasizing host-side permission hygiene. Known limitations include <strong>absence of security boundaries between agents</strong> (file system/MCP sharing) and <strong>absence of OS level sandbox</strong> (<code>SECURITY.md</code> Known Limitations).</p>
<hr>
<h2>6. End-to-end trace: when ralph runs <code>cat src/foo.ts</code></h2>
<ol>
<li>ralph loop calls <code>Bash(cat src/foo.ts)</code> → enters <code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code> of <code>PreToolUse</code>.</li>
<li><code>DISABLE_OMC</code>/<code>OMC_SKIP_HOOKS</code> not set → ultragoal gate disabled (ralph mode) → Bash is not subject to model routing deny → No SLOP language → Only attach <code>additionalContext</code> reminder and <code>{continue:true}</code>(<code>:1255~1261</code>).</li>
<li>Since it is a Bash tool, enter <code>permission-handler.mjs</code> → <code>processPermissionRequest</code> of <code>PermissionRequest</code>.</li>
<li><code>hasClaudePermissionAsk('Bash','cat src/foo.ts')</code> = false → <code>isSafeAutoApprovedCommand</code> evaluation.</li>
<li><code>cat</code> is not in SAFE_PATTERNS, but <code>isSafeRepoInspectionCommand</code> evaluates: no metacharacters → tokenized → <code>src/foo.ts</code> inside worktree, non-sensitive, single file → <strong>true</strong>.</li>
<li><code>behavior:'allow'</code>, reason <code>"Safe read-only or test command"</code> Returns → Executes immediately without user prompt.</li>
<li>What if the command was <code>cat .env</code>? <code>isSensitiveRepoRelativePath</code> rejects <code>.env</code> → <code>isSafeAutoApprovedCommand</code> false → <code>{continue:true}</code> passes through → <strong>Claude Code native permission flow + Tengu</strong> decides (OMC is not involved).</li>
</ol>
<p>This flow shows the essence of the OMC guardian — it passes safe things quickly and passes ambiguous things to the host.</p>
<h3>6.1 Second example: Invalid Task model denied in forceInherit environment</h3>
<p>When calling <code>Task(subagent_type="executor", model="claude-sonnet-4-6")</code> in a Bedrock environment (<code>isForceInheritEnabled</code>=true):</p>
<ol>
<li>Enter <code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs</code> of <code>PreToolUse</code> → toolName=<code>Task</code> → forceInherit branch (<code>:1100</code>).</li>
<li><code>model="claude-sonnet-4-6"</code> is not a tier alias + <code>isSubagentSafeModelId</code> failed (not provider-specific format) → <strong>deny</strong>(<code>:1124~1139</code>).</li>
<li>Instructions for "Use Set ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=… or model='sonnet' tier alias" with <code>[MODEL ROUTING]</code> in <code>permissionDecisionReason</code>.</li>
<li>Retry the model as instructed → This time, <code>model="sonnet"</code> tier alias → <code>resolveTierAliasToSafeModel</code> is resolved to the safe model → Pass (<code>:1122</code>).</li>
</ol>
<p>This deny is not security, but <strong>preemptive blocking of runtime 400 errors</strong>, but the guardian pattern, which “blocks incorrect calls before tool execution and suggests a correction path,” is the same.</p>
<h3>6.2 Guardian mode recognition: <code>isActiveModeRunning</code></h3>
<p><code>isActiveModeRunning</code> in <code>index.ts:600~635</code> reads autopilot/ralph/ultrawork/team/omc-teams <code>-state.json</code> in <code>.omc/state/</code> to check whether <code>active===true</code> or <code>status===running|active</code>. This is a helper for mode-awarely adjusting the permission policy while persistent mode is running, and is the basis for the automatic approval whitelist (target test in 2.4, etc.) to prevent an overload of permission prompts during repeated execution of the persistent loop. <code>hasActiveMode</code> (<code>pre-tool-enforcer.mjs:640~653</code>) on the PreToolUse side performs the same mode recognition by looking at the session-scope state.</p>
<hr>
<h2>cli-jaw comparison implications</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Auto-approval whitelist vs. tool schema lazy loading</strong>: OMC defines "Safe Bash" with a single regular expression <code>SAFE_PATTERNS</code> + <code>DANGEROUS_SHELL_CHARS</code>, invalidating the whitelist if any metacharacters are present. When cli-jaw wants to reduce permission prompts (e.g. creating an allowlist for the <code>fewer-permission-prompts</code> skill), OMC's "metacharacter 0 + prefix regex + repo-path verification (realpath symlink resolution/sensitive path blacklist)" triple condition is a proven recipe for <strong>safely narrowing down the automatic approval of read commands</strong>. In particular, the decision to remove <code>cat/head/tail</code> from the whitelist and separate it into a path verification path (to prevent arbitrary file exposure) can be borrowed as is.</li>
<li><strong>deny delegation boundary of responsibility</strong>: OMC never reimplements “what the host already checks (Bash risks)” and only denies “risks inherent to the mechanisms it introduces (persistent mode consistency, routing failures, own tool boundaries).” When cli-jaw designs its own guardians, a clear separation of cli-jaw's inherent risks from the areas the host/harness is already responsible for avoids redundant checks and conflicts.</li>
<li><strong>Single master switch + one-way tightening</strong>: 7 functions are activated at once with <code>OMC_SECURITY=strict</code>, and the config file is boolean <code>||</code>·<code>Math.min</code>, so the design allows <strong>only tightening, not loosening</strong>, making it ideal for enterprise deployment. By introducing the “User override in strict profile is one-way (tighten-only)” rule in the settings.json policy of cli-jaw, bypassing privilege relaxation can be structurally blocked.</li>
<li><strong>Duplicate runaway safeguards</strong>: OMC double-protects persistent loops with a <code>/cancel</code> prompt (soft exit) in the reason string and <code>hardMaxIterations</code> (hard upper limit, strict=200). Even in cli-jaw's <code>/loop</code>·poll·ScheduleWakeup-based infinite waiting design, the triple safety device of "explicit termination notification + counter hard cap + automatic disabling when reached" is essential — OMC's <code>[HARD LIMIT]</code> automatic disabling pattern can be directly referenced.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
